# Run secure Location service
docker run -d -p 8003:8003 --name location-secure anihpj/location-service:secure

# Test geographic API with secure build
curl "http://localhost:8003/api/locations/geocode/?address=San Francisco, CA"
{ "success": true, "location": { "id": 7, "name": "San Francisco", "formatted_address": "San Francisco, CA, USA", "latitude": 37.7749295, "longitude": -122.4194155, "country": "United States", "state": "California", "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles", "accuracy": "ROOFTOP" }, "build_security": "secrets_mounted", "api_configured": true, "geocoded_at": "2026-01-11T14:05:00Z" }

Location Model Secure Build Operations:

# Test Location model with secure build features
docker exec -it location-secure python manage.py shell

from jobpost.models import Location, JobPost, Author, Skills, User
from django.contrib.gis.geos import Point
from django.contrib.gis.measure import Distance
import os

# Location model with secure API integration
print("Secure Location Service Build")
print(f"API Keys Configured: {bool(os.getenv('GEO_API_CONFIGURED'))}")

# Geographic queries with secure build
sf_point = Point(-122.4194, 37.7749, srid=4326)
nearby_locations = Location.objects.filter(
    point__distance_lte=(sf_point, Distance(mi=30))
).order_by('name')

print("Nearby Locations (Secure Build):")
for location in nearby_locations:
    distance = sf_point.distance(location.point) * 100  # Convert to km
    print(f"  {location.name}: {distance:.1f}km")
    
# Location-based job distribution
location_jobs = {}
for location in nearby_locations:
    job_count = JobPost.objects.filter(location=location).count()
    location_jobs[location.name] = job_count
    
print("Job Distribution:", location_jobs)
Secure Location Service Build API Keys Configured: True Nearby Locations (Secure Build): Berkeley: 19.2km Oakland: 13.8km Palo Alto: 47.3km San Francisco: 0.0km San Jose: 67.8km Job Distribution: {'Berkeley': 89, 'Oakland': 156, 'Palo Alto': 78, 'San Francisco': 245, 'San Jose': 189} Location QuerySet: , , , , ]>
Example 5: JobPost Processing with BuildKit Experimental Features

Build JobPost processing service using experimental BuildKit features including inline cache, advanced networking, and performance optimization for high-throughput job processing workflows.

# Enable BuildKit experimental features
export DOCKER_CLI_EXPERIMENTAL=enabled
export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1

# Create experimental Dockerfile for JobPost processing
cat > Dockerfile.jobpost-experimental << 'EOF'
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.6-labs
FROM python:3.11-slim as base

# Use experimental cache inline feature
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt,sharing=shared \
    --mount=type=cache,target=/var/lib/apt,sharing=shared \
    apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    libpq-dev build-essential && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

FROM base as dependencies
WORKDIR /app

# Advanced cache mounting for pip
COPY requirements-jobpost.txt requirements-celery.txt ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip,sharing=shared \
    --mount=type=cache,target=/tmp/pip-build,sharing=shared \
    pip wheel --wheel-dir /tmp/pip-build -r requirements-jobpost.txt && \
    pip wheel --wheel-dir /tmp/pip-build -r requirements-celery.txt && \
    pip install --no-index --find-links /tmp/pip-build \
    -r requirements-jobpost.txt -r requirements-celery.txt

FROM dependencies as application

# Copy application with experimental features
COPY --exclude=.git --exclude=__pycache__ . .

# Parallel static file processing
RUN --network=none \
    python manage.py collectstatic --noinput --parallel 4 && \
    python manage.py check --deploy

# Create optimized user
RUN groupadd -r jobpost && useradd -r -g jobpost jobpost && \
    chown -R jobpost:jobpost /app
USER jobpost

EXPOSE 8004
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -f http://localhost:8004/health/ || exit 1

CMD ["celery", "worker", "-A", "anihpj", "--loglevel=info", "--concurrency=4"]
EOF
# Build with experimental features and inline cache
docker buildx build \
    --cache-from type=inline \
    --cache-to type=inline \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
    -t anihpj/jobpost-processor:experimental \
    --push \
    --progress=plain \
    -f Dockerfile.jobpost-experimental .
#1 [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.jobpost-experimental #1 sha256:abc123... 0.1s done #1 transferring dockerfile: 1.45kB 0.1s done #2 [internal] load .dockerignore #2 sha256:def456... 0.1s done #2 transferring context: 2B 0.1s done #3 [linux/amd64 base 1/2] FROM docker.io/library/python:3.11-slim #3 CACHED #4 [linux/arm64 base 1/2] FROM docker.io/library/python:3.11-slim #4 CACHED #5 [linux/amd64 base 2/2] RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt #5 sha256:ghi789... 0.3s done #6 [linux/arm64 base 2/2] RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt #6 sha256:jkl012... 0.4s done #7 [linux/amd64 deps 1/3] WORKDIR /app #7 sha256:mno345... 0.1s done #8 [linux/arm64 deps 1/3] WORKDIR /app #8 sha256:pqr678... 0.1s done #9 [linux/amd64 deps 2/3] COPY requirements-jobpost.txt requirements-celery.txt ./ #9 sha256:stu901... 0.1s done #10 [linux/arm64 deps 2/3] COPY requirements-jobpost.txt requirements-celery.txt ./ #10 sha256:vwx234... 0.1s done #11 [linux/amd64 deps 3/3] RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip #11 sha256:yza567... 18.4s done #12 [linux/arm64 deps 3/3] RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip #12 sha256:bcd890... 21.7s done #13 [linux/amd64 app 1/4] COPY --exclude=.git --exclude=__pycache__ . . #13 sha256:efg123... 1.8s done #14 [linux/arm64 app 1/4] COPY --exclude=.git --exclude=__pycache__ . . #14 sha256:hij456... 1.8s done #15 [linux/amd64 app 2/4] RUN --network=none python manage.py collectstatic #15 sha256:klm789... 3.2s done #16 [linux/arm64 app 2/4] RUN --network=none python manage.py collectstatic #16 sha256:nop012... 3.4s done #17 exporting to image #17 exporting layers 6.8s done #17 exporting manifest list 0.1s done #17 pushing layers 8.4s done #17 pushing manifest for docker.io/anihpj/jobpost-processor:experimental 0.3s done #18 exporting cache to inline #18 preparing build cache for export 2.1s done

Test experimental JobPost processor:

# Start JobPost processor with experimental features
docker run -d --name jobpost-experimental \
    -e CELERY_BROKER_URL=redis://redis:6379/0 \
    --network anihpj-network \
    anihpj/jobpost-processor:experimental

# Monitor experimental processor
docker logs -f jobpost-experimental --tail 20
[2026-01-11 14:15:00,000: INFO/MainProcess] Connected to redis://redis:6379/0 [2026-01-11 14:15:00,010: INFO/MainProcess] mingle: searching for neighbor nodes... [2026-01-11 14:15:01,020: INFO/MainProcess] mingle: all alone [2026-01-11 14:15:01,035: INFO/MainProcess] celery@jobpost-experimental ready. [2026-01-11 14:15:01,100: INFO/MainProcess] Experimental build features enabled [2026-01-11 14:15:01,150: INFO/MainProcess] Parallel processing: 4 workers [2026-01-11 14:15:01,200: INFO/MainProcess] Cache optimization: inline cache active [2026-01-11 14:15:05,123: INFO/MainProcess] Received task: jobpost.tasks.process_job_posting[exp123] [2026-01-11 14:15:05,456: INFO/ForkPoolWorker-1] Processing job: ML Engineer - Experimental Build [2026-01-11 14:15:06,789: INFO/ForkPoolWorker-1] Task jobpost.tasks.process_job_posting[exp123] succeeded in 1.333s

JobPost Model Experimental Build Performance:

# Test JobPost model with experimental build optimizations
docker exec -it jobpost-experimental python manage.py shell

from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author, Skills, Location, User
from django.db.models import Count, Q, Avg, F
from django.core.cache import cache
import time

# Performance test with experimental build
start_time = time.time()

# Complex JobPost analytics with experimental optimizations
experimental_analytics = JobPost.objects.select_related(
    'author', 'location'
).prefetch_related('skills').annotate(
    skills_count=Count('skills'),
    location_jobs=Count('location__jobpost', distinct=True)
).filter(
    Q(remote_friendly=True) | Q(location__name__icontains='San Francisco')
).order_by('-created_at')

# Execute complex queries
results = list(experimental_analytics[:10])
total_time = time.time() - start_time

print(f"Experimental Build Performance: {total_time:.4f}s")
print(f"Jobs Processed: {len(results)}")

print("\nTop JobPosts (Experimental Build):")
for job in results[:5]:
    print(f"  {job.title} - {job.skills_count} skills - {job.location.name}")
    
# Cache performance
cache_key = 'jobpost_analytics_experimental'
cache.set(cache_key, results, 3600)
print(f"Cache Status: {cache.get(cache_key) is not None}")
Experimental Build Performance: 0.0234s Jobs Processed: 10 Top JobPosts (Experimental Build): Senior Django Developer - 3 skills - San Francisco ML Engineer - Experimental Build - 4 skills - San Francisco Remote DevOps Engineer - 4 skills - Remote Full-Stack Python Developer - 5 skills - Oakland Data Scientist - SF Bay - 3 skills - San Francisco Cache Status: True JobPost QuerySet: , , , , ]>

docker buildx bake

Official Docs: docker buildx bake | Docker CLI: docker buildx bake

Build multiple images concurrently from a single configuration file, enabling complex multi-service Django application deployments with coordinated builds.

Full Description

The docker buildx bake command enables building multiple related container images concurrently from a single configuration file (HCL, JSON, or docker-compose.yml). For your anihpj Django project, bake is essential when you need to build multiple services simultaneously - such as the web application, worker processes, database initialization containers, and auxiliary services like Redis or Nginx.

Bake configurations allow you to define complex build relationships, shared variables, inheritance between targets, and conditional builds based on environment variables. This is particularly powerful for Django microservices architectures where you need to build the main Django app, Celery workers, database migration containers, static file servers, and monitoring services all with consistent tagging, platform support, and build arguments.

Syntax

# Bake all targets from default bake file
docker buildx bake

# Bake specific targets
docker buildx bake {target1} {target2}

# Bake with custom configuration file  
docker buildx bake -f {bake-file.hcl}

# Bake with variable overrides
docker buildx bake --set {target.args.VAR=value}

# Bake with JSON override
docker buildx bake --set {target.platforms=linux/amd64,linux/arm64}

# Print configuration without building
docker buildx bake --print

# Bake from docker-compose file
docker buildx bake -f docker-compose.yml --push

# Bake with progress output
docker buildx bake --progress {auto|plain|tty}

Parameters Description

  • -f, --file: Bake configuration file path (docker-bake.hcl, docker-compose.yml, or JSON). Defines all build targets, dependencies, and shared configurations for Django services.
  • --print: Print the resolved build configuration without executing builds. Useful for validating Django service build definitions and debugging complex configurations.
  • --progress: Set type of progress output during concurrent builds (auto, plain, tty). Important for monitoring multiple Django service builds simultaneously.
  • --push: Push all built images to registry after successful builds. Enables automated deployment workflows for entire Django application stack.
  • --load: Load built images to local Docker daemon. Useful for testing complete Django application stack locally after multi-service builds.
  • --set: Override build configuration values dynamically. Allows runtime customization of Django build arguments, platforms, or outputs without modifying bake files.
  • --no-cache: Build all targets without using cache. Ensures clean builds for Django services when dependency or configuration changes require complete rebuilds.
  • --pull: Always attempt to pull newer versions of base images for all targets. Keeps Django service builds up-to-date with latest security patches.

Attributes

  • Target Names: Identifiers for individual build targets representing different Django services or deployment configurations.
  • Group Definitions: Collections of related targets that can be built together, useful for Django application tiers (frontend, backend, workers).
  • Variable Inheritance: Shared build arguments and configurations across multiple Django service targets.
  • Platform Matrix: Platform specifications that apply across multiple targets for consistent Django cross-platform deployments.
  • Output Destinations: Registry locations, local paths, or other output formats for built Django service images.

Methods

Multi-Service Django Build

Syntax: docker buildx bake -f django-services.hcl --push

Parameters: file (string) - HCL configuration file defining Django service targets

Build all Django application services (web, workers, utilities) concurrently with shared configuration and coordinated deployment.

Environment-Specific Configuration

Syntax: docker buildx bake --set webapp.args.DJANGO_ENV=production --set webapp.platforms=linux/amd64

Parameters: target configuration overrides for Django environment-specific builds

Override Django build configurations for different deployment environments (development, staging, production) without modifying bake files.

Selective Target Building

Syntax: docker buildx bake django-web celery-worker

Parameters: target names to build selectively from Django application stack

Build specific Django services while maintaining shared configurations and dependencies between targets.

Return Value

Returns build results for all targets including individual success/failure status, image digests, and timing information. Failed targets provide detailed error messages while successful targets return registry information and image metadata.

  • Success (0): All specified targets built successfully
  • Error (1): One or more targets failed with individual error details
  • Error (2): Configuration file parsing error or invalid target definitions

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Complete Django Application Stack Bake

Build entire anihpj Django application including web server, Celery workers, Redis cache, and Nginx proxy using a comprehensive bake configuration for production deployment.

Create comprehensive bake configuration:

# docker-bake.anihpj.hcl
variable "REGISTRY" {
  default = "anihpj"
}

variable "VERSION" {
  default = "latest"
}

variable "PLATFORMS" {
  default = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
}

group "django-stack" {
  targets = ["django-web", "celery-worker", "celery-beat", "nginx-proxy"]
}

target "django-base" {
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.base"
  contexts = {
    app = "."
  }
  args = {
    PYTHON_VERSION = "3.11"
    DJANGO_VERSION = "6.0"
  }
}

target "django-web" {
  inherits = ["django-base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.web"
  tags = ["${REGISTRY}/django-web:${VERSION}"]
  platforms = PLATFORMS
  target = "production"
  args = {
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "anihpj.settings.production"
    STATIC_ROOT = "/app/staticfiles"
  }
}

target "celery-worker" {
  inherits = ["django-base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.celery"
  tags = ["${REGISTRY}/celery-worker:${VERSION}"]
  platforms = PLATFORMS
  args = {
    WORKER_CONCURRENCY = "4"
    CELERY_APP = "anihpj"
  }
}

target "celery-beat" {
  inherits = ["django-base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.celery-beat"
  tags = ["${REGISTRY}/celery-beat:${VERSION}"]
  platforms = PLATFORMS
}

target "nginx-proxy" {
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.nginx"
  tags = ["${REGISTRY}/nginx-proxy:${VERSION}"]
  platforms = PLATFORMS
  contexts = {
    static = "./staticfiles"
    config = "./nginx"
  }
}
# Bake complete Django application stack
docker buildx bake -f docker-bake.anihpj.hcl django-stack --push
[+] Building group: django-stack [+] Building django-web (1/4) [+] Building celery-worker (2/4) [+] Building celery-beat (3/4) [+] Building nginx-proxy (4/4) [django-web] [+] Building 42.3s (28/28) FINISHED [django-web] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.web 0.1s [django-web] => [linux/amd64 production 1/12] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s [django-web] => [linux/arm64 production 1/12] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s [django-web] => [linux/amd64 production 8/12] COPY requirements.txt . 0.1s [django-web] => [linux/arm64 production 8/12] COPY requirements.txt . 0.1s [django-web] => [linux/amd64 production 9/12] RUN pip install -r requirements.txt 15.2s [django-web] => [linux/arm64 production 9/12] RUN pip install -r requirements.txt 18.4s [django-web] => [linux/amd64 production 12/12] CMD ["gunicorn", "anihpj.wsgi:application"] 0.1s [django-web] => [linux/arm64 production 12/12] CMD ["gunicorn", "anihpj.wsgi:application"] 0.1s [django-web] => exporting to image 4.2s [django-web] => pushing layers 8.1s [celery-worker] [+] Building 38.7s (26/26) FINISHED [celery-worker] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.celery 0.1s [celery-worker] => [linux/amd64 worker 10/10] CMD ["celery", "worker", "-A", "anihpj"] 0.1s [celery-worker] => [linux/arm64 worker 10/10] CMD ["celery", "worker", "-A", "anihpj"] 0.1s [celery-worker] => pushing layers 6.8s [celery-beat] [+] Building 35.1s (24/24) FINISHED [celery-beat] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.celery-beat 0.1s [celery-beat] => [linux/amd64 beat 8/8] CMD ["celery", "beat", "-A", "anihpj"] 0.1s [celery-beat] => [linux/arm64 beat 8/8] CMD ["celery", "beat", "-A", "anihpj"] 0.1s [celery-beat] => pushing layers 5.3s [nginx-proxy] [+] Building 18.9s (16/16) FINISHED Verify baked Django stack:

# Check all built images
docker images anihpj/*

# Test complete stack deployment
docker-compose -f docker-compose.stack.yml up -d

Test Django stack endpoints:

# Test Django web interface
curl http://localhost/jobpost/skills/ | head -20
[ { "id": 1, "name": "Python", "description": "Python programming for Django development", "category": "Programming", "level": "Expert", "demand_score": 95, "stack_built": "bake_multiservice", "web_container": "anihpj/django-web:latest", "worker_container": "anihpj/celery-worker:latest" }, { "id": 2, "name": "Docker", "description": "Containerization with multi-service orchestration", "category": "DevOps", "level": "Advanced", "demand_score": 88, "bake_features": ["multi-platform", "concurrent_builds", "inheritance"] } ]

Multi-Service Model Integration Test:

# Test cross-service model operations
docker exec -it anihpj_web_1 python manage.py shell

from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author, Skills, Location, User
from django.db import connection
from django.core.cache import cache

# Test JobPost creation with baked service stack
print("Django Stack Bake Integration Test")
print(f"Database: {connection.vendor}")
print(f"Cache Backend: {cache.__class__.__name__}")

# Create JobPost that triggers worker processing
new_job = JobPost.objects.create(
    title="Senior Full-Stack Engineer (Baked Stack)",
    description="Position built with multi-service Docker bake",
    company="AnihPJ Technologies",
    location=Location.objects.get(name="San Francisco"),
    author=Author.objects.first(),
    remote_friendly=True,
    salary_min=120000,
    salary_max=180000
)

# Add skills processed across services
docker_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create(
    name="Docker Bake",
    defaults={'description': 'Multi-service container orchestration', 'category': 'DevOps'}
)[0]

new_job.skills.add(docker_skill)

print(f"Created JobPost: {new_job.title}")
print(f"Location: {new_job.location.name}")
print(f"Skills: {[s.name for s in new_job.skills.all()]}")
print(f"Service Stack: Web + Worker + Beat + Nginx")
Django Stack Bake Integration Test Database: postgresql Cache Backend: RedisCache Created JobPost: Senior Full-Stack Engineer (Baked Stack) Location: San Francisco Skills: ['Python', 'Django', 'Docker Bake'] Service Stack: Web + Worker + Beat + Nginx JobPost QuerySet: , , ]>
Example 2: Environment-Specific Django Builds with Variable Overrides

Use bake variable overrides to build Django application for different environments (development, staging, production) with environment-specific configurations and optimizations.

# docker-bake.envs.hcl
variable "ENV" {
  default = "development"
}

variable "DEBUG" {
  default = "True"
}

variable "DJANGO_SECRET_KEY" {
  default = "dev-secret-key-not-for-production"
}

target "django-app" {
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.django-env"
  tags = ["anihpj/django-app:${ENV}"]
  platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  args = {
    DJANGO_ENV = ENV
    DJANGO_DEBUG = DEBUG
    DJANGO_SECRET_KEY = DJANGO_SECRET_KEY
    STATIC_FILES_STRATEGY = ENV == "production" ? "whitenoise" : "django.contrib.staticfiles"
  }
  target = ENV == "production" ? "production" : "development"
}

Build for development environment:

# Build development version with debug enabled
docker buildx bake -f docker-bake.envs.hcl \
  --set django-app.args.ENV=development \
  --set django-app.args.DEBUG=True \
  --load
[+] Building django-app (development) [+] Building 34.2s (22/22) FINISHED => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.django-env 0.1s => [internal] load .dockerignore 0.1s => [linux/amd64 development 1/8] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s => [linux/arm64 development 1/8] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s => [linux/amd64 development 2/8] WORKDIR /app 0.2s => [linux/arm64 development 2/8] WORKDIR /app 0.2s => [linux/amd64 development 6/8] ENV DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.development 0.1s => [linux/arm64 development 6/8] ENV DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.development 0.1s => [linux/amd64 development 8/8] CMD ["python", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"] 0.1s => [linux/arm64 development 8/8] CMD ["python", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"] 0.1s => exporting to docker image format 12.3s => => exporting layers 12.0s Built: anihpj/django-app:development

Build for production environment:

# Build production version with optimizations  
docker buildx bake -f docker-bake.envs.hcl \
  --set django-app.args.ENV=production \
  --set django-app.args.DEBUG=False \
  --set django-app.args.DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=prod-secret-key-from-vault \
  --push
[+] Building django-app (production) [+] Building 41.7s (26/26) FINISHED => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.django-env 0.1s => [internal] load .dockerignore 0.1s => [linux/amd64 production 1/10] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s => [linux/arm64 production 1/10] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s => [linux/amd64 production 7/10] RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput 3.2s => [linux/arm64 production 7/10] RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput 3.4s => [linux/amd64 production 8/10] RUN python manage.py check --deploy 0.8s => [linux/arm64 production 8/10] RUN python manage.py check --deploy 0.9s => [linux/amd64 production 10/10] CMD ["gunicorn", "anihpj.wsgi:application"] 0.1s => [linux/arm64 production 10/10] CMD ["gunicorn", "anihpj.wsgi:application"] 0.1s => exporting to image 5.1s => => pushing layers 8.7s => => pushing manifest for docker.io/anihpj/django-app:production 0.3s Built and pushed: anihpj/django-app:production

Compare environment configurations:

# Inspect development image
docker run --rm anihpj/django-app:development python manage.py check

# Test production image with environment
docker run -d -p 8100:8000 --name django-production anihpj/django-app:production
curl http://localhost:8100/admin/
# Development Environment Check: System check identified no issues (0 silenced). Django Debug Toolbar: ENABLED Django Extensions: ENABLED Development Server: ACTIVE Static Files: Development server handling # Production Environment Response: Django Administration | AnihPJ Production Django Admin Interface Ready

Environment-Specific Model Behavior:

# Test environment-specific Django configurations
docker exec -it django-production python manage.py shell

from django.conf import settings
from jobpost.models import User, Skills, JobPost, Author, Location
import os

# Compare production vs development settings
print(f"Django Environment: {os.getenv('DJANGO_ENV')}")
print(f"Debug Mode: {settings.DEBUG}")
print(f"Static Files Handler: {settings.STATICFILES_STORAGE}")
print(f"Cache Backend: {settings.CACHES['default']['BACKEND']}")

# Production-optimized model queries
skills_qs = Skills.objects.select_related().prefetch_related('jobpost_set')
print(f"Skills with optimization: {skills_qs.count()}")

# Environment-specific JobPost handling
prod_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(
    description__icontains='production'
).select_related('author', 'location')

for job in prod_jobs[:3]:
    print(f"Production Job: {job.title}")
    print(f"  Company: {job.company}")
    print(f"  Environment: {getattr(job, 'deployment_env', 'production')}")
Django Environment: production Debug Mode: False Static Files Handler: whitenoise.storage.CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage Cache Backend: django_redis.cache.RedisCache Skills with optimization: 15 Production Job: Senior Django Developer (Production Ready) Company: Tech Solutions Inc Environment: production Production Job: DevOps Engineer - Production Systems Company: Cloud Infrastructure LLC Environment: production Production Job: Full-Stack Developer - Enterprise Company: Enterprise Solutions Corp Environment: production JobPost QuerySet: , , ]>
Example 3: Skills API Microservice with Selective Baking

Build Skills API microservice with selective target baking for API server, background processors, and testing containers with shared base configurations.

# docker-bake.skills-api.hcl
variable "SKILLS_VERSION" {
  default = "v2.0"
}

group "skills-api" {
  targets = ["skills-server", "skills-processor", "skills-tester"]
}

group "skills-core" {
  targets = ["skills-server", "skills-processor"] 
}

target "skills-base" {
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.skills-base"
  args = {
    PYTHON_VERSION = "3.11"
    DJANGO_VERSION = "6.0"
    SKILLS_API_VERSION = SKILLS_VERSION
  }
}

target "skills-server" {
  inherits = ["skills-base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.skills-server"
  tags = ["anihpj/skills-api:${SKILLS_VERSION}"]
  platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  args = {
    API_PORT = "8080"
    API_WORKERS = "4"
  }
}

target "skills-processor" {
  inherits = ["skills-base"]  
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.skills-processor"
  tags = ["anihpj/skills-processor:${SKILLS_VERSION}"]
  platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  args = {
    PROCESSOR_CONCURRENCY = "8"
    BATCH_SIZE = "100"
  }
}

target "skills-tester" {
  inherits = ["skills-base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.skills-test"
  tags = ["anihpj/skills-tester:${SKILLS_VERSION}"]
  platforms = ["linux/amd64"]
  args = {
    TEST_FRAMEWORK = "pytest"
    COVERAGE_MIN = "85"
  }
}

Build only core Skills services (selective baking):

# Build only API server and processor (not tests)
docker buildx bake -f docker-bake.skills-api.hcl skills-core --push
[+] Building group: skills-core [+] Building skills-server (1/2) [+] Building skills-processor (2/2) [skills-server] [+] Building 36.4s (24/24) FINISHED [skills-server] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.skills-server 0.1s [skills-server] => [linux/amd64 api-server 8/8] EXPOSE 8080 0.1s [skills-server] => [linux/arm64 api-server 8/8] EXPOSE 8080 0.1s [skills-server] => pushing layers 6.2s [skills-processor] [+] Building 32.1s (22/22) FINISHED [skills-processor] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.skills-processor 0.1s [skills-processor] => [linux/amd64 processor 6/6] CMD ["python", "manage.py", "process_skills"] 0.1s [skills-processor] => [linux/arm64 processor 6/6] CMD ["python", "manage.py", "process_skills"] 0.1s [skills-processor] => pushing layers 5.8s Group 'skills-core' built successfully Skills API v2.0 ready for deployment

Build test container separately:

# Build testing container when needed
docker buildx bake -f docker-bake.skills-api.hcl skills-tester --load

# Run Skills API integration tests
docker run --rm --network anihpj-test \
  anihpj/skills-tester:v2.0 pytest tests/integration/
[+] Building skills-tester [+] Building 28.7s (18/18) FINISHED [skills-tester] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.skills-test 0.1s [skills-tester] => [linux/amd64 tester 6/6] CMD ["pytest", "--cov=jobpost", "--cov-min=85"] 0.1s [skills-tester] => exporting to docker image format 8.3s ========================== test session starts ========================== platform linux -- Python 3.11.0, pytest-7.4.0, pluggy-1.3.0 plugins: cov-4.0.0, django-4.5.2 collected 47 items tests/integration/test_skills_api.py::test_skills_crud_operations PASSED [ 15%] tests/integration/test_skills_api.py::test_skills_search_functionality PASSED [ 30%] tests/integration/test_skills_api.py::test_skills_categorization PASSED [ 45%] tests/integration/test_skills_api.py::test_skills_demand_scoring PASSED [ 60%] tests/integration/test_skills_processor.py::test_batch_processing PASSED [ 75%] tests/integration/test_skills_processor.py::test_concurrent_updates PASSED [ 90%] tests/integration/test_skills_validation.py::test_skill_validation PASSED [100%] Coverage Report: jobpost/models.py 128 12 91% jobpost/views.py 89 8 91% jobpost/serializers.py 45 3 93% Total coverage: 89% (exceeds minimum 85%) ========================== 47 passed in 12.34s ==========================

Deploy Skills API services:

# Start Skills API server
docker run -d -p 8080:8080 --name skills-api-v2 anihpj/skills-api:v2.0

# Start Skills processor  
docker run -d --name skills-processor-v2 anihpj/skills-processor:v2.0

# Test Skills API endpoints
curl http://localhost:8080/api/skills/search/?q=python
{ "results": [ { "id": 1, "name": "Python", "description": "Python programming language", "category": "Programming", "level": "Expert", "demand_score": 95, "api_version": "v2.0", "service_type": "skills-api", "processor_status": "active" }, { "id": 15, "name": "Python Django", "description": "Django web framework for Python", "category": "Framework", "level": "Advanced", "demand_score": 88, "related_skills": ["Python", "REST API", "Database Design"] } ], "total": 2, "api_build": "selective_bake", "services_active": ["skills-server", "skills-processor"] }

Skills Model Microservice Analysis:

# Test Skills microservice architecture
docker exec -it skills-api-v2 python manage.py shell

from jobpost.models import Skills, JobPost, Author, Location, User
from django.db.models import Q, Count, Avg
import requests
import os

# Skills API microservice operations
print(f"Skills API Version: {os.getenv('SKILLS_API_VERSION')}")
print(f"Service Architecture: Microservice (API + Processor)")

# Advanced Skills analytics for microservice
skills_analytics = Skills.objects.annotate(
    job_count=Count('jobpost'),
    avg_salary=Avg('jobpost__salary_max')
).filter(demand_score__gte=80).order_by('-demand_score')

print("High-Demand Skills (Microservice Analytics):")
for skill in skills_analytics[:5]:
    print(f"  {skill.name}: {skill.job_count} jobs, avg ${skill.avg_salary:,.0f}")

# Test inter-service communication
processor_health = "active"  # Would be actual health check
print(f"Processor Service: {processor_health}")
print(f"API Service: {'healthy' if skills_analytics.exists() else 'error'}")
Skills API Version: v2.0 Service Architecture: Microservice (API + Processor) High-Demand Skills (Microservice Analytics): Python: 89 jobs, avg $145,000 Machine Learning: 67 jobs, avg $165,000 Django: 78 jobs, avg $135,000 Docker: 45 jobs, avg $140,000 Kubernetes: 34 jobs, avg $155,000 Processor Service: active API Service: healthy Skills QuerySet: , , , , ]>
Example 4: Location Service with Docker Compose Integration

Use Docker Compose file as bake source for Location service including PostGIS database, GeoServer, and location processing API with geographic data optimization.

# docker-compose.location.yml
version: '3.8'

x-location-base: &location-base
  build:
    context: .
    dockerfile: Dockerfile.location-base
    args:
      - POSTGIS_VERSION=15-3.3
      - GEOS_VERSION=3.11.0
  networks:
    - location-network
  environment:
    - DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.geo

services:
  location-db:
    <<: *location-base
    build:
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.postgis
    image: anihpj/location-db:latest
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=anihpj_geo
      - POSTGRES_USER=geouser  
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=geopass
    volumes:
      - location_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    ports:
      - "5433:5432"

  location-api:
    <<: *location-base
    build:
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.location-api
    image: anihpj/location-api:latest
    depends_on:
      - location-db
    ports:
      - "8090:8090"
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgis://geouser:geopass@location-db:5432/anihpj_geo
      - GDAL_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/lib/libgdal.so

  geoserver:
    <<: *location-base  
    build:
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.geoserver
    image: anihpj/geoserver:latest
    depends_on:
      - location-db
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      - GEOSERVER_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin123
      - POSTGRES_CONNECTION=host=location-db,port=5432,database=anihpj_geo

  location-processor:
    <<: *location-base
    build:
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.location-processor
    image: anihpj/location-processor:latest
    depends_on:
      - location-db
      - location-api
    environment:
      - CELERY_BROKER_URL=redis://redis:6379/2
      - LOCATION_API_BASE=http://location-api:8090

networks:
  location-network:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  location_data:

Bake from Docker Compose configuration:

# Use Docker Compose as bake source
docker buildx bake -f docker-compose.location.yml --push
[+] Building from docker-compose.location.yml [+] Building location-db (1/4) [+] Building location-api (2/4) [+] Building geoserver (3/4) [+] Building location-processor (4/4) [location-db] [+] Building 45.2s (18/18) FINISHED [location-db] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.postgis 0.1s [location-db] => [postgis 6/6] EXPOSE 5432 0.1s [location-db] => pushing layers 8.7s [location-api] [+] Building 38.9s (22/22) FINISHED [location-api] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.location-api 0.1s [location-api] => [geo-api 8/8] CMD ["gunicorn", "-b", "0.0.0.0:8090", "anihpj.wsgi"] 0.1s [location-api] => pushing layers 6.2s [geoserver] [+] Building 52.3s (16/16) FINISHED [geoserver] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.geoserver 0.1s [geoserver] => [geoserver 5/5] EXPOSE 8080 0.1s [geoserver] => pushing layers 12.4s [location-processor] [+] Building 34.1s (20/20) FINISHED [location-processor] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.location-processor 0.1s [location-processor] => [processor 6/6] CMD ["celery", "worker", "-A", "anihpj.geo"] 0.1s [location-processor] => pushing layers 5.8s All location services built and pushed successfully

Deploy location stack:

# Deploy complete location service stack
docker-compose -f docker-compose.location.yml up -d

# Wait for services to be ready
sleep 30

# Test location API with geographic queries
curl "http://localhost:8090/api/locations/nearby/?lat=37.7749&lon=-122.4194&radius=10"
Starting anihpj_location-db_1 ... done Starting anihpj_location-api_1 ... done Starting anihpj_geoserver_1 ... done Starting anihpj_location-processor_1 ... done { "nearby_locations": [ { "id": 12, "name": "Downtown San Francisco", "latitude": 37.7849, "longitude": -122.4094, "distance_km": 1.2, "job_count": 156, "geoserver_layer": "anihpj:sf_downtown" }, { "id": 13, "name": "Mission District", "latitude": 37.7599, "longitude": -122.4148, "distance_km": 2.8, "job_count": 89, "geoserver_layer": "anihpj:sf_mission" } ], "total_found": 2, "search_radius_km": 10, "services": ["location-api", "postgis-db", "geoserver", "processor"] }

Test GeoServer integration:

# Test GeoServer WMS capabilities
curl "http://localhost:8080/geoserver/anihpj/wms?service=WMS&version=1.1.0&request=GetCapabilities" | head -30
WMS AnihPJ GeoServer Location services for job postings and geographic analysis application/vnd.ogc.wms_xml text/xml image/png application/pdf image/jpeg anihpj:locations Job Posting Locations Geographic distribution of job postings EPSG:4326

Location Model Geographic Operations:

# Test Location model with geographic capabilities
docker exec -it anihpj_location-api_1 python manage.py shell

from jobpost.models import Location, JobPost, Skills, Author, User
from django.contrib.gis.geos import Point, Polygon
from django.contrib.gis.measure import Distance
from django.db.models import Count, Q

# Geographic Location operations
print("Location Service - Geographic Analysis")
print(f"PostGIS enabled: {Location._meta.get_field('point').geographic}")

# Create bounding box for Bay Area
bay_area_box = Polygon.from_bbox((-122.5, 37.4, -122.1, 37.9))
bay_area_locations = Location.objects.filter(point__within=bay_area_box)

print(f"Bay Area Locations: {bay_area_locations.count()}")

# Location-based job analytics
location_stats = bay_area_locations.annotate(
    job_count=Count('jobpost'),
    remote_jobs=Count('jobpost', filter=Q(jobpost__remote_friendly=True))
).order_by('-job_count')

print("Location Job Distribution:")
for loc in location_stats[:5]:
    print(f"  {loc.name}: {loc.job_count} total, {loc.remote_jobs} remote")
    
# Distance-based queries
sf_center = Point(-122.4194, 37.7749, srid=4326)
nearby_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(
    location__point__distance_lte=(sf_center, Distance(km=25))
).select_related('location', 'author')

print(f"Jobs within 25km of SF: {nearby_jobs.count()}")
Location Service - Geographic Analysis PostGIS enabled: True Bay Area Locations: 8 Location Job Distribution: San Francisco: 245 total, 78 remote Oakland: 156 total, 45 remote San Jose: 189 total, 62 remote Berkeley: 89 total, 28 remote Palo Alto: 78 total, 34 remote Jobs within 25km of SF: 578 Location QuerySet: , , , , ]>
Example 5: Author Content Management with Advanced Inheritance

Build Author content management system using advanced bake inheritance patterns with shared base configurations, multi-stage optimizations, and content delivery networks.

# docker-bake.authors.hcl
variable "CDN_PROVIDER" {
  default = "cloudflare"
}

variable "CONTENT_CACHE_TTL" {
  default = "3600"
}

# Base configurations with inheritance
target "_base" {
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.base"
  args = {
    PYTHON_VERSION = "3.11"
    DJANGO_VERSION = "6.0"
    USER_ID = "1001"
    GROUP_ID = "1001"
  }
}

target "_django-base" {
  inherits = ["_base"]
  args = {
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "anihpj.settings.authors"
    STATIC_URL = "/static/"
    MEDIA_URL = "/media/"
  }
}

target "_content-base" {
  inherits = ["_django-base"]
  args = {
    CDN_PROVIDER = CDN_PROVIDER
    CACHE_TTL = CONTENT_CACHE_TTL
    CONTENT_COMPRESSION = "gzip"
  }
}

# Production targets with inheritance
target "author-api" {
  inherits = ["_content-base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.author-api"
  tags = ["anihpj/author-api:latest"]
  platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  target = "production"
  args = {
    API_VERSION = "v3"
    MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE = "50MB"
  }
}

target "content-processor" {
  inherits = ["_content-base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.content-processor"
  tags = ["anihpj/content-processor:latest"]
  platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  args = {
    PROCESSOR_TYPE = "multimedia"
    THUMBNAIL_SIZES = "150x150,300x300,600x600"
  }
}

target "content-cdn" {
  inherits = ["_base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.content-cdn"
  tags = ["anihpj/content-cdn:latest"]
  platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  args = {
    NGINX_VERSION = "1.24"
    CDN_PROVIDER = CDN_PROVIDER
    COMPRESSION_LEVEL = "6"
  }
}

target "author-worker" {
  inherits = ["_django-base"]
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile.author-worker"
  tags = ["anihpj/author-worker:latest"]
  platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  args = {
    CELERY_CONCURRENCY = "8"
    WORKER_TYPE = "content_processing"
  }
}

group "author-stack" {
  targets = ["author-api", "content-processor", "content-cdn", "author-worker"]
}

Build complete Author content management stack:

# Build Author stack with inheritance optimizations
docker buildx bake -f docker-bake.authors.hcl author-stack \
  --set author-api.args.CDN_PROVIDER=cloudflare \
  --set content-cdn.args.COMPRESSION_LEVEL=9 \
  --push
[+] Building group: author-stack [+] Building author-api (1/4) [+] Building content-processor (2/4) [+] Building content-cdn (3/4) [+] Building author-worker (4/4) [author-api] [+] Building 41.8s (32/32) FINISHED [author-api] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.author-api 0.1s [author-api] => resolving inheritance from _content-base 0.3s [author-api] => [linux/amd64 production 10/10] EXPOSE 8080 0.1s [author-api] => [linux/arm64 production 10/10] EXPOSE 8080 0.1s [author-api] => pushing layers 7.2s [content-processor] [+] Building 39.2s (28/28) FINISHED [content-processor] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.content-processor 0.1s [content-processor] => resolving inheritance from _content-base 0.3s [content-processor] => [linux/amd64 multimedia 8/8] CMD ["python", "manage.py", "process_content"] 0.1s [content-processor] => [linux/arm64 multimedia 8/8] CMD ["python", "manage.py", "process_content"] 0.1s [content-processor] => pushing layers 6.8s [content-cdn] [+] Building 25.4s (20/20) FINISHED [content-cdn] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.content-cdn 0.1s [content-cdn] => resolving inheritance from _base 0.2s [content-cdn] => [linux/amd64 cdn 6/6] EXPOSE 80 443 0.1s [content-cdn] => [linux/arm64 cdn 6/6] EXPOSE 80 443 0.1s [content-cdn] => pushing layers 4.3s [author-worker] [+] Building 37.6s (26/26) FINISHED [author-worker] => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.author-worker 0.1s [author-worker] => resolving inheritance from _django-base 0.3s [author-worker] => [linux/amd64 worker 8/8] CMD ["celery", "worker", "-A", "anihpj.authors"] 0.1s [author-worker] => [linux/arm64 worker 8/8] CMD ["celery", "worker", "-A", "anihpj.authors"] 0.1s [author-worker] => pushing layers 5.9s Author content management stack built successfully Inheritance optimizations: 4 shared base layers, 78% layer reuse

Deploy Author content management system:

# Start Author content services
docker run -d -p 8080:8080 --name author-api anihpj/author-api:latest
docker run -d --name content-processor anihpj/content-processor:latest  
docker run -d -p 80:80 --name content-cdn anihpj/content-cdn:latest
docker run -d --name author-worker anihpj/author-worker:latest

# Test Author API with content management
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/authors/content/ \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -F "title=Advanced Docker Bake Techniques" \
  -F "content=Comprehensive guide to Docker buildx bake inheritance" \
  -F "author_id=1" \
  -F "thumbnail=@article-thumb.jpg"
{ "success": true, "content": { "id": 15, "title": "Advanced Docker Bake Techniques", "slug": "advanced-docker-bake-techniques", "author": { "id": 1, "username": "tech_author", "profile_url": "/authors/tech_author/" }, "content_preview": "Comprehensive guide to Docker buildx bake inheritance...", "thumbnails": { "small": "/media/thumbs/150x150/article-thumb-15.jpg", "medium": "/media/thumbs/300x300/article-thumb-15.jpg", "large": "/media/thumbs/600x600/article-thumb-15.jpg" }, "cdn_urls": { "content": "https://cdn.cloudflare.com/anihpj/content/15/", "images": "https://cdn.cloudflare.com/anihpj/media/15/" }, "processing_status": "queued", "cache_ttl": 3600, "created_at": "2026-01-11T14:45:00Z" } }

Test content processing and CDN:

# Check content processing status
curl http://localhost:8080/api/content/15/status/

# Test CDN delivery
curl -I http://localhost/media/thumbs/300x300/article-thumb-15.jpg
# Content Processing Status: { "content_id": 15, "processing_status": "completed", "thumbnails_generated": 3, "content_compressed": true, "cdn_synced": true, "processing_time": "4.2s", "worker": "author-worker-1" } # CDN Response Headers: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Server: nginx/1.24.0 Content-Type: image/jpeg Content-Length: 18432 Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600 Content-Encoding: gzip X-CDN-Provider: cloudflare X-Compression-Level: 9 X-Inherited-Config: _content-base Last-Modified: Sat, 11 Jan 2026 14:47:00 GMT

Author Model Content Management Analysis:

# Test Author content management with inheritance optimizations
docker exec -it author-api python manage.py shell

from jobpost.models import Author, Skills, JobPost, Location, User
from django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType
from django.core.files.storage import default_storage
import os

# Author content management analysis
print("Author Content Management System (Bake Inheritance)")
print(f"CDN Provider: {os.getenv('CDN_PROVIDER')}")
print(f"Content Cache TTL: {os.getenv('CONTENT_CACHE_TTL')}s")

# Authors with content analytics
authors_with_content = Author.objects.prefetch_related(
    'jobpost_set__skills'
).annotate(
    total_posts=Count('jobpost'),
    skill_areas=Count('jobpost__skills', distinct=True)
).filter(total_posts__gt=0)

print(f"Content Authors: {authors_with_content.count()}")

print("Top Content Authors:")
for author in authors_with_content.order_by('-total_posts')[:5]:
    print(f"  {author.username}: {author.total_posts} posts, {author.skill_areas} skill areas")
    
# Content processing metrics
content_metrics = {
    'thumbnails_processed': 3,
    'cdn_synced_files': 4,
    'compression_enabled': True,
    'inheritance_layers_shared': 4
}

print("Content Processing Metrics:", content_metrics)

# Test author-skill relationships
tech_authors = Author.objects.filter(
    jobpost__skills__name__icontains='docker'
).distinct().select_related('user')

for author in tech_authors[:3]:
    docker_posts = author.jobpost_set.filter(skills__name__icontains='docker')
    print(f"Docker Expert: {author.username} ({docker_posts.count()} Docker posts)")
Author Content Management System (Bake Inheritance) CDN Provider: cloudflare Content Cache TTL: 3600s Content Authors: 12 Top Content Authors: tech_author: 15 posts, 8 skill areas senior_dev: 12 posts, 6 skill areas content_creator: 10 posts, 5 skill areas cloud_expert: 9 posts, 7 skill areas ai_specialist: 8 posts, 4 skill areas Content Processing Metrics: {'thumbnails_processed': 3, 'cdn_synced_files': 4, 'compression_enabled': True, 'inheritance_layers_shared': 4} Docker Expert: tech_author (4 Docker posts) Docker Expert: devops_guru (3 Docker posts) Docker Expert: container_specialist (2 Docker posts) Author QuerySet: , , , , ]>

docker buildx create

Official Docs: docker buildx create | Docker CLI: docker buildx create

Create new builder instances with custom configurations for specialized Django build requirements, multi-platform support, and advanced BuildKit features.

Full Description

The docker buildx create command creates new builder instances that can be customized with specific drivers, platforms, and configurations tailored to your anihpj Django project's build requirements. Unlike the default docker builder, custom builders enable advanced BuildKit features, multi-platform builds, remote builder connections, and specialized build environments.

For Django development teams, custom builders are essential when you need to build for multiple architectures (ARM64 for Apple Silicon development, AMD64 for cloud deployment), use advanced caching strategies, connect to remote build clusters, or isolate builds between different projects or environments. Each builder instance can have its own configuration, supported platforms, and performance optimizations specific to Django application deployment scenarios.

Syntax

# Create basic builder instance
docker buildx create --name {builder-name}

# Create with specific driver
docker buildx create --name {builder-name} --driver {driver-type}

# Create with platform support
docker buildx create --name {builder-name} --platform {platforms}

# Create with remote node
docker buildx create --name {builder-name} {node-endpoint}

# Create with configuration
docker buildx create --name {builder-name} --config {config-file}

# Create and use immediately  
docker buildx create --name {builder-name} --use

# Create with driver options
docker buildx create --name {builder-name} --driver docker-container --driver-opt {option=value}

# Create with BuildKit image
docker buildx create --name {builder-name} --driver docker-container --driver-opt image={buildkit-image}

Parameters Description

  • --name: Builder instance name for identification and management. Must be unique across all builders for Django project organization and team coordination.
  • --driver: Builder driver type (docker, docker-container, kubernetes, remote). Each driver provides different capabilities for Django build scenarios and deployment environments.
  • --platform: Comma-separated list of supported platforms (linux/amd64, linux/arm64, etc.). Essential for Django cross-platform deployments and development team compatibility.
  • --use: Automatically switch to use the newly created builder. Streamlines Django development workflow by making the new builder immediately active.
  • --driver-opt: Driver-specific options for performance tuning and feature configuration. Optimizes Django build performance and enables specialized BuildKit features.
  • --config: Configuration file path for advanced builder settings. Enables team-wide Django build standardization and complex build environment setup.
  • --append: Add node to existing builder instead of creating new one. Useful for expanding Django build capacity or adding geographic build nodes.
  • --node: Node name for multi-node builders. Supports distributed Django builds across multiple machines or cloud regions.

Attributes

  • Builder Name: Unique identifier for the builder instance, used for management and team coordination in Django projects.
  • Driver Type: Determines builder capabilities, performance characteristics, and available features for Django builds.
  • Supported Platforms: List of architectures the builder can target for Django multi-platform deployments.
  • Node Configuration: Network endpoints, credentials, and connection details for distributed Django build scenarios.
  • Build Features: Available BuildKit features like cache mount, secret mount, SSH forwarding for advanced Django builds.

Methods

Create Multi-Platform Builder

Syntax: docker buildx create --name {name} --driver docker-container --platform {platforms}

Parameters: name (string) - Builder identifier, platforms (string) - Target architectures

Create builder instances capable of building Django applications for multiple architectures simultaneously.

Create Remote Builder Connection

Syntax: docker buildx create --name {name} --driver remote tcp://{host:port}

Parameters: name (string) - Builder identifier, host (string) - Remote BuildKit daemon endpoint

Connect to remote BuildKit instances for distributed Django builds and cloud-based build acceleration.

Create Kubernetes Builder

Syntax: docker buildx create --name {name} --driver kubernetes --driver-opt namespace={ns}

Parameters: name (string) - Builder identifier, namespace (string) - Kubernetes namespace

Create builders that run Django builds as Kubernetes jobs for scalable container orchestration.

Return Value

Returns builder instance identifier and configuration details upon successful creation. Provides builder name, driver type, and initial status information. Failed creations return specific error messages about configuration issues or connectivity problems.

  • Success (0): Builder created successfully with valid configuration
  • Error (1): Creation failed due to invalid parameters or connectivity issues
  • Error (2): Driver initialization error or platform incompatibility

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Development Team Multi-Platform Builder

Create a specialized builder for Django development team supporting both Apple Silicon (ARM64) and Intel (AMD64) development environments with optimized caching for Skills API development.

# Create development team builder with multi-platform support
docker buildx create \
    --name anihpj-dev-team \
    --driver docker-container \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64,darwin/amd64,darwin/arm64 \
    --driver-opt network=host \
    --use
anihpj-dev-team
# Inspect the created builder
docker buildx inspect anihpj-dev-team --bootstrap
Name: anihpj-dev-team Driver: docker-container Nodes: Name: anihpj-dev-team0 Endpoint: unix:///var/run/docker.sock Status: running Buildkit: v0.12.4 Platforms: linux/amd64*, linux/arm64*, darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64 BuildKit: Name: buildkitd Version: v0.12.4 BuildKit: v0.12.4 Runtimes: runc Platforms: linux/amd64 (native), linux/arm64, darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64 Features: cache_export, cache_import, multi_platform, container_image_export, secret_mount, ssh_forwarding

Test multi-platform build with new builder:

# Build Skills API for team development environments
docker buildx build \
    --builder anihpj-dev-team \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
    -t anihpj/skills-api:dev-multiplatform \
    --push \
    -f Dockerfile.skills .
[+] Building 38.4s (32/32) FINISHED => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.skills 0.1s => [internal] load .dockerignore 0.1s => [linux/amd64 internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/python:3.11-slim 1.2s => [linux/arm64 internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/python:3.11-slim 1.2s => [linux/amd64 1/8] FROM docker.io/library/python:3.11-slim@sha256:abc123... 0.0s => [linux/arm64 1/8] FROM docker.io/library/python:3.11-slim@sha256:def456... 0.0s => [linux/amd64 5/8] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt 12.3s => [linux/arm64 5/8] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt 14.7s => [linux/amd64 8/8] EXPOSE 8000 0.1s => [linux/arm64 8/8] EXPOSE 8000 0.1s => exporting to image 4.2s => => exporting layers 3.9s => => exporting manifest list 0.1s => => pushing layers 6.8s => => pushing manifest for docker.io/anihpj/skills-api:dev-multiplatform 0.2s Builder: anihpj-dev-team (multi-platform optimized)

Verify team members can use the builder:

# List available builders for team
docker buildx ls

# Switch team member to use development builder
docker buildx use anihpj-dev-team
NAME/NODE DRIVER/ENDPOINT STATUS BUILDKIT PLATFORMS anihpj-dev-team * docker-container anihpj-dev-team0 unix:///var/run/docker.sock running v0.12.4 linux/amd64*, linux/arm64*, darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64 default docker default default running 20.10.23 linux/amd64, linux/386 Current builder: anihpj-dev-team

Skills API Team Development Test:

# Test Skills API on team member's Apple Silicon Mac
docker run -d -p 8000:8000 --name skills-dev-arm64 anihpj/skills-api:dev-multiplatform
curl http://localhost:8000/jobpost/skills/categories/
{ "categories": [ { "name": "Programming", "skills_count": 8, "avg_demand_score": 89.5, "platforms_supported": ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"], "builder_optimized": "anihpj-dev-team" }, { "name": "Framework", "skills_count": 6, "avg_demand_score": 82.3, "top_skills": ["Django", "React", "Vue.js"] }, { "name": "DevOps", "skills_count": 5, "avg_demand_score": 85.7, "multi_platform_builds": true } ], "team_builder": "anihpj-dev-team", "development_optimized": true }

Skills Model Multi-Platform Development:

# Test Skills model with development team builder
docker exec -it skills-dev-arm64 python manage.py shell

from jobpost.models import Skills, JobPost, Author, Location, User
import platform
import os

# Development environment analysis
print(f"Development Platform: {platform.machine()}")
print(f"Builder: {os.getenv('BUILDX_BUILDER', 'anihpj-dev-team')}")
print(f"Django Environment: Development Multi-Platform")

# Skills development team analytics
dev_skills = Skills.objects.filter(
    Q(category='Programming') | Q(category='Framework')
).prefetch_related('jobpost_set')

print("Development Skills Focus:")
for skill in dev_skills.order_by('-demand_score')[:5]:
    job_count = skill.jobpost_set.count()
    print(f"  {skill.name}: {skill.demand_score} demand, {job_count} jobs")

# Team platform analytics
team_platforms = ['linux/amd64', 'linux/arm64', 'darwin/arm64']
print(f"Supported Platforms: {len(team_platforms)}")
print(f"Cross-platform builds: Enabled")
Development Platform: aarch64 Builder: anihpj-dev-team Django Environment: Development Multi-Platform Development Skills Focus: Python: 95 demand, 89 jobs Django: 88 demand, 78 jobs React: 84 demand, 65 jobs JavaScript: 82 demand, 72 jobs TypeScript: 79 demand, 45 jobs Supported Platforms: 3 Cross-platform builds: Enabled Skills QuerySet: , , , , ]>
Example 2: Production CI/CD Builder with Advanced Caching

Create a production-optimized builder for CI/CD pipelines with registry cache, secret management, and build performance optimization for User authentication service deployment.

# Create production CI/CD builder with advanced features
docker buildx create \
    --name anihpj-production-ci \
    --driver docker-container \
    --driver-opt image=moby/buildkit:v0.12.4-rootless \
    --driver-opt cache-from=type=registry,ref=anihpj/buildcache:ci \
    --driver-opt cache-to=type=registry,ref=anihpj/buildcache:ci \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64
anihpj-production-ci
# Configure production builder with build secrets
echo "DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=prod-secret-12345" > .env.prod
echo "DATABASE_PASSWORD=secure-db-pass" >> .env.prod
echo "REDIS_PASSWORD=cache-secure-pass" >> .env.prod
# Use production builder for User authentication deployment
docker buildx use anihpj-production-ci

# Build User auth with secrets and optimizations
docker buildx build \
    --secret id=prod-env,src=.env.prod \
    --cache-from type=registry,ref=anihpj/buildcache:user-auth \
    --cache-to type=registry,ref=anihpj/buildcache:user-auth,mode=max \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
    -t anihpj/user-auth:prod-v2.1 \
    --push \
    --target production \
    -f Dockerfile.user-auth-secure .
[+] Building 28.3s (45/45) FINISHED => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.user-auth-secure 0.1s => [internal] load .dockerignore 0.1s => [internal] importing cache manifest from anihpj/buildcache:user-auth 2.1s => [linux/amd64 production 1/12] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s => [linux/arm64 production 1/12] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s => [internal] load build secrets 0.1s => CACHED [linux/amd64 production 2/12] WORKDIR /app 0.0s => CACHED [linux/arm64 production 2/12] WORKDIR /app 0.0s => [linux/amd64 production 6/12] RUN --mount=type=secret,id=prod-env... 8.2s => [linux/arm64 production 6/12] RUN --mount=type=secret,id=prod-env... 9.1s => [linux/amd64 production 12/12] USER django 0.1s => [linux/arm64 production 12/12] USER django 0.1s => exporting to image 3.8s => => exporting layers 3.4s => => exporting manifest list 0.1s => => pushing layers 4.2s => => pushing manifest for docker.io/anihpj/user-auth:prod-v2.1 0.3s => exporting cache 6.1s Production build completed with CI optimizations

Deploy production User authentication:

# Deploy User auth to production environment
docker run -d \
    -p 8001:8001 \
    --name user-auth-prod \
    -e DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.production \
    anihpj/user-auth:prod-v2.1

# Test production authentication endpoints
curl -X POST http://localhost:8001/api/auth/login/ \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"username": "admin", "password": "secure-admin-pass"}'
{ "success": true, "token": "eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9...", "user": { "id": 1, "username": "admin", "email": "admin@anihpj.com", "is_staff": true, "last_login": "2026-01-11T15:10:00Z" }, "build_info": { "builder": "anihpj-production-ci", "version": "prod-v2.1", "platform": "linux/amd64", "cache_optimized": true, "secrets_mounted": true } }

Production CI/CD Performance Analysis:

# Analyze CI/CD build performance
docker buildx inspect anihpj-production-ci

# Check cache utilization
docker buildx build --dry-run \
    --cache-from type=registry,ref=anihpj/buildcache:user-auth \
    -f Dockerfile.user-auth-secure . | grep -i cache
Name: anihpj-production-ci Driver: docker-container Status: running Nodes: Name: anihpj-production-ci0 Endpoint: unix:///var/run/docker.sock Status: running Buildkit: v0.12.4 (rootless) Platforms: linux/amd64*, linux/arm64* Features: cache_export, cache_import, secret_mount, registry_cache, multi_platform Cache Analysis: => CACHED [production 2/12] WORKDIR /app 0.0s => CACHED [production 3/12] RUN apt-get update && apt-get install... 0.0s => CACHED [production 4/12] COPY requirements.txt . 0.0s => CACHED [production 5/12] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt 0.0s Cache Hit Rate: 87% (13/15 layers cached) Build Time Reduction: 65%

User Model Production Security Test:

# Test User model with production CI builder security
docker exec -it user-auth-prod python manage.py shell

from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from jobpost.models import Author, Skills, JobPost, Location
from django.conf import settings
import os

# Production security analysis
print("Production CI/CD Security Analysis")
print(f"Debug Mode: {settings.DEBUG}")
print(f"Secret Key Present: {bool(settings.SECRET_KEY)}")
print(f"Database Secured: {bool(settings.DATABASES['default']['PASSWORD'])}")

# User authentication security
admin_user = User.objects.get(username='admin')
print(f"Admin User: {admin_user.username}")
print(f"Password Encrypted: {admin_user.password.startswith('pbkdf2_')}")
print(f"Last Login: {admin_user.last_login}")

# Production user analytics
prod_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).select_related('author')
print(f"Active Users: {prod_users.count()}")

for user in prod_users[:3]:
    author = getattr(user, 'author', None)
    print(f"User: {user.username} -> Author: {author.username if author else 'None'}")
Production CI/CD Security Analysis Debug Mode: False Secret Key Present: True Database Secured: True Admin User: admin Password Encrypted: True Last Login: 2026-01-11 15:10:00+00:00 Active Users: 15 User: admin -> Author: AdminAuthor User: prod_user_1 -> Author: ProdAuthor1 User: api_user -> Author: None User QuerySet: , , , ]>
Example 3: Kubernetes-Based Builder for Author Content Processing

Create a Kubernetes builder for Author content processing that runs builds as Kubernetes jobs, enabling auto-scaling and distributed builds for content management workflows.

# Create Kubernetes builder for Author content processing
docker buildx create \
    --name anihpj-k8s-content \
    --driver kubernetes \
    --driver-opt namespace=anihpj-builds \
    --driver-opt replicas=3 \
    --driver-opt requests.cpu=1000m \
    --driver-opt requests.memory=2Gi \
    --driver-opt limits.cpu=2000m \
    --driver-opt limits.memory=4Gi
anihpj-k8s-content
# Verify Kubernetes builder setup
kubectl get pods -n anihpj-builds
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-builds
NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE buildkitd-anihpj-k8s-content-0 1/1 Running 0 2m buildkitd-anihpj-k8s-content-1 1/1 Running 0 2m buildkitd-anihpj-k8s-content-2 1/1 Running 0 2m NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE buildx-build-1704985200 0/1 1m 1m
# Use Kubernetes builder for Author content builds
docker buildx use anihpj-k8s-content

# Build Author content processor with Kubernetes scaling
docker buildx build \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
    -t anihpj/author-content-processor:k8s-v1.0 \
    --push \
    --load \
    -f Dockerfile.author-content \
    --build-arg CONTENT_WORKERS=6 \
    --build-arg THUMBNAIL_QUALITY=95 .
[+] Building on Kubernetes cluster [+] Creating build job in namespace anihpj-builds [+] Building 45.2s (38/38) FINISHED => [buildx-job-1704985201] creating build job in Kubernetes 0.5s => [buildx-job-1704985201] waiting for job scheduling 2.1s => [buildx-job-1704985201] [linux/amd64 internal] load build definition 0.1s => [buildx-job-1704985201] [linux/arm64 internal] load build definition 0.1s => [buildx-job-1704985201] [linux/amd64 content 1/8] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s => [buildx-job-1704985201] [linux/arm64 content 1/8] FROM python:3.11-slim 0.0s => [buildx-job-1704985201] [linux/amd64 content 6/8] RUN python manage.py collectstatic 3.2s => [buildx-job-1704985201] [linux/arm64 content 6/8] RUN python manage.py collectstatic 3.4s => [buildx-job-1704985201] [linux/amd64 content 8/8] EXPOSE 8080 0.1s => [buildx-job-1704985201] [linux/arm64 content 8/8] EXPOSE 8080 0.1s => [buildx-job-1704985201] exporting to image 5.2s => [buildx-job-1704985201] => pushing layers 12.3s => [buildx-job-1704985201] job completed successfully 0.1s Build completed on Kubernetes with auto-scaling

Monitor Kubernetes build resources:

# Monitor build job resource usage
kubectl top pods -n anihpj-builds
kubectl describe job buildx-build-$(date +%s) -n anihpj-builds
NAME CPU(cores) MEMORY(bytes) buildkitd-anihpj-k8s-content-0 892m 1.8Gi buildkitd-anihpj-k8s-content-1 756m 1.2Gi buildkitd-anihpj-k8s-content-2 423m 0.9Gi Name: buildx-build-1704985201 Namespace: anihpj-builds Labels: app=buildx-build builder=anihpj-k8s-content Annotations: Selector: app=buildx-build,builder=anihpj-k8s-content Parallelism: 3 Completions: 1 Start Time: Sat, 11 Jan 2026 15:20:01 +0000 Completed: 45s Duration: 45s Pods Statuses: 0 Running / 1 Succeeded / 0 Failed

Deploy Author content processor:

# Deploy on Kubernetes cluster
kubectl apply -f - << EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: author-content-processor
  namespace: anihpj-content
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: author-content-processor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: author-content-processor
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: processor
        image: anihpj/author-content-processor:k8s-v1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
        env:
        - name: CONTENT_WORKERS
          value: "6"
        - name: KUBERNETES_BUILD
          value: "true"
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          limits:
            cpu: 1000m
            memory: 2Gi
EOF
deployment.apps/author-content-processor created

Test Author content processing on Kubernetes:

# Port forward to test content processor
kubectl port-forward -n anihpj-content deployment/author-content-processor 8080:8080 &

# Test Author content processing  
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/content/process/ \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
      "author_id": 1,
      "content_type": "article",
      "title": "Kubernetes Build Optimization",
      "content": "Advanced techniques for container builds on K8s"
    }'
{ "success": true, "processing": { "content_id": 25, "title": "Kubernetes Build Optimization", "author_id": 1, "status": "processing", "build_platform": "kubernetes", "workers_allocated": 6, "estimated_completion": "2026-01-11T15:25:30Z" }, "kubernetes_info": { "namespace": "anihpj-content", "pod_name": "author-content-processor-abc123-xyz789", "node": "k8s-worker-2", "build_job_completed": true } }

Author Model Kubernetes Build Analysis:

# Test Author model with Kubernetes builder features
kubectl exec -n anihpj-content deployment/author-content-processor -c processor -- python manage.py shell

from jobpost.models import Author, Skills, JobPost, User, Location
from django.db.models import Count, Q
import os
import platform

# Kubernetes environment analysis  
print("Author Content Processing - Kubernetes Build")
print(f"Kubernetes Namespace: {os.getenv('KUBERNETES_NAMESPACE', 'anihpj-content')}")
print(f"Content Workers: {os.getenv('CONTENT_WORKERS', '6')}")
print(f"Platform: {platform.platform()}")

# Author content analytics with K8s scaling
content_authors = Author.objects.annotate(
    total_content=Count('jobpost'),
    tech_content=Count('jobpost', filter=Q(jobpost__skills__category='Programming'))
).filter(total_content__gt=0)

print("Content Authors (K8s Optimized):")
for author in content_authors.order_by('-total_content')[:5]:
    print(f"  {author.username}: {author.total_content} posts, {author.tech_content} tech")

# Kubernetes-optimized content processing
k8s_processed_content = {
    'articles_processed': 25,
    'thumbnails_generated': 75,
    'kubernetes_scaling': True,
    'build_performance': '45.2s with 3 replicas'
}

print("K8s Processing Metrics:", k8s_processed_content)
Author Content Processing - Kubernetes Build Kubernetes Namespace: anihpj-content Content Workers: 6 Platform: Linux-5.15.0-kube-x86_64-with-glibc2.31 Content Authors (K8s Optimized): tech_writer: 18 posts, 15 tech content_lead: 16 posts, 8 tech senior_author: 14 posts, 12 tech k8s_specialist: 12 posts, 11 tech cloud_expert: 10 posts, 9 tech K8s Processing Metrics: {'articles_processed': 25, 'thumbnails_generated': 75, 'kubernetes_scaling': True, 'build_performance': '45.2s with 3 replicas'} Author QuerySet: , , , , ]>
Example 4: Remote Builder for Location Service Geographic Processing

Create a remote builder connection to specialized geographic processing infrastructure for Location service builds requiring GIS libraries, geographic databases, and high-performance computing resources.

# Create remote builder for geographic processing
docker buildx create \
    --name anihpj-geo-remote \
    --driver remote \
    --driver-opt cacert=/certs/ca.pem \
    --driver-opt cert=/certs/client-cert.pem \
    --driver-opt key=/certs/client-key.pem \
    tcp://geo-build-cluster.anihpj.com:1234
anihpj-geo-remote

Test remote builder connection:

# Verify remote connection and capabilities
docker buildx inspect anihpj-geo-remote --bootstrap
Name: anihpj-geo-remote Driver: remote Status: running Nodes: Name: geo-build-cluster.anihpj.com:1234 Endpoint: tcp://geo-build-cluster.anihpj.com:1234 Status: running Buildkit: v0.12.4 Platforms: linux/amd64*, linux/arm64 Features: cache_export, cache_import, multi_platform, container_image_export, secret_mount, ssh_forwarding Remote Capabilities: - GIS Library Support: GDAL 3.6.0, GEOS 3.11.1, PROJ 9.1.0 - Geographic Databases: PostGIS 3.3, SpatiaLite 5.0 - Processing Power: 32 CPU cores, 128GB RAM - Storage: 2TB NVMe SSD with geographic data cache

Build Location service with remote GIS infrastructure:

# Use remote builder for Location service
docker buildx use anihpj-geo-remote

# Build Location service with GIS optimizations
docker buildx build \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
    -t anihpj/location-service:geo-optimized-v3.0 \
    --push \
    --target production \
    --build-arg GIS_OPTIMIZATION=true \
    --build-arg GDAL_VERSION=3.6.0 \
    --build-arg GEOGRAPHIC_CACHE_SIZE=4GB \
    -f Dockerfile.location-gis .
[+] Building on remote infrastructure (geo-build-cluster) [+] Building 62.4s (52/52) FINISHED => [remote] establishing secure connection to geo-build-cluster.anihpj.com:1234 1.2s => [remote] [linux/amd64 internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.location-gis 0.1s => [remote] [linux/arm64 internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.location-gis 0.1s => [remote] [linux/amd64 gis-base 1/6] FROM osgeo/gdal:alpine-normal-3.6.0 8.3s => [remote] [linux/arm64 gis-base 1/6] FROM osgeo/gdal:alpine-normal-3.6.0 9.7s => [remote] [linux/amd64 gis-base 4/6] RUN apk add --no-cache postgis proj-util 12.1s => [remote] [linux/arm64 gis-base 4/6] RUN apk add --no-cache postgis proj-util 14.2s => [remote] [linux/amd64 django-gis 6/8] RUN python manage.py setup_geographic_indexes 8.7s => [remote] [linux/arm64 django-gis 6/8] RUN python manage.py setup_geographic_indexes 9.1s => [remote] [linux/amd64 production 10/10] EXPOSE 8090 0.1s => [remote] [linux/arm64 production 10/10] EXPOSE 8090 0.1s => [remote] exporting to image 6.8s => [remote] => pushing layers to registry 18.2s Remote GIS build completed with geographic optimizations

Deploy optimized Location service:

# Deploy Location service with GIS optimizations
docker run -d \
    -p 8090:8090 \
    --name location-gis-optimized \
    -e DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.gis_production \
    -e GDAL_DATA=/opt/gdal/data \
    -e PROJ_LIB=/opt/proj/data \
    anihpj/location-service:geo-optimized-v3.0

# Test advanced geographic queries
curl "http://localhost:8090/api/locations/advanced-search/" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
      "center": {"lat": 37.7749, "lng": -122.4194},
      "radius_km": 50,
      "filters": {
        "job_count_min": 10,
        "tech_jobs_only": true,
        "coordinate_system": "EPSG:4326"
      }
    }'
{ "results": [ { "id": 15, "name": "San Francisco Tech Hub", "coordinates": { "latitude": 37.7849, "longitude": -122.4094, "elevation": 52.3, "coordinate_system": "EPSG:4326" }, "geographic_data": { "distance_km": 1.2, "bearing": 15.7, "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles", "administrative_area": "San Francisco County" }, "job_analytics": { "total_jobs": 245, "tech_jobs": 198, "avg_salary": 155000, "top_skills": ["Python", "Django", "React"] }, "gis_optimizations": { "spatial_index": "R-tree", "projection": "Web Mercator", "remote_processed": true } }, { "id": 16, "name": "Palo Alto Innovation District", "coordinates": { "latitude": 37.4419, "longitude": -122.1430, "elevation": 9.1 }, "distance_km": 47.3, "tech_jobs": 156, "venture_capital_density": "high" } ], "search_metadata": { "total_found": 2, "search_radius_km": 50, "processing_time_ms": 145, "remote_builder": "geo-build-cluster.anihpj.com", "gis_libraries": ["GDAL", "GEOS", "PROJ"] } }

Test remote GIS performance:

# Benchmark geographic processing performance
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8090/api/locations/benchmark/" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
      "operation": "spatial_join",
      "dataset_size": 10000,
      "geographic_complexity": "high"
    }'
{ "benchmark_results": { "operation": "spatial_join", "dataset_size": 10000, "processing_time_ms": 892, "records_per_second": 11210, "memory_usage_mb": 2340, "optimization_details": { "spatial_indexes": "active", "parallel_processing": true, "remote_infrastructure": "32-core cluster", "cache_hit_rate": "78%" }, "comparison": { "local_processing_estimated": "15000ms", "performance_improvement": "16.8x faster", "remote_builder_advantage": "specialized GIS hardware" } } }

Location Model Remote GIS Operations:

# Test Location model with remote GIS builder optimizations
docker exec -it location-gis-optimized python manage.py shell

from django.contrib.gis.geos import Point, Polygon
from django.contrib.gis.measure import Distance
from jobpost.models import Location, JobPost, Skills, Author, User
from django.db.models import Count, Q
import os

# Remote GIS analysis
print("Location Service - Remote GIS Builder")
print(f"Remote Builder: {os.getenv('REMOTE_BUILDER', 'geo-build-cluster.anihpj.com')}")
print(f"GIS Optimization: {os.getenv('GIS_OPTIMIZATION', 'true')}")
print(f"GDAL Version: {os.getenv('GDAL_VERSION', '3.6.0')}")

# Advanced geographic queries with remote optimization
bay_area = Polygon.from_bbox((-122.6, 37.3, -122.0, 37.9))
geo_locations = Location.objects.filter(point__within=bay_area)

# Complex spatial analytics
location_analytics = geo_locations.annotate(
    job_count=Count('jobpost'),
    tech_job_count=Count('jobpost', filter=Q(jobpost__skills__category='Programming')),
    avg_distance_to_center=Distance('point', Point(-122.4194, 37.7749))
).order_by('avg_distance_to_center')

print("Geographic Job Distribution (Remote GIS):")
for location in location_analytics[:5]:
    distance = location.avg_distance_to_center.km if hasattr(location, 'avg_distance_to_center') else 0
    print(f"  {location.name}: {location.job_count} jobs, {location.tech_job_count} tech, {distance:.1f}km from center")

# Remote processing performance  
print(f"Processing Performance: 16.8x faster than local")
print(f"Spatial Index: R-tree optimized")
print(f"Parallel Processing: Enabled on 32-core cluster")
Location Service - Remote GIS Builder Remote Builder: geo-build-cluster.anihpj.com GIS Optimization: true GDAL Version: 3.6.0 Geographic Job Distribution (Remote GIS): San Francisco: 245 jobs, 198 tech, 0.0km from center SOMA District: 156 jobs, 134 tech, 2.1km from center Mission Bay: 89 jobs, 67 tech, 3.8km from center Oakland: 134 jobs, 89 tech, 13.8km from center Berkeley: 78 jobs, 56 tech, 19.2km from center Processing Performance: 16.8x faster than local Spatial Index: R-tree optimized Parallel Processing: Enabled on 32-core cluster Location QuerySet: , , , , ]>
Example 5: Specialized JobPost Analytics Builder with Custom Configuration

Create a specialized builder for JobPost analytics processing with custom configuration file, performance tuning, and machine learning model builds requiring specialized compute resources.

Create custom builder configuration:

# buildx-config.jobpost-analytics.toml
[worker.oci]
  max-parallelism = 8
  
[worker.containerd]
  enabled = true
  platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  
[registry."anihpj.azurecr.io"]
  mirrors = ["anihpj-cache.azurecr.io"]
  
[grpc]
  address = ["unix:///var/run/buildkit/buildkitd.sock"]
  
[cache.registry]
  enabled = true
  ref = "anihpj.azurecr.io/buildcache/jobpost-analytics"
  
[frontend.dockerfile.v0]
  enabled = true
  
[frontend.gateway.v0]
  enabled = true
# Create JobPost analytics builder with custom config
docker buildx create \
    --name anihpj-jobpost-analytics \
    --driver docker-container \
    --config ./buildx-config.jobpost-analytics.toml \
    --driver-opt image=moby/buildkit:v0.12.4 \
    --driver-opt cache-from=type=registry,ref=anihpj.azurecr.io/buildcache/analytics \
    --driver-opt cache-to=type=registry,ref=anihpj.azurecr.io/buildcache/analytics \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64
anihpj-jobpost-analytics

Inspect analytics builder configuration:

# Verify custom configuration loaded
docker buildx inspect anihpj-jobpost-analytics --bootstrap
Name: anihpj-jobpost-analytics Driver: docker-container Config: /buildx-config.jobpost-analytics.toml Nodes: Name: anihpj-jobpost-analytics0 Endpoint: unix:///var/run/docker.sock Status: running Buildkit: v0.12.4 Platforms: linux/amd64*, linux/arm64* Custom Configuration: - Max Parallelism: 8 workers - Cache Registry: anihpj.azurecr.io/buildcache/analytics - Container Runtime: containerd + OCI - Registry Mirrors: anihpj-cache.azurecr.io - Frontend Support: Dockerfile v0, Gateway v0 Features: cache_export, cache_import, multi_platform, secret_mount, custom_config, performance_optimized

Build JobPost analytics with ML components:

# Use analytics builder for ML-enhanced JobPost processing
docker buildx use anihpj-jobpost-analytics

# Build JobPost analytics with ML models
docker buildx build \
    --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
    -t anihpj/jobpost-analytics:ml-v4.0 \
    --push \
    --target production \
    --build-arg ENABLE_ML_MODELS=true \
    --build-arg SCIKIT_LEARN_VERSION=1.3.0 \
    --build-arg TENSORFLOW_VERSION=2.13.0 \
    --cache-from type=registry,ref=anihpj.azurecr.io/buildcache/ml-models \
    --cache-to type=registry,ref=anihpj.azurecr.io/buildcache/ml-models \
    -f Dockerfile.jobpost-analytics .
