Development Environment Reset

Syntax: docker compose down -v --rmi all

Parameters: -v (remove volumes), --rmi all (remove all images)

Complete Django development environment cleanup including database data and built images for fresh environment setup.

Production Maintenance Shutdown

Syntax: docker compose down --timeout 30

Parameters: --timeout (integer) - Grace period in seconds for Django application shutdown

Graceful Django application shutdown allowing current requests to complete before stopping services.

Return Value

Returns shutdown status and cleanup progress during container stopping and resource removal. Shows Django application shutdown logs and cleanup completion status.

  • Success (0): All services stopped and resources cleaned successfully
  • Error (1): Service shutdown failure or resource cleanup issues
  • Warning: Volumes preserved when not explicitly removed

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Development Environment Shutdown (Preserving Database)

Shut down Django Skills management development environment while preserving database data and project progress.

# Stop skills development environment (preserve data)
docker compose down

# Check cleanup status
docker compose ps -a

# Verify volume preservation
docker volume ls | grep anihpj

Django Models Context & Data Preservation:

# Skills model for development environment
class Skills(models.Model):
    SKILL_TYPES = [
        ('technical', 'Technical'),
        ('soft', 'Soft Skill'),
        ('language', 'Language'),
        ('certification', 'Certification'),
        ('tool', 'Tool/Software')
    ]
    
    PROFICIENCY_LEVELS = [
        ('beginner', 'Beginner'),
        ('intermediate', 'Intermediate'),
        ('advanced', 'Advanced'),
        ('expert', 'Expert'),
        ('master', 'Master')
    ]
    
    skill_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True, db_index=True)
    skill_name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True, db_index=True)
    skill_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=SKILL_TYPES, default='technical')
    description = models.TextField()
    proficiency_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=PROFICIENCY_LEVELS, default='intermediate')
    years_experience = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=1)
    is_verified = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    industry_demand = models.CharField(max_length=20, 
                                     choices=[('low', 'Low'), ('medium', 'Medium'), ('high', 'High')], 
                                     default='medium')
    created_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['skill_type', 'proficiency_level']),
            models.Index(fields=['skill_name', 'is_verified']),
            models.Index(fields=['industry_demand', 'years_experience'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.skill_name} ({self.proficiency_level})"

# Skills development data that will be preserved
skills_data = [
    Skills(skill_name="Python Django", skill_type="technical", proficiency_level="expert", years_experience=5, is_verified=True, industry_demand="high"),
    Skills(skill_name="Docker Containerization", skill_type="technical", proficiency_level="advanced", years_experience=3, is_verified=True, industry_demand="high"),
    Skills(skill_name="Project Management", skill_type="soft", proficiency_level="intermediate", years_experience=4, is_verified=False, industry_demand="medium"),
    Skills(skill_name="PostgreSQL Database", skill_type="technical", proficiency_level="advanced", years_experience=4, is_verified=True, industry_demand="high"),
    Skills(skill_name="JavaScript React", skill_type="technical", proficiency_level="intermediate", years_experience=2, is_verified=False, industry_demand="high")
]

# Print Skills data preservation status
print("Skills Development Environment - Data Preservation Status:")
print(f"Total skills in development: {len(skills_data)}")
verified_skills = sum(1 for skill in skills_data if skill.is_verified)
print(f"Verified skills: {verified_skills}/{len(skills_data)}")

technical_skills = [s for s in skills_data if s.skill_type == 'technical']
high_demand_skills = [s for s in skills_data if s.industry_demand == 'high']
print(f"Technical skills: {len(technical_skills)}, High demand skills: {len(high_demand_skills)}")

for skill in skills_data:
    verification_status = "✓ Verified" if skill.is_verified else "⚠ Pending"
    print(f"  - {skill.skill_name}: {skill.proficiency_level.title()} ({skill.years_experience}yr exp) {verification_status}")
    print(f"    Type: {skill.skill_type.title()}, Demand: {skill.industry_demand.title()}")
    
print("\n⚠ Data Preservation: Skills database data preserved in Docker volumes")
print("   Use 'docker compose up' to restore development environment with data intact")
Stopping anihpj-web-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-worker-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-db-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-redis-1 ... done Removing anihpj-web-1 ... done Removing anihpj-worker-1 ... done Removing anihpj-db-1 ... done Removing anihpj-redis-1 ... done Removing network anihpj_default Skills Development Environment - Data Preservation Status: Total skills in development: 5 Verified skills: 3/5 Technical skills: 4, High demand skills: 4 - Python Django: Expert (5yr exp) ✓ Verified Type: Technical, Demand: High - Docker Containerization: Advanced (3yr exp) ✓ Verified Type: Technical, Demand: High - Project Management: Intermediate (4yr exp) ⚠ Pending Type: Soft, Demand: Medium - PostgreSQL Database: Advanced (4yr exp) ✓ Verified Type: Technical, Demand: High - JavaScript React: Intermediate (2yr exp) ⚠ Pending Type: Technical, Demand: High ⚠ Data Preservation: Skills database data preserved in Docker volumes Use 'docker compose up' to restore development environment with data intact
Example 2: Author Profile Service Maintenance Shutdown (Graceful Production Shutdown)

Gracefully shut down Django Author profile production service allowing active sessions to complete before maintenance window.

# Graceful production shutdown with timeout
docker compose down --timeout 60

# Verify graceful shutdown completion
docker compose ps -a

# Check service logs for clean shutdown
docker compose logs --tail 20 web

Django Models Context & Production Shutdown:

# Author model for production service shutdown
class Author(models.Model):
    EXPERTISE_LEVELS = [
        ('junior', 'Junior Developer (1-3 years)'),
        ('mid', 'Mid-Level Developer (3-6 years)'),
        ('senior', 'Senior Developer (6-10 years)'),
        ('lead', 'Lead Developer (10-15 years)'),
        ('principal', 'Principal Developer (15+ years)')
    ]
    
    AVAILABILITY_STATUS = [
        ('available', 'Available for Work'),
        ('busy', 'Currently Busy'),
        ('on_vacation', 'On Vacation'),
        ('in_interview', 'In Interview Process'),
        ('unavailable', 'Unavailable')
    ]
    
    author_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    username = models.CharField(max_length=50, unique=True, db_index=True)
    full_name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    email = models.EmailField(unique=True)
    bio = models.TextField(max_length=1000)
    expertise_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=EXPERTISE_LEVELS)
    specialization = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    years_experience = models.PositiveIntegerField()
    hourly_rate = models.DecimalField(max_digits=8, decimal_places=2)
    availability_status = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=AVAILABILITY_STATUS, default='available')
    current_projects = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    total_completed_projects = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    client_rating = models.DecimalField(max_digits=3, decimal_places=2, default=5.0)
    location = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    timezone = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    last_active = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['expertise_level', 'availability_status']),
            models.Index(fields=['specialization', 'hourly_rate']),
            models.Index(fields=['client_rating', 'total_completed_projects'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.full_name} ({self.expertise_level})"

# Author profile production data
production_authors = [
    Author(username="alex_senior", full_name="Alex Rodriguez", email="alex@anihpj.com",
           expertise_level="senior", specialization="Full-Stack Development", years_experience=8,
           hourly_rate=125.00, availability_status="busy", current_projects=2, 
           total_completed_projects=47, client_rating=4.9, location="Austin, TX", timezone="CST"),
    Author(username="maria_lead", full_name="Maria Santos", email="maria@anihpj.com",
           expertise_level="lead", specialization="DevOps & Cloud Architecture", years_experience=12,
           hourly_rate=160.00, availability_status="available", current_projects=0,
           total_completed_projects=73, client_rating=4.95, location="San Francisco, CA", timezone="PST"),
    Author(username="david_principal", full_name="David Kim", email="david@anihpj.com",
           expertise_level="principal", specialization="AI/ML Engineering", years_experience=16,
           hourly_rate=200.00, availability_status="in_interview", current_projects=1,
           total_completed_projects=89, client_rating=4.98, location="Seattle, WA", timezone="PST"),
    Author(username="sarah_mid", full_name="Sarah Johnson", email="sarah@anihpj.com",
           expertise_level="mid", specialization="Frontend Development", years_experience=5,
           hourly_rate=95.00, availability_status="available", current_projects=1,
           total_completed_projects=28, client_rating=4.8, location="Remote", timezone="EST")
]

# Print Author service production shutdown status
print("Author Profile Production Service - Graceful Shutdown Status:")
print(f"Total authors in production: {len(production_authors)}")
active_authors = [a for a in production_authors if a.availability_status in ['available', 'busy']]
currently_busy = [a for a in production_authors if a.availability_status == 'busy']

print(f"Currently active authors: {len(active_authors)}/{len(production_authors)}")
print(f"Authors with ongoing projects: {len(currently_busy)}")

total_active_projects = sum(a.current_projects for a in production_authors)
avg_rating = sum(float(a.client_rating) for a in production_authors) / len(production_authors)
print(f"Total active projects being handled: {total_active_projects}")
print(f"Service average rating: {avg_rating:.2f}/5.0")

print("\n📝 Production Shutdown Summary:")
for author in production_authors:
    status_icon = "🔴" if author.availability_status == 'busy' else "🏫" if author.availability_status == 'in_interview' else "📢" if author.availability_status == 'on_vacation' else "🟢"
    project_info = f", {author.current_projects} active" if author.current_projects > 0 else ", no active projects"
    
    print(f"  {status_icon} {author.full_name}: {author.expertise_level.title()} | {author.specialization}")
    print(f"     Status: {author.availability_status.replace('_', ' ').title()}{project_info}")
    print(f"     Rate: ${author.hourly_rate}/hr | Rating: {author.client_rating}/5.0 | {author.total_completed_projects} completed")
    print(f"     Location: {author.location} ({author.timezone})")

print(f"\n🗑️ Graceful Shutdown Complete: All author services stopped safely")
print(f"   {total_active_projects} active projects preserved, data integrity maintained")
Stopping anihpj-web-1 ... Gracefully shutting down Django workers... Completing active requests... Closing database connections... Stopping anihpj-web-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-worker-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-db-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-redis-1 ... done Removing anihpj-web-1 ... done Removing anihpj-worker-1 ... done Removing anihpj-db-1 ... done Removing anihpj-redis-1 ... done Removing network anihpj_default Author Profile Production Service - Graceful Shutdown Status: Total authors in production: 4 Currently active authors: 3/4 Authors with ongoing projects: 1 Total active projects being handled: 4 Service average rating: 4.91/5.0 📝 Production Shutdown Summary: 🔴 Alex Rodriguez: Senior | Full-Stack Development Status: Busy, 2 active Rate: $125.0/hr | Rating: 4.9/5.0 | 47 completed Location: Austin, TX (CST) 🟢 Maria Santos: Lead | DevOps & Cloud Architecture Status: Available, no active projects Rate: $160.0/hr | Rating: 4.95/5.0 | 73 completed Location: San Francisco, CA (PST) 🏫 David Kim: Principal | AI/ML Engineering Status: In Interview Process, 1 active Rate: $200.0/hr | Rating: 4.98/5.0 | 89 completed Location: Seattle, WA (PST) 🟢 Sarah Johnson: Mid | Frontend Development Status: Available, 1 active Rate: $95.0/hr | Rating: 4.8/5.0 | 28 completed Location: Remote (EST) 🗑️ Graceful Shutdown Complete: All author services stopped safely 4 active projects preserved, data integrity maintained
Example 3: Location Service Complete Reset (Development Environment Cleanup)

Completely reset Django Location service development environment including volumes and images for fresh start.

# Complete development environment reset
docker compose down -v --rmi all --remove-orphans

# Verify complete cleanup
docker system df
docker volume ls | grep anihpj
docker images | grep anihpj

# Check for any remaining containers
docker ps -a --filter name=anihpj

Django Models Context & Development Reset:

# Location model for development environment reset
class Location(models.Model):
    LOCATION_CATEGORIES = [
        ('megacity', 'Megacity (10M+ population)'),
        ('major_city', 'Major City (1M-10M)'),
        ('city', 'City (100K-1M)'),
        ('town', 'Town (10K-100K)'),
        ('small_town', 'Small Town (<10K)'),
        ('remote', 'Remote/Virtual'),
        ('multiple', 'Multiple Locations')
    ]
    
    ECONOMIC_TIERS = [
        ('tier_1', 'Tier 1 (High Cost, High Opportunity)'),
        ('tier_2', 'Tier 2 (Medium Cost, Good Opportunity)'),
        ('tier_3', 'Tier 3 (Low Cost, Emerging Market)'),
        ('special', 'Special Economic Zone')
    ]
    
    location_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, db_index=True)
    country = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    state_region = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    category = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=LOCATION_CATEGORIES)
    economic_tier = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=ECONOMIC_TIERS, default='tier_2')
    population = models.BigIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    cost_of_living_index = models.PositiveIntegerField(help_text="Relative to global average (100)")
    tech_job_market_score = models.PositiveIntegerField(help_text="Tech job availability (0-100)")
    average_tech_salary = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    remote_work_adoption = models.FloatField(help_text="Remote work percentage (0.0-1.0)")
    internet_speed_mbps = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    time_zone = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    languages_spoken = models.JSONField(default=list)
    tech_companies_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    startup_ecosystem_rank = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['category', 'economic_tier']),
            models.Index(fields=['tech_job_market_score', 'average_tech_salary']),
            models.Index(fields=['remote_work_adoption', 'cost_of_living_index'])
        ]
        unique_together = [['name', 'country', 'state_region']]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name}, {self.country}"

# Location development data to be reset
development_locations = [
    Location(name="San Francisco", country="United States", state_region="California", 
             category="major_city", economic_tier="tier_1", population=884363, 
             cost_of_living_index=185, tech_job_market_score=98, average_tech_salary=165000.00,
             remote_work_adoption=0.75, internet_speed_mbps=150, time_zone="PST",
             languages_spoken=["English", "Spanish", "Chinese"], tech_companies_count=2847, startup_ecosystem_rank=1),
    Location(name="Austin", country="United States", state_region="Texas", 
             category="major_city", economic_tier="tier_1", population=964254, 
             cost_of_living_index=105, tech_job_market_score=89, average_tech_salary=125000.00,
             remote_work_adoption=0.68, internet_speed_mbps=120, time_zone="CST",
             languages_spoken=["English", "Spanish"], tech_companies_count=1253, startup_ecosystem_rank=8),
    Location(name="Bangalore", country="India", state_region="Karnataka", 
             category="megacity", economic_tier="tier_2", population=12300000, 
             cost_of_living_index=35, tech_job_market_score=92, average_tech_salary=25000.00,
             remote_work_adoption=0.82, internet_speed_mbps=85, time_zone="IST",
             languages_spoken=["English", "Hindi", "Kannada"], tech_companies_count=3456, startup_ecosystem_rank=15),
    Location(name="Remote Global", country="Worldwide", category="remote", 
             economic_tier="special", tech_job_market_score=95, 
             remote_work_adoption=1.0, time_zone="UTC",
             languages_spoken=["English"], tech_companies_count=15000)
]

# Print Location service reset status
print("Location Service Development Environment - Reset Status:")
print(f"Total locations in development database: {len(development_locations)}")
tier_1_locations = [loc for loc in development_locations if loc.economic_tier == 'tier_1']
high_score_locations = [loc for loc in development_locations if loc.tech_job_market_score >= 90]

print(f"Tier 1 economic locations: {len(tier_1_locations)}/{len(development_locations)}")
print(f"High tech job market locations (90+): {len(high_score_locations)}/{len(development_locations)}")

avg_tech_jobs_score = sum(loc.tech_job_market_score for loc in development_locations) / len(development_locations)
avg_remote_adoption = sum(loc.remote_work_adoption for loc in development_locations) / len(development_locations)
print(f"Average tech job market score: {avg_tech_jobs_score:.1f}/100")
print(f"Average remote work adoption: {avg_remote_adoption:.1%}")

print("\n🗺️ Development Data Being Reset:")
for location in development_locations:
    tier_icon = "🌆" if location.economic_tier == 'tier_1' else "🌇" if location.economic_tier == 'tier_2' else "🏡"
    market_icon = "🔥" if location.tech_job_market_score >= 95 else "📈" if location.tech_job_market_score >= 85 else "📊"
    
    if location.category != 'remote':
        salary_info = f"Avg Salary: ${location.average_tech_salary:,.0f}" if location.average_tech_salary else "Salary: TBD"
        population_info = f"Population: {location.population:,}" if location.population else "Population: N/A"
        cost_info = f"Cost Index: {location.cost_of_living_index}"
    else:
        salary_info = "Global Remote Opportunities"
        population_info = "Distributed Workforce"
        cost_info = "Variable Costs"
    
    print(f"\n  {tier_icon} {location.name}: {location.category.replace('_', ' ').title()} {market_icon}")
    print(f"     {location.country}, {location.state_region or 'N/A'} | {location.economic_tier.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"     Tech Market Score: {location.tech_job_market_score}/100 | Remote Adoption: {location.remote_work_adoption:.1%}")
    print(f"     {population_info} | {cost_info}")
    print(f"     {salary_info} | {len(location.languages_spoken)} languages")
    print(f"     Tech Companies: {location.tech_companies_count} | Time Zone: {location.time_zone}")

print(f"\n⚠️ COMPLETE RESET: All location development data will be permanently deleted")
print(f"   Database volumes, container images, and cached data will be removed")
print(f"   Use 'docker compose up' to rebuild from scratch after reset")
Stopping anihpj-location-web-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-location-worker-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-location-db-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-location-redis-1 ... done Removing anihpj-location-web-1 ... done Removing anihpj-location-worker-1 ... done Removing anihpj-location-db-1 ... done Removing anihpj-location-redis-1 ... done Removing network anihpj-location_default Removing volume anihpj-location_postgres_data Removing volume anihpj-location_redis_data Removing image anihpj-location-web:latest Removing image anihpj-location-worker:latest Location Service Development Environment - Reset Status: Total locations in development database: 4 Tier 1 economic locations: 2/4 High tech job market locations (90+): 4/4 Average tech job market score: 93.5/100 Average remote work adoption: 81.2% 🗺️ Development Data Being Reset: 🌆 San Francisco: Major City 🔥 United States, California | Tier 1 Tech Market Score: 98/100 | Remote Adoption: 75.0% Population: 884,363 | Cost Index: 185 Avg Salary: $165,000 | 3 languages Tech Companies: 2847 | Time Zone: PST 🌆 Austin: Major City 📊 United States, Texas | Tier 1 Tech Market Score: 89/100 | Remote Adoption: 68.0% Population: 964,254 | Cost Index: 105 Avg Salary: $125,000 | 2 languages Tech Companies: 1253 | Time Zone: CST 🌇 Bangalore: Megacity 📈 India, Karnataka | Tier 2 Tech Market Score: 92/100 | Remote Adoption: 82.0% Population: 12,300,000 | Cost Index: 35 Avg Salary: $25,000 | 3 languages Tech Companies: 3456 | Time Zone: IST 🏡 Remote Global: Remote 🔥 Worldwide, N/A | Special Tech Market Score: 95/100 | Remote Adoption: 100.0% Distributed Workforce | Variable Costs Global Remote Opportunities | 1 languages Tech Companies: 15000 | Time Zone: UTC ⚠️ COMPLETE RESET: All location development data will be permanently deleted Database volumes, container images, and cached data will be removed Use 'docker compose up' to rebuild from scratch after reset
Example 4: User Service Selective Shutdown (Maintenance Mode with Data Protection)

Shut down specific Django User service components while maintaining database and critical services for maintenance operations.

# Selective service shutdown (preserve database)
docker compose down web worker nginx

# Verify database and cache remain running
docker compose ps

# Check preserved data integrity
docker compose exec db pg_isready -U anihpj_user

Django Models Context & Selective Maintenance:

# Enhanced User model for selective service maintenance
from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractUser
from django.db import models
import uuid

class User(AbstractUser):
    ACCOUNT_STATUSES = [
        ('active', 'Active Account'),
        ('suspended', 'Temporarily Suspended'),
        ('pending', 'Pending Verification'),
        ('inactive', 'Inactive'),
        ('maintenance', 'Under Maintenance')
    ]
    
    SUBSCRIPTION_TIERS = [
        ('free', 'Free Tier'),
        ('basic', 'Basic Plan'),
        ('premium', 'Premium Plan'),
        ('enterprise', 'Enterprise'),
        ('admin', 'Admin Access')
    ]
    
    user_uuid = models.UUIDField(default=uuid.uuid4, unique=True, editable=False)
    account_status = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=ACCOUNT_STATUSES, default='active')
    subscription_tier = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=SUBSCRIPTION_TIERS, default='free')
    profile_image = models.URLField(blank=True)
    phone_verified = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    email_verified = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    two_factor_auth = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    last_activity = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    login_streak = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    total_logins = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    failed_attempts = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    account_locked_until = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    preferences = models.JSONField(default=dict)
    notification_settings = models.JSONField(default=dict)
    api_usage_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    monthly_api_limit = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=1000)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['account_status', 'subscription_tier']),
            models.Index(fields=['email_verified', 'phone_verified']),
            models.Index(fields=['last_activity', 'total_logins']),
            models.Index(fields=['subscription_tier', 'account_status'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.username} ({self.subscription_tier})"

# User service maintenance data
maintenance_users = [
    User(username="john_premium", email="john@premium.com", first_name="John", last_name="Smith",
         account_status="active", subscription_tier="premium", email_verified=True, phone_verified=True,
         two_factor_auth=True, login_streak=45, total_logins=342, api_usage_count=8765, monthly_api_limit=10000,
         preferences={"theme": "dark", "notifications": True}, notification_settings={"email": True, "sms": False}),
    User(username="sarah_enterprise", email="sarah@enterprise.com", first_name="Sarah", last_name="Connor",
         account_status="active", subscription_tier="enterprise", email_verified=True, phone_verified=True,
         two_factor_auth=True, login_streak=123, total_logins=1547, api_usage_count=45230, monthly_api_limit=100000,
         preferences={"theme": "light", "dashboard": "advanced"}, notification_settings={"email": True, "sms": True}),
    User(username="mike_basic", email="mike@basic.com", first_name="Mike", last_name="Johnson",
         account_status="active", subscription_tier="basic", email_verified=True, phone_verified=False,
         two_factor_auth=False, login_streak=12, total_logins=89, api_usage_count=234, monthly_api_limit=2500,
         preferences={"theme": "auto"}, notification_settings={"email": True}),
    User(username="admin_system", email="admin@anihpj.com", first_name="System", last_name="Admin",
         account_status="maintenance", subscription_tier="admin", email_verified=True, phone_verified=True,
         two_factor_auth=True, login_streak=365, total_logins=5432, is_staff=True, is_superuser=True,
         preferences={"admin_mode": True}, notification_settings={"critical_alerts": True})
]

# Print User service maintenance status
print("User Service - Selective Maintenance Shutdown Status:")
print(f"Total users affected by maintenance: {len(maintenance_users)}")
active_users = [u for u in maintenance_users if u.account_status == 'active']
premium_users = [u for u in maintenance_users if u.subscription_tier in ['premium', 'enterprise']]

print(f"Active user accounts: {len(active_users)}/{len(maintenance_users)}")
print(f"Premium/Enterprise users: {len(premium_users)}")

total_api_usage = sum(u.api_usage_count for u in maintenance_users)
avg_login_streak = sum(u.login_streak for u in maintenance_users) / len(maintenance_users)
print(f"Total API usage this month: {total_api_usage:,} requests")
print(f"Average login streak: {avg_login_streak:.1f} days")

print("\n👥 Users During Maintenance Window:")
for user in maintenance_users:
    tier_icon = "🎯" if user.subscription_tier == 'enterprise' else "💸" if user.subscription_tier == 'premium' else "💵" if user.subscription_tier == 'basic' else "👑"
    security_score = "High" if user.two_factor_auth and user.email_verified and user.phone_verified else "Medium" if user.email_verified else "Basic"
    status_icon = "🔧" if user.account_status == 'maintenance' else "🟢"
    
    api_usage_pct = (user.api_usage_count / user.monthly_api_limit * 100) if user.monthly_api_limit else 0
    
    print(f"\n  {status_icon} {user.username}: {user.subscription_tier.title()} {tier_icon}")
    print(f"     {user.first_name} {user.last_name} | {user.email}")
    print(f"     Status: {user.account_status.title()} | Security: {security_score}")
    print(f"     Login Streak: {user.login_streak} days | Total Logins: {user.total_logins:,}")
    print(f"     API Usage: {user.api_usage_count:,}/{user.monthly_api_limit:,} ({api_usage_pct:.1f}%)")
    print(f"     Notifications: {len([k for k, v in user.notification_settings.items() if v])} active channels")

print(f"\n🗑️ Selective Shutdown Complete: Web and worker services stopped for maintenance")
print(f"   Database and cache services preserved to maintain user data integrity")
print(f"   {len(active_users)} active users can resume normal activity after maintenance")
Stopping anihpj-user-web-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-user-worker-1 ... done Stopping anihpj-user-nginx-1 ... done Remaining running: anihpj-user-db-1 Up 2 hours anihpj-user-redis-1 Up 2 hours User Service - Selective Maintenance Shutdown Status: Total users affected by maintenance: 4 Active user accounts: 3/4 Premium/Enterprise users: 2 Total API usage this month: 54,229 requests Average login streak: 136.2 days 👥 Users During Maintenance Window: 🟢 john_premium: Premium 💸 John Smith | john@premium.com Status: Active | Security: High Login Streak: 45 days | Total Logins: 342 API Usage: 8,765/10,000 (87.7%) Notifications: 1 active channels 🟢 sarah_enterprise: Enterprise 🎯 Sarah Connor | sarah@enterprise.com Status: Active | Security: High Login Streak: 123 days | Total Logins: 1,547 API Usage: 45,230/100,000 (45.2%) Notifications: 2 active channels 🟢 mike_basic: Basic 💵 Mike Johnson | mike@basic.com Status: Active | Security: Medium Login Streak: 12 days | Total Logins: 89 API Usage: 234/2,500 (9.4%) Notifications: 1 active channels 🔧 admin_system: Admin 👑 System Admin | admin@anihpj.com Status: Maintenance | Security: High Login Streak: 365 days | Total Logins: 5,432 API Usage: 0/0 (0.0%) Notifications: 1 active channels 🗑️ Selective Shutdown Complete: Web and worker services stopped for maintenance Database and cache services preserved to maintain user data integrity 3 active users can resume normal activity after maintenance
Example 5: JobPost Service Emergency Shutdown (Critical Issue Response)

Emergency shutdown of Django JobPost service stack with immediate termination for critical security incident response.

# Emergency shutdown with immediate termination
docker compose down --timeout 0 --remove-orphans

# Force cleanup of any remaining containers
docker container prune -f

# Check for security incident artifacts
docker system events --filter type=container --since 1h

Django Models Context & Emergency Response:

# JobPost model for emergency shutdown scenario
from django.db import models
from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

class JobPost(models.Model):
    URGENCY_LEVELS = [
        ('low', 'Standard Posting'),
        ('medium', 'Urgent Hiring'),
        ('high', 'Critical Position'),
        ('emergency', 'Emergency Replacement')
    ]
    
    SECURITY_CLEARANCE = [
        ('public', 'Public Access'),
        ('confidential', 'Confidential'),
        ('secret', 'Secret'),
        ('top_secret', 'Top Secret'),
        ('restricted', 'Restricted Access')
    ]
    
    job_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    company = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    description = models.TextField()
    urgency_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=URGENCY_LEVELS, default='medium')
    security_clearance = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=SECURITY_CLEARANCE, default='public')
    salary_range = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    remote_eligible = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    security_requirements = models.JSONField(default=list)
    compliance_certifications = models.JSONField(default=list)
    background_check_required = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    emergency_contact = models.EmailField(blank=True)
    incident_response_role = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    on_call_requirements = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    threat_level_clearance = models.CharField(max_length=50, blank=True)
    access_control_level = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=1)
    created_by = models.ForeignKey(get_user_model(), on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    applications_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    views_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    priority_score = models.FloatField(default=50.0)
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    expires_at = models.DateTimeField()
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['urgency_level', 'security_clearance']),
            models.Index(fields=['is_active', 'priority_score']),
            models.Index(fields=['incident_response_role', 'on_call_requirements'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} at {self.company} ({self.urgency_level})"

# Critical job posts requiring emergency shutdown
critical_jobposts = [
    JobPost(title="Senior Security Engineer", company="CyberDefense Corp", urgency_level="emergency",
            security_clearance="secret", salary_range="$150,000-$200,000", remote_eligible=False,
            security_requirements=["CISSP", "Security+", "Top Secret Clearance"],
            compliance_certifications=["SOX", "HIPAA", "PCI-DSS"], background_check_required=True,
            incident_response_role=True, on_call_requirements=True, threat_level_clearance="Level 5",
            access_control_level=5, applications_count=23, views_count=156, priority_score=98.5,
            expires_at=datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)),
    JobPost(title="Incident Response Specialist", company="SecOps Solutions", urgency_level="high",
            security_clearance="confidential", salary_range="$120,000-$160,000", remote_eligible=True,
            security_requirements=["GCIH", "GCFA", "Security Clearance"],
            compliance_certifications=["ISO 27001", "NIST Framework"], background_check_required=True,
            incident_response_role=True, on_call_requirements=True, threat_level_clearance="Level 4",
            access_control_level=4, applications_count=31, views_count=287, priority_score=94.2,
            expires_at=datetime.now() + timedelta(days=14)),
    JobPost(title="DevSecOps Lead", company="TechSecure Inc", urgency_level="high",
            security_clearance="public", salary_range="$140,000-$180,000", remote_eligible=True,
            security_requirements=["Docker Security", "Kubernetes Security", "OWASP"],
            compliance_certifications=["SOC 2", "FedRAMP"], background_check_required=False,
            incident_response_role=False, on_call_requirements=True, access_control_level=3,
            applications_count=45, views_count=389, priority_score=87.8,
            expires_at=datetime.now() + timedelta(days=21)),
    JobPost(title="Critical Infrastructure Engineer", company="Infrastructure Defense", urgency_level="emergency",
            security_clearance="top_secret", salary_range="$180,000-$250,000", remote_eligible=False,
            security_requirements=["TS/SCI Clearance", "Critical Infrastructure", "ICS Security"],
            compliance_certifications=["NERC CIP", "FISMA"], background_check_required=True,
            incident_response_role=True, on_call_requirements=True, threat_level_clearance="Level 6",
            access_control_level=6, applications_count=8, views_count=67, priority_score=99.1,
            expires_at=datetime.now() + timedelta(days=3))
]

# Print JobPost emergency shutdown status
print("JobPost Security Service - Emergency Shutdown Status:")
print(f"Total critical job posts in system: {len(critical_jobposts)}")
emergency_posts = [jp for jp in critical_jobposts if jp.urgency_level == 'emergency']
secret_clearance_posts = [jp for jp in critical_jobposts if jp.security_clearance in ['secret', 'top_secret']]

print(f"Emergency urgency posts: {len(emergency_posts)}/{len(critical_jobposts)}")
print(f"Secret/Top Secret clearance posts: {len(secret_clearance_posts)}")

total_applications = sum(jp.applications_count for jp in critical_jobposts)
avg_priority_score = sum(jp.priority_score for jp in critical_jobposts) / len(critical_jobposts)
print(f"Total applications at risk: {total_applications}")
print(f"Average priority score: {avg_priority_score:.1f}/100")

print("\n🆘 Critical Job Posts Affected by Emergency Shutdown:")
for jobpost in critical_jobposts:
    urgency_icon = "🆘" if jobpost.urgency_level == 'emergency' else "🔴" if jobpost.urgency_level == 'high' else "🟡"
    clearance_icon = "🔒" if jobpost.security_clearance in ['secret', 'top_secret'] else "🔐" if jobpost.security_clearance == 'confidential' else "🔓"
    incident_badge = " 🚨 IR" if jobpost.incident_response_role else ""
    
    days_to_expire = (jobpost.expires_at - datetime.now()).days
    expire_urgency = "⚠️" if days_to_expire <= 7 else "📅"
    
    print(f"\n  {urgency_icon} {jobpost.title} at {jobpost.company}{incident_badge}")
    print(f"     Urgency: {jobpost.urgency_level.title()} | Clearance: {jobpost.security_clearance.replace('_', ' ').title()} {clearance_icon}")
    print(f"     Salary: {jobpost.salary_range} | Remote: {'Yes' if jobpost.remote_eligible else 'No'}")
    print(f"     Priority Score: {jobpost.priority_score}/100 | Access Level: {jobpost.access_control_level}")
    print(f"     Applications: {jobpost.applications_count} | Views: {jobpost.views_count}")
    print(f"     Security Reqs: {len(jobpost.security_requirements)} | Compliance: {len(jobpost.compliance_certifications)}")
    print(f"     {expire_urgency} Expires in {days_to_expire} days | Background Check: {'Required' if jobpost.background_check_required else 'Optional'}")

print(f"\n🆘 EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN: JobPost service terminated immediately due to security incident")
print(f"   {total_applications} job applications and {len(critical_jobposts)} critical postings secured")
print(f"   All security-sensitive data isolated pending investigation")
Stopping anihpj-jobpost-web-1 ... killed Stopping anihpj-jobpost-worker-1 ... killed Stopping anihpj-jobpost-db-1 ... killed Stopping anihpj-jobpost-redis-1 ... killed Stopping anihpj-jobpost-nginx-1 ... killed Removing anihpj-jobpost-web-1 ... done Removing anihpj-jobpost-worker-1 ... done Removing anihpj-jobpost-db-1 ... done Removing anihpj-jobpost-redis-1 ... done Removing anihpj-jobpost-nginx-1 ... done Removing network anihpj-jobpost_default Removing orphaned containers... JobPost Security Service - Emergency Shutdown Status: Total critical job posts in system: 4 Emergency urgency posts: 2/4 Secret/Top Secret clearance posts: 2 Total applications at risk: 107 Average priority score: 94.9/100 🆘 Critical Job Posts Affected by Emergency Shutdown: 🆘 Senior Security Engineer at CyberDefense Corp 🚨 IR Urgency: Emergency | Clearance: Secret 🔒 Salary: $150,000-$200,000 | Remote: No Priority Score: 98.5/100 | Access Level: 5 Applications: 23 | Views: 156 Security Reqs: 3 | Compliance: 3 ⚠️ Expires in 7 days | Background Check: Required 🔴 Incident Response Specialist at SecOps Solutions 🚨 IR Urgency: High | Clearance: Confidential 🔐 Salary: $120,000-$160,000 | Remote: Yes Priority Score: 94.2/100 | Access Level: 4 Applications: 31 | Views: 287 Security Reqs: 3 | Compliance: 2 📅 Expires in 14 days | Background Check: Required 🔴 DevSecOps Lead at TechSecure Inc Urgency: High | Clearance: Public 🔓 Salary: $140,000-$180,000 | Remote: Yes Priority Score: 87.8/100 | Access Level: 3 Applications: 45 | Views: 389 Security Reqs: 3 | Compliance: 2 📅 Expires in 21 days | Background Check: Optional 🆘 Critical Infrastructure Engineer at Infrastructure Defense 🚨 IR Urgency: Emergency | Clearance: Top Secret 🔒 Salary: $180,000-$250,000 | Remote: No Priority Score: 99.1/100 | Access Level: 6 Applications: 8 | Views: 67 Security Reqs: 3 | Compliance: 2 ⚠️ Expires in 3 days | Background Check: Required 🆘 EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN: JobPost service terminated immediately due to security incident 107 job applications and 4 critical postings secured All security-sensitive data isolated pending investigation

docker compose build

Official Docs: docker compose build | Docker CLI: docker compose build

Build or rebuild services defined in the compose file with custom images.

Full Description

The docker compose build command builds or rebuilds Docker images for services that have a build configuration in your compose file. For your anihpj Django project, this command compiles your Django application code, installs Python dependencies, configures environment settings, and creates optimized images ready for container deployment.

This command is essential for Django development workflows when you modify application code, update requirements.txt dependencies, change Dockerfile configurations, or need to incorporate new features into your anihpj jobpost application. It ensures your container images reflect the latest code changes and are ready for testing or production deployment.

Syntax

# Build all services with build configuration
docker compose build

# Build specific service
docker compose build web

# Build with no cache (fresh build)
docker compose build --no-cache

# Build with build arguments
docker compose build --build-arg ENV=production

# Build in parallel
docker compose build --parallel

# Build with progress output
docker compose build --progress plain

# Build specific services
docker compose build web worker

Parameters Description

  • --no-cache: Build without using cache from previous builds. Ensures Django application gets completely fresh build with latest dependencies.
  • --build-arg: Set build-time variables. Pass environment configurations, API keys, or build settings to Django application during image creation.
  • --parallel: Build multiple services simultaneously. Speeds up Django multi-service builds when services have independent build processes.
  • --progress: Set progress output format (auto, plain, tty). Choose output format for monitoring Django application build progress.
  • --pull: Always pull newer base images before building. Ensures Django application uses latest security updates from base Python images.

Attributes

  • Build Context: Local directory containing Dockerfile and source code. Includes Django application files, requirements, and static assets.
  • Layer Caching: Optimized build performance through Docker layer caching. Speeds up Django development by reusing unchanged code layers.
  • Dependency Management: Automatic Python package installation and virtual environment setup for Django application requirements.
  • Multi-stage Builds: Support for optimized production builds with separate development and production stages for Django applications.
  • Build Arguments: Runtime configuration through build-time variables for Django environment customization.

Methods

Production Build Optimization

Syntax: docker compose build --no-cache --build-arg ENV=production

Parameters: ENV (string) - Django environment setting for production optimization

Create optimized Django production images with clean builds and production environment configurations.

Development Hot Reload Setup

Syntax: docker compose build --build-arg DEBUG=True web

Parameters: DEBUG (boolean) - Django debug mode for development builds

Build Django development images with debug mode enabled and development tools included.

Return Value

Returns build status and progress during image creation. Shows Django application build logs, dependency installation progress, and any build errors or warnings.

  • Success (0): All specified services built successfully
  • Error (1): Build failure due to code issues or dependency problems
  • Error (125): Invalid build configuration or missing Dockerfile

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Author Profile Service Build (Django Application with Dependencies)

Build Django Author profile service with Python dependencies, static assets, and database configurations for anihpj project.

# Build author profile service
docker compose build web --no-cache

# Check build completion
docker image ls anihpj*

# Verify build with quick test
docker compose run --rm web python manage.py check

Django Models Context & Build Configuration:

# Author model for profile service build
class Author(models.Model):
    EXPERTISE_LEVELS = [
        ('junior', 'Junior'),
        ('mid', 'Mid-Level'),
        ('senior', 'Senior'),
        ('lead', 'Lead'),
        ('principal', 'Principal')
    ]
    
    SPECIALIZATIONS = [
        ('frontend', 'Frontend Development'),
        ('backend', 'Backend Development'),
        ('fullstack', 'Full Stack'),
        ('devops', 'DevOps'),
        ('mobile', 'Mobile Development'),
        ('ai_ml', 'AI/Machine Learning'),
        ('data', 'Data Science'),
        ('security', 'Cybersecurity')
    ]
    
    author_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True, db_index=True)
    username = models.CharField(max_length=50, unique=True, db_index=True)
    first_name = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    last_name = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    email = models.EmailField(unique=True, db_index=True)
    bio = models.TextField(max_length=500, blank=True)
    expertise_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=EXPERTISE_LEVELS, default='junior')
    specialization = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=SPECIALIZATIONS, default='fullstack')
    years_experience = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=1)
    github_profile = models.URLField(blank=True)
    linkedin_profile = models.URLField(blank=True)
    portfolio_website = models.URLField(blank=True)
    hourly_rate = models.DecimalField(max_digits=6, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    is_available = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    location = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    timezone = models.CharField(max_length=50, default='UTC')
    created_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['expertise_level', 'specialization']),
            models.Index(fields=['is_available', 'hourly_rate']),
            models.Index(fields=['username', 'email'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.first_name} {self.last_name} ({self.expertise_level})"

# Author profile data for build testing
author_profiles = [
    Author(username="sarah_dev", first_name="Sarah", last_name="Chen", email="sarah@anihpj.com", 
           expertise_level="senior", specialization="fullstack", years_experience=7, 
           hourly_rate=95.00, is_available=True, location="San Francisco, CA"),
    Author(username="mike_backend", first_name="Mike", last_name="Rodriguez", email="mike@anihpj.com", 
           expertise_level="lead", specialization="backend", years_experience=10, 
           hourly_rate=120.00, is_available=True, location="Austin, TX"),
    Author(username="emma_frontend", first_name="Emma", last_name="Johnson", email="emma@anihpj.com", 
           expertise_level="mid", specialization="frontend", years_experience=4, 
           hourly_rate=75.00, is_available=False, location="Seattle, WA"),
    Author(username="alex_devops", first_name="Alex", last_name="Kumar", email="alex@anihpj.com", 
           expertise_level="senior", specialization="devops", years_experience=8, 
           hourly_rate=110.00, is_available=True, location="New York, NY")
]

# Print Author service build verification
print("Author Profile Service - Docker Build Verification:")
print(f"Total author profiles configured: {len(author_profiles)}")
available_authors = sum(1 for author in author_profiles if author.is_available)
print(f"Available authors: {available_authors}/{len(author_profiles)}")

expertise_distribution = {}
for author in author_profiles:
    expertise_distribution[author.expertise_level] = expertise_distribution.get(author.expertise_level, 0) + 1

print(f"Expertise distribution: {expertise_distribution}")
avg_hourly_rate = sum(float(author.hourly_rate or 0) for author in author_profiles) / len(author_profiles)
print(f"Average hourly rate: ${avg_hourly_rate:.2f}")

for author in author_profiles:
    availability_status = "🟢 Available" if author.is_available else "🔴 Busy"
    rate_display = f"${author.hourly_rate}/hr" if author.hourly_rate else "Rate TBD"
    print(f"  - {author.username}: {author.expertise_level.title()} {author.specialization.replace('_', '/').title()} Developer")
    print(f"    {author.first_name} {author.last_name} | {rate_display} | {availability_status}")
    print(f"    Location: {author.location}, Experience: {author.years_experience} years")

print("\n✅ Build Status: Author profile service built successfully with Django dependencies")
print("   Ready for deployment with profile management features")
Building web [+] Building 45.2s (15/15) FINISHED => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile => [internal] load .dockerignore => [internal] load metadata for python:3.11-slim => [1/10] FROM python:3.11-slim => [2/10] WORKDIR /app => [3/10] COPY requirements.txt . => [4/10] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt => [5/10] COPY . . => [6/10] RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput => [7/10] EXPOSE 8000 => exporting to image Successfully built anihpj-web:latest Author Profile Service - Docker Build Verification: Total author profiles configured: 4 Available authors: 3/4 Expertise distribution: {'senior': 2, 'lead': 1, 'mid': 1} Average hourly rate: $100.00 - sarah_dev: Senior Fullstack Developer Sarah Chen | $95.0/hr | 🟢 Available Location: San Francisco, CA, Experience: 7 years - mike_backend: Lead Backend Developer Mike Rodriguez | $120.0/hr | 🟢 Available Location: Austin, TX, Experience: 10 years - emma_frontend: Mid Frontend Developer Emma Johnson | $75.0/hr | 🔴 Busy Location: Seattle, WA, Experience: 4 years - alex_devops: Senior Devops Developer Alex Kumar | $110.0/hr | 🟢 Available Location: New York, NY, Experience: 8 years ✅ Build Status: Author profile service built successfully with Django dependencies Ready for deployment with profile management features
Example 2: Skills API Service Build (Multi-Stage Production Build)

Build optimized production Django Skills API service with multi-stage Dockerfile for reduced image size and enhanced security.

# Build production skills service with optimizations
docker compose build --build-arg ENV=production --target production skills-api

# Verify production build size
docker images anihpj-skills*

# Test production build
docker compose run --rm skills-api python manage.py check --deploy

Django Models Context & Production Configuration:

# Skills model for production API service
class Skills(models.Model):
    SKILL_CATEGORIES = [
        ('programming', 'Programming Languages'),
        ('framework', 'Web Frameworks'),
        ('database', 'Database Systems'),
        ('devops', 'DevOps & Infrastructure'),
        ('cloud', 'Cloud Platforms'),
        ('testing', 'Testing & QA'),
        ('design', 'UI/UX Design'),
        ('mobile', 'Mobile Development')
    ]
    
    PROFICIENCY_LEVELS = [
        ('novice', 'Novice (0-1 years)'),
        ('beginner', 'Beginner (1-2 years)'),
        ('intermediate', 'Intermediate (2-5 years)'),
        ('advanced', 'Advanced (5-8 years)'),
        ('expert', 'Expert (8+ years)')
    ]
    
    skill_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True, db_index=True)
    category = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=SKILL_CATEGORIES, db_index=True)
    level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=PROFICIENCY_LEVELS, default='intermediate')
    description = models.TextField(max_length=500)
    market_demand = models.CharField(
        max_length=20,
        choices=[('low', 'Low Demand'), ('medium', 'Medium Demand'), ('high', 'High Demand'), ('critical', 'Critical Need')],
        default='medium'
    )
    average_salary = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    learning_resources = models.JSONField(default=list)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['category', 'name']
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['category', 'market_demand']),
            models.Index(fields=['level', 'average_salary']),
            models.Index(fields=['name', 'category'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} - {self.level}"

# Skills production data for API testing
production_skills = [
    Skills(name="Python", category="programming", level="expert", market_demand="critical", average_salary=125000.00, 
           description="Advanced Python programming for web development and data science"),
    Skills(name="Django REST Framework", category="framework", level="expert", market_demand="high", average_salary=115000.00,
           description="Building robust APIs and web services with Django"),
    Skills(name="PostgreSQL", category="database", level="advanced", market_demand="high", average_salary=95000.00,
           description="Advanced PostgreSQL database design and optimization"),
    Skills(name="Docker", category="devops", level="advanced", market_demand="critical", average_salary=110000.00,
           description="Container orchestration and microservices deployment"),
    Skills(name="Kubernetes", category="devops", level="intermediate", market_demand="critical", average_salary=130000.00,
           description="Container orchestration and cluster management")
]

# Print Skills production build results
print("Skills API Production Service - Build Verification:")
print(f"Total skills configured for production API: {len(production_skills)}")
critical_skills = [s for s in production_skills if s.market_demand == 'critical']
high_salary_skills = [s for s in production_skills if s.average_salary and s.average_salary >= 110000]

print(f"Critical demand skills: {len(critical_skills)}/{len(production_skills)}")
print(f"High-paying skills (110k+): {len(high_salary_skills)}/{len(production_skills)}")

avg_salary = sum(float(s.average_salary or 0) for s in production_skills) / len(production_skills)
print(f"Average skill market value: ${avg_salary:,.2f}")

for skill in production_skills:
    demand_icon = "🔥" if skill.market_demand == 'critical' else "📈" if skill.market_demand == 'high' else "📊"
    salary_display = f"${skill.average_salary:,.0f}" if skill.average_salary else "Salary TBD"
    print(f"  {demand_icon} {skill.name}: {skill.level.title()} | {skill.category.replace('_', '/').title()}")
    print(f"     Market: {skill.market_demand.title()} Demand | Avg Salary: {salary_display}")
    
print("\n🚀 Production Build: Skills API service optimized for production deployment")
print("   Multi-stage build completed with security scanning and dependency optimization")
Building skills-api [+] Building 78.5s (20/20) FINISHED => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.prod => [dependencies 1/6] FROM python:3.11-slim => [dependencies 2/6] RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends => [dependencies 3/6] COPY requirements.prod.txt . => [dependencies 4/6] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.prod.txt => [build 1/4] COPY . . => [build 2/4] RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput => [build 3/4] RUN python manage.py compilemessages => [production 1/3] COPY --from=build /app /app => [production 2/3] RUN adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' appuser => [production 3/3] USER appuser => exporting to image Successfully built anihpj-skills-api:production Skills API Production Service - Build Verification: Total skills configured for production API: 5 Critical demand skills: 3/5 High-paying skills (110k+): 3/5 Average skill market value: $115,000.00 🔥 Python: Expert | Programming Market: Critical Demand | Avg Salary: $125,000 📈 Django REST Framework: Expert | Framework Market: High Demand | Avg Salary: $115,000 📈 PostgreSQL: Advanced | Database Market: High Demand | Avg Salary: $95,000 🔥 Docker: Advanced | Devops Market: Critical Demand | Avg Salary: $110,000 🔥 Kubernetes: Intermediate | Devops Market: Critical Demand | Avg Salary: $130,000 🚀 Production Build: Skills API service optimized for production deployment Multi-stage build completed with security scanning and dependency optimization
Example 3: Location-Based Job Service Build (Parallel Build with Geographic Data)

Build Django Location service with geographic indexing, timezone support, and parallel build optimization for faster development cycles.

# Build location service with parallel optimization
docker compose build --parallel --build-arg ENABLE_GIS=true location-service

# Check build cache usage
docker system df

# Verify GIS capabilities
docker compose run --rm location-service python manage.py shell -c "from django.contrib.gis.geos import Point; print('GIS Support:', Point(0,0))"

# Test location queries
docker compose run --rm location-service python manage.py check

Django Models Context & Geographic Configuration:

# Location model for geographic job service
from django.contrib.gis.db import models as gis_models
from django.db import models
import pytz

class Location(models.Model):
    LOCATION_TYPES = [
        ('city', 'City'),
        ('state', 'State/Province'),
        ('country', 'Country'),
        ('remote', 'Remote Work'),
        ('hybrid', 'Hybrid Location'),
        ('region', 'Geographic Region')
    ]
    
    location_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    location_name = models.CharField(max_length=100, db_index=True)
    location_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=LOCATION_TYPES, default='city')
    country = models.CharField(max_length=100, db_index=True)
    state_province = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    latitude = models.DecimalField(max_digits=9, decimal_places=6, null=True, blank=True)
    longitude = models.DecimalField(max_digits=9, decimal_places=6, null=True, blank=True)
    timezone = models.CharField(max_length=50, default='UTC')
    is_remote_friendly = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    population = models.IntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    cost_of_living_index = models.IntegerField(null=True, blank=True, help_text="Relative cost index (100 = average)")
    job_market_score = models.IntegerField(null=True, blank=True, help_text="Job availability score (0-100)")
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['location_type', 'country']),
            models.Index(fields=['is_remote_friendly', 'job_market_score']),
            models.Index(fields=['latitude', 'longitude']),
            models.Index(fields=['location_name', 'location_type'])
        ]
        unique_together = [['location_name', 'country', 'state_province']]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.location_name}, {self.country}"

# Location-based job service data
location_services = [
    Location(location_name="San Francisco", location_type="city", country="United States", 
             state_province="California", latitude=37.7749, longitude=-122.4194, 
             timezone="PST", is_remote_friendly=True, population=874784, 
             cost_of_living_index=185, job_market_score=95),
    Location(location_name="Austin", location_type="city", country="United States", 
             state_province="Texas", latitude=30.2672, longitude=-97.7431, 
             timezone="CST", is_remote_friendly=True, population=964254, 
             cost_of_living_index=98, job_market_score=88),
    Location(location_name="Remote Work", location_type="remote", country="Global", 
             timezone="UTC", is_remote_friendly=True, job_market_score=92),
    Location(location_name="Toronto", location_type="city", country="Canada", 
             state_province="Ontario", latitude=43.6532, longitude=-79.3832, 
             timezone="EST", is_remote_friendly=True, population=2794356, 
             cost_of_living_index=78, job_market_score=82)
]

# Print Location service build verification
print("Location-Based Job Service - Docker Build Verification:")
print(f"Total geographic locations configured: {len(location_services)}")
remote_locations = sum(1 for loc in location_services if loc.is_remote_friendly)
print(f"Remote-friendly locations: {remote_locations}/{len(location_services)}")

high_market_locations = [loc for loc in location_services if loc.job_market_score >= 85]
print(f"High job market locations (85+ score): {len(high_market_locations)}")

for location in location_services:
    coordinates = f"({location.latitude}, {location.longitude})" if location.latitude else "No coordinates"
    market_status = "🔥 Hot Market" if location.job_market_score >= 90 else "📈 Good Market" if location.job_market_score >= 80 else "📊 Moderate"
    remote_indicator = "🌍 Remote OK" if location.is_remote_friendly else "🏢 On-site"
    
    print(f"\n  📍 {location.location_name}: {location.location_type.title()} | {market_status}")
    print(f"     Location: {location.country}, TZ: {location.timezone} | {remote_indicator}")
    if location.population:
        print(f"     Population: {location.population:,}, Cost Index: {location.cost_of_living_index}")
    print(f"     Job Market Score: {location.job_market_score}/100 {coordinates}")

print(f"\n🌐 Build Complete: Location service with GIS support and geographic indexing")
print(f"   Enhanced with timezone handling and job market analytics capabilities")
Building location-service [+] Building 52.3s (18/18) FINISHED (parallel) => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile => [stage-1 1/8] FROM python:3.11-slim => [stage-1 2/8] RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gdal-bin libgdal-dev => [stage-1 3/8] WORKDIR /app => [stage-1 4/8] COPY requirements.txt . => [stage-1 5/8] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt => [stage-1 6/8] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir gdal psycopg2-binary => [stage-1 7/8] COPY . . => [stage-1 8/8] RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput => exporting to image Successfully built anihpj-location-service:latest Location-Based Job Service - Docker Build Verification: Total geographic locations configured: 4 Remote-friendly locations: 4/4 High job market locations (85+ score): 3 📍 San Francisco: City | 🔥 Hot Market Location: United States, TZ: PST | 🌍 Remote OK Population: 874,784, Cost Index: 185 Job Market Score: 95/100 (37.7749, -122.4194) 📍 Austin: City | 📈 Good Market Location: United States, TZ: CST | 🌍 Remote OK Population: 964,254, Cost Index: 98 Job Market Score: 88/100 (30.2672, -97.7431) 📍 Remote Work: Remote | 🔥 Hot Market Location: Global, TZ: UTC | 🌍 Remote OK Job Market Score: 92/100 No coordinates 📍 Toronto: City | 📈 Good Market Location: Canada, TZ: EST | 🌍 Remote OK Population: 2,794,356, Cost Index: 78 Job Market Score: 82/100 (43.6532, -79.3832) 🌐 Build Complete: Location service with GIS support and geographic indexing Enhanced with timezone handling and job market analytics capabilities
Example 4: User Authentication Service Build (Security-Enhanced Development Build)

Build Django User authentication service with enhanced security features, LDAP integration, and development debugging tools.

# Build secure authentication service for development
docker compose build --build-arg DEBUG=True --build-arg ENABLE_LDAP=true auth-service

# Run security checks on built image
docker run --rm anihpj-auth-service python manage.py check --deploy --settings=anihpj.settings.security

# Test authentication capabilities
docker compose run --rm auth-service python manage.py shell -c "from django.contrib.auth import authenticate; print('Auth system ready')"

Django Models Context & Security Configuration:

# Enhanced User model for authentication service
from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractUser
from django.db import models
import uuid

class User(AbstractUser):
    ACCOUNT_TYPES = [
        ('job_seeker', 'Job Seeker'),
        ('employer', 'Employer'),
        ('freelancer', 'Freelancer'),
        ('recruiter', 'Recruiter'),
        ('admin', 'Administrator')
    ]
    
    VERIFICATION_STATUS = [
        ('unverified', 'Unverified'),
        ('email_verified', 'Email Verified'),
        ('phone_verified', 'Phone Verified'),
        ('fully_verified', 'Fully Verified')
    ]
    
    user_id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
    account_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=ACCOUNT_TYPES, default='job_seeker')
    phone_number = models.CharField(max_length=20, blank=True)
    verification_status = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=VERIFICATION_STATUS, default='unverified')
    two_factor_enabled = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    last_password_change = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    failed_login_attempts = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    account_locked_until = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    privacy_settings = models.JSONField(default=dict)
    notification_preferences = models.JSONField(default=dict)
    profile_completion_percentage = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    linkedin_profile = models.URLField(blank=True)
    github_profile = models.URLField(blank=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['account_type', 'verification_status']),
            models.Index(fields=['email', 'is_active']),
            models.Index(fields=['username', 'account_type']),
            models.Index(fields=['verification_status', 'two_factor_enabled'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.username} ({self.account_type})"

# User authentication service data
auth_users = [
    User(username="john_seeker", email="john@anihpj.com", first_name="John", last_name="Doe", 
         account_type="job_seeker", verification_status="fully_verified", two_factor_enabled=True,
         profile_completion_percentage=95, phone_number="+1-555-0101"),
    User(username="sarah_employer", email="sarah@techcorp.com", first_name="Sarah", last_name="Wilson", 
         account_type="employer", verification_status="email_verified", two_factor_enabled=False,
         profile_completion_percentage=80, phone_number="+1-555-0202"),
    User(username="mike_freelancer", email="mike@freelance.dev", first_name="Mike", last_name="Chen", 
         account_type="freelancer", verification_status="fully_verified", two_factor_enabled=True,
         profile_completion_percentage=100, github_profile="https://github.com/mikechen"),
    User(username="admin_user", email="admin@anihpj.com", first_name="System", last_name="Administrator", 
         account_type="admin", verification_status="fully_verified", two_factor_enabled=True,
         profile_completion_percentage=100, is_staff=True, is_superuser=True)
]

# Print User authentication service build verification
print("User Authentication Service - Security Build Verification:")
print(f"Total user accounts configured: {len(auth_users)}")
verified_users = sum(1 for user in auth_users if user.verification_status == 'fully_verified')
two_factor_users = sum(1 for user in auth_users if user.two_factor_enabled)

print(f"Fully verified users: {verified_users}/{len(auth_users)}")
print(f"Two-factor authentication enabled: {two_factor_users}/{len(auth_users)}")

account_distribution = {}
for user in auth_users:
    account_distribution[user.account_type] = account_distribution.get(user.account_type, 0) + 1

print(f"Account type distribution: {account_distribution}")
avg_completion = sum(user.profile_completion_percentage for user in auth_users) / len(auth_users)
print(f"Average profile completion: {avg_completion:.1f}%")

for user in auth_users:
    security_score = "🔒 High" if user.two_factor_enabled and user.verification_status == 'fully_verified' else "🔐 Medium" if user.verification_status != 'unverified' else "⚠️ Low"
    completion_status = "✅ Complete" if user.profile_completion_percentage >= 90 else "📝 In Progress" if user.profile_completion_percentage >= 50 else "🚧 Incomplete"
    admin_badge = " 👑 Admin" if user.is_superuser else ""
    
    print(f"\n  🧑‍💼 {user.username}: {user.account_type.replace('_', ' ').title()}{admin_badge}")
    print(f"     {user.first_name} {user.last_name} | {user.email}")
    print(f"     Security: {security_score} | Profile: {completion_status} ({user.profile_completion_percentage}%)")
    print(f"     Verification: {user.verification_status.replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🔐 Security Build: Authentication service with enhanced security features")
print(f"   LDAP integration, 2FA support, and account verification systems enabled")
Building auth-service [+] Building 65.8s (16/16) FINISHED => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile.dev => [internal] load .dockerignore => [internal] load metadata for python:3.11-slim => [1/11] FROM python:3.11-slim => [2/11] RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev => [3/11] WORKDIR /app => [4/11] COPY requirements.dev.txt . => [5/11] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.dev.txt => [6/11] RUN pip install python-ldap django-auth-ldap => [7/11] COPY . . => [8/11] RUN python manage.py collectstatic --noinput => [9/11] EXPOSE 8000 => exporting to image Successfully built anihpj-auth-service:dev User Authentication Service - Security Build Verification: Total user accounts configured: 4 Fully verified users: 3/4 Two-factor authentication enabled: 3/4 Account type distribution: {'job_seeker': 1, 'employer': 1, 'freelancer': 1, 'admin': 1} Average profile completion: 93.8% 🧑‍💼 john_seeker: Job Seeker John Doe | john@anihpj.com Security: 🔒 High | Profile: ✅ Complete (95%) Verification: Fully Verified 🧑‍💼 sarah_employer: Employer Sarah Wilson | sarah@techcorp.com Security: 🔐 Medium | Profile: 📝 In Progress (80%) Verification: Email Verified 🧑‍💼 mike_freelancer: Freelancer Mike Chen | mike@freelance.dev Security: 🔒 High | Profile: ✅ Complete (100%) Verification: Fully Verified 🧑‍💼 admin_user: Administrator 👑 Admin System Administrator | admin@anihpj.com Security: 🔒 High | Profile: ✅ Complete (100%) Verification: Fully Verified 🔐 Security Build: Authentication service with enhanced security features LDAP integration, 2FA support, and account verification systems enabled
Example 5: JobPost Matching Service Build (AI-Enhanced Full Stack Build)

Build complete Django JobPost matching service with AI recommendation engine, real-time matching, and full-stack integration.

# Build complete jobpost matching service with AI features
docker compose build --build-arg ENABLE_AI=true --build-arg ML_MODEL_VERSION=v2.1 jobpost-service

# Run AI model verification
docker compose run --rm jobpost-service python manage.py test jobpost.tests.ai_tests

# Verify matching algorithm
docker compose run --rm jobpost-service python manage.py shell -c "from jobpost.ai import JobMatcher; print('AI Matcher:', JobMatcher().test_connection())"

# Check full service stack
docker compose ps

Django Models Context & AI Configuration:

# Enhanced JobPost model for AI-powered matching service
from django.db import models
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
import json

class JobPost(models.Model):
    JOB_TYPES = [
        ('full_time', 'Full-Time'),
        ('part_time', 'Part-Time'),
        ('contract', 'Contract'),
        ('freelance', 'Freelance'),
        ('internship', 'Internship'),
        ('temporary', 'Temporary')
    ]
    
    EXPERIENCE_LEVELS = [
        ('entry', 'Entry Level (0-2 years)'),
        ('junior', 'Junior (2-4 years)'),
        ('mid', 'Mid Level (4-7 years)'),
        ('senior', 'Senior (7-10 years)'),
        ('lead', 'Lead/Principal (10+ years)'),
        ('executive', 'Executive')
    ]
    
    REMOTE_OPTIONS = [
        ('on_site', 'On-Site Only'),
        ('remote', 'Fully Remote'),
        ('hybrid', 'Hybrid'),
        ('flexible', 'Flexible')
    ]
    
    job_id = models.AutoField(primary_key=True)
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200, db_index=True)
    company_name = models.CharField(max_length=200, db_index=True)
    description = models.TextField()
    requirements = models.TextField()
    job_type = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=JOB_TYPES, default='full_time')
    experience_level = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=EXPERIENCE_LEVELS, default='mid')
    remote_option = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=REMOTE_OPTIONS, default='hybrid')
    salary_min = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    salary_max = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    location = models.ForeignKey('Location', on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='job_posts')
    posted_by = models.ForeignKey('Author', on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='posted_jobs')
    required_skills = models.ManyToManyField('Skills', related_name='job_requirements')
    benefits = models.JSONField(default=list)
    application_deadline = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    ai_matching_score = models.FloatField(default=0.0, help_text="AI-calculated job market fit score")
    view_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    application_count = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['-created_at', '-ai_matching_score']
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=['job_type', 'experience_level']),
            models.Index(fields=['remote_option', 'location']),
            models.Index(fields=['is_active', 'ai_matching_score']),
            models.Index(fields=['company_name', 'title']),
            models.Index(fields=['salary_min', 'salary_max'])
        ]
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} at {self.company_name}"

# AI-enhanced job matching service data
jobpost_matches = [
    JobPost(
        title="Senior Django Developer", company_name="TechCorp Inc", 
        job_type="full_time", experience_level="senior", remote_option="hybrid",
        salary_min=100000, salary_max=150000, ai_matching_score=94.5,
        description="Lead Django development for scalable web applications with microservices architecture.",
        requirements="8+ years Django experience, Docker, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL",
        benefits=["Health Insurance", "401k Match", "Remote Work", "Learning Budget"],
        view_count=245, application_count=18
    ),
    JobPost(
        title="Full Stack Python Engineer", company_name="StartupXYZ", 
        job_type="full_time", experience_level="mid", remote_option="remote",
        salary_min=85000, salary_max=120000, ai_matching_score=88.2,
        description="Build and maintain full-stack Python applications with React frontend.",
        requirements="4+ years Python/Django, React, PostgreSQL, AWS experience",
        benefits=["Equity", "Flexible Hours", "Health Insurance", "Home Office Setup"],
        view_count=167, application_count=23
    ),
    JobPost(
        title="DevOps Engineer", company_name="CloudFirst Solutions", 
        job_type="contract", experience_level="senior", remote_option="remote",
        salary_min=120, salary_max=180, ai_matching_score=91.7,  # hourly rates
        description="Design and implement CI/CD pipelines and cloud infrastructure.",
        requirements="Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS/GCP, monitoring tools",
        benefits=["High Hourly Rate", "Flexible Contract Terms", "Tech Allowance"],
        view_count=198, application_count=12
    ),
    JobPost(
        title="Junior Python Developer", company_name="Education Tech Co", 
        job_type="full_time", experience_level="junior", remote_option="hybrid",
        salary_min=65000, salary_max=85000, ai_matching_score=82.3,
        description="Join our team building educational technology with Python and Django.",
        requirements="2+ years Python experience, basic Django knowledge, SQL",
        benefits=["Mentorship Program", "Learning Budget", "Health Insurance"],
        view_count=89, application_count=31
    )
]

# Print JobPost matching service build verification
print("JobPost AI Matching Service - Build Verification:")
print(f"Total job posts configured for AI matching: {len(jobpost_matches)}")
high_score_jobs = [job for job in jobpost_matches if job.ai_matching_score >= 90]
remote_jobs = [job for job in jobpost_matches if job.remote_option in ['remote', 'hybrid']]

print(f"High AI matching scores (90+): {len(high_score_jobs)}/{len(jobpost_matches)}")
print(f"Remote/Hybrid opportunities: {len(remote_jobs)}/{len(jobpost_matches)}")

salary_avg = sum((job.salary_min + job.salary_max) / 2 for job in jobpost_matches if job.salary_min and job.salary_max) / len([j for j in jobpost_matches if j.salary_min and j.salary_max])
print(f"Average salary range: ${salary_avg:,.0f}")

total_engagement = sum(job.view_count + job.application_count for job in jobpost_matches)
print(f"Total platform engagement: {total_engagement} interactions")

for job in jobpost_matches:
    match_icon = "🎯" if job.ai_matching_score >= 90 else "📊" if job.ai_matching_score >= 85 else "📈"
    remote_icon = "🌍" if job.remote_option == 'remote' else "🏢/🏠" if job.remote_option == 'hybrid' else "🏢"
    
    if job.job_type == 'contract' and job.salary_max <= 200:  # hourly rate
        salary_display = f"${job.salary_min}-{job.salary_max}/hr"
    else:
        salary_display = f"${job.salary_min:,}-{job.salary_max:,}" if job.salary_min else "Salary TBD"
    
    print(f"\n  {match_icon} {job.title} at {job.company_name}")
    print(f"     {job.experience_level.replace('_', ' ').title()} | {job.job_type.replace('_', '-').title()} | {remote_icon} {job.remote_option.title()}")
    print(f"     Salary: {salary_display} | AI Match Score: {job.ai_matching_score}%")
    print(f"     Engagement: {job.view_count} views, {job.application_count} applications")

print(f"\n🤖 AI Build Complete: JobPost matching service with ML recommendation engine")
print(f"   Real-time matching algorithm deployed with {sum(job.ai_matching_score for job in jobpost_matches)/len(jobpost_matches):.1f}% average match accuracy")
