validation for suspicious logins • Enable two-factor authentication for all users • Monitor API rate limiting and implement progressive delays • Set up automated alerts for session hijacking attempts • Require password changes every 60 days for security

docker compose exec

Official Docs: docker compose exec | Docker CLI: docker compose exec

Execute commands in running compose service containers for debugging and administration.

Full Description

The docker compose exec command allows you to execute commands inside running containers of your anihpj Django application. This is essential for database management, Django shell access, file system operations, debugging running applications, and performing administrative tasks without stopping your services.

For Django development workflows, this command enables direct access to running Django containers for migrations, shell sessions, log analysis, package installation, configuration changes, and troubleshooting. It's particularly useful for interactive debugging and live system administration in your anihpj jobpost application environment.

Syntax

# Execute command in service container
docker compose exec web python manage.py shell

# Interactive bash shell
docker compose exec web bash

# Run Django management commands
docker compose exec web python manage.py migrate

# Database shell access
docker compose exec db psql -U postgres

# Non-interactive command execution
docker compose exec -T web python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

# Execute as specific user
docker compose exec --user postgres db psql

# Set environment variables
docker compose exec -e DEBUG=True web python manage.py runserver

Parameters Description

  • -T, --no-TTY: Disable pseudo-TTY allocation. Essential for Django automated scripts and CI/CD pipeline operations.
  • --user: Run command as specific user. Access Django containers with appropriate permissions for database or file operations.
  • -e, --env: Set environment variables. Configure Django settings and debug modes during command execution.
  • --workdir: Set working directory for command execution. Navigate to specific Django project directories within containers.
  • --index: Run command against specific container instance when scaled. Target Django web server instances in scaled deployments.

Attributes

  • Interactive Sessions: Full shell access to Django containers for real-time debugging and development workflow.
  • Live Environment: Execute commands in running production environment without service disruption for Django operations.
  • Process Inheritance: Commands inherit container environment variables and network configuration for Django context.
  • TTY Support: Full terminal support for interactive Django shell sessions and complex debugging workflows.
  • User Context: Execute commands with specific user permissions for Django database and file system access.

Methods

Django Administration

Syntax: docker compose exec web python manage.py {command}

Parameters: command (string) - Django management command to execute

Execute Django management commands like migrations, user creation, and data operations in running containers.

Database Operations

Syntax: docker compose exec db psql -U postgres -d {database}

Parameters: database (string) - Target database name for PostgreSQL operations

Direct database access for Django model debugging, data analysis, and database administration tasks.

Return Value

Returns command exit status and output from executed commands within Django container environment. Provides direct access to command results and error messages.

  • Success (0): Command executed successfully within container
  • Error (1-255): Command failure with specific exit code from executed process
  • Interactive Mode: Continuous session until manual exit or termination

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Django Shell Access for Model Operations (Interactive Development)

Access Django shell in running container to perform model operations, test queries, and debug application logic for all model relationships.

# Access Django shell in running container
docker compose exec web python manage.py shell

# Run Django migrations
docker compose exec web python manage.py migrate

# Create superuser
docker compose exec web python manage.py createsuperuser --noinput --username admin --email admin@anihpj.com

Django Models Context & Shell Operations:

# Django shell session operations for all models
# Models available in shell: User, Author, Skills, Location, JobPost

# Shell session demonstration
shell_operations = """
# Import all models for interactive development
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from jobpost.models import Author, Skills, Location, JobPost
from django.db import transaction

# User management operations
admin_user = User.objects.create_superuser(
    username='admin',
    email='admin@anihpj.com',
    password='admin123'
)

# Author profile creation and management
sarah_author = Author.objects.create(
    username='sarah_dev',
    first_name='Sarah',
    last_name='Chen',
    email='sarah@anihpj.com',
    expertise_level='senior',
    specialization='fullstack',
    years_experience=7,
    hourly_rate=95.00,
    is_available=True,
    location='San Francisco, CA'
)

# Skills database operations
python_skill = Skills.objects.create(
    skill_name='Python Django',
    skill_type='technical',
    proficiency_level='expert',
    years_experience=5,
    is_verified=True,
    industry_demand='high'
)

docker_skill = Skills.objects.create(
    skill_name='Docker Containerization',
    skill_type='technical', 
    proficiency_level='advanced',
    years_experience=3,
    is_verified=True,
    industry_demand='high'
)

# Location setup for job posting
sf_location = Location.objects.create(
    location_name='San Francisco',
    location_type='city',
    country='United States',
    state_province='California',
    latitude=37.7749,
    longitude=-122.4194,
    timezone='PST',
    is_remote_friendly=True,
    population=874784,
    job_market_score=95
)

# JobPost creation with relationships
senior_job = JobPost.objects.create(
    title='Senior Django Developer',
    company_name='TechCorp Inc',
    location=sf_location,
    job_type='full_time',
    experience_level='senior',
    salary_range='100k-150k',
    description='Senior Django developer role with React frontend experience.',
    requirements='5+ years Django, PostgreSQL, Docker experience required.',
    posted_by=sarah_author,
    is_active=True,
    is_featured=True
)

# Add skills to job post
senior_job.skills_required.add(python_skill, docker_skill)

# Query operations for testing
print("=== Django Shell Operations Summary ===")
print(f"Total users: {User.objects.count()}")
print(f"Total authors: {Author.objects.count()}")
print(f"Total skills: {Skills.objects.count()}")
print(f"Total locations: {Location.objects.count()}")
print(f"Total job posts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

# Advanced queries
active_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True)
senior_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(experience_level='senior')
high_demand_skills = Skills.objects.filter(industry_demand='high')

print(f"Active job posts: {active_jobs.count()}")
print(f"Senior level positions: {senior_jobs.count()}")
print(f"High demand skills: {high_demand_skills.count()}")

# Relationship queries
for job in JobPost.objects.all():
    skills_count = job.skills_required.count()
    print(f"Job: {job.title} - Skills required: {skills_count}")
    print(f"  Posted by: {job.posted_by.first_name} {job.posted_by.last_name}")
    print(f"  Location: {job.location.location_name}")
    print(f"  Salary: {job.salary_range}, Type: {job.job_type}")
"""

# Simulate shell operations output
print("Django Shell Access - Docker Compose Exec Operations:")
print("Successfully connected to running Django container")
print("Python 3.11.7 (main, Dec  8 2023, 18:56:58)")
print("Type 'help', 'copyright' or 'license' for more information.")
print("(InteractiveConsole)")
print(">>> from jobpost.models import *")
print(">>> from django.contrib.auth.models import User")

# Model creation results
models_created = {
    "User": {"count": 1, "example": "admin (superuser)"},
    "Author": {"count": 1, "example": "sarah_dev (Senior Fullstack)"},
    "Skills": {"count": 2, "example": "Python Django, Docker Containerization"},
    "Location": {"count": 1, "example": "San Francisco, CA (Score: 95)"},
    "JobPost": {"count": 1, "example": "Senior Django Developer at TechCorp Inc"}
}

print("\n=== Model Creation Results ===")
for model_name, data in models_created.items():
    print(f"{model_name}: {data['count']} created")
    print(f"  Example: {data['example']}")

# Relationship verification
print("\n=== Relationship Verification ===")
print("JobPost 'Senior Django Developer':")
print("  - Skills Required: Python Django (Expert), Docker Containerization (Advanced)")
print("  - Posted by: Sarah Chen (sarah_dev)")
print("  - Location: San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly)")
print("  - Type: full_time, Level: senior, Salary: $100k-150k")

print("\n=== Query Performance ===")
print("Database operations completed successfully")
print("All relationships established and validated")
print("Ready for application testing and development")

print("\n>>> # Shell remains active for continued development")
print(">>> # Use Ctrl+D or exit() to leave shell")
Python 3.11.7 (main, Dec 8 2023, 18:56:58) [GCC 11.4.0] Type "help", "copyright" or "license" for more information. (InteractiveConsole) Django Shell Access - Docker Compose Exec Operations: Successfully connected to running Django container Python 3.11.7 (main, Dec 8 2023, 18:56:58) Type 'help', 'copyright' or 'license' for more information. (InteractiveConsole) >>> from jobpost.models import * >>> from django.contrib.auth.models import User === Model Creation Results === User: 1 created Example: admin (superuser) Author: 1 created Example: sarah_dev (Senior Fullstack) Skills: 2 created Example: Python Django, Docker Containerization Location: 1 created Example: San Francisco, CA (Score: 95) JobPost: 1 created Example: Senior Django Developer at TechCorp Inc === Relationship Verification === JobPost 'Senior Django Developer': - Skills Required: Python Django (Expert), Docker Containerization (Advanced) - Posted by: Sarah Chen (sarah_dev) - Location: San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly) - Type: full_time, Level: senior, Salary: $100k-150k === Query Performance === Database operations completed successfully All relationships established and validated Ready for application testing and development >>> # Shell remains active for continued development >>> # Use Ctrl+D or exit() to leave shell

docker compose run

Official Docs: docker compose run | Docker CLI: docker compose run

Run one-time commands against services in new containers with compose configuration.

Full Description

The docker compose run command creates and runs a new container for a service to execute one-time commands. Unlike exec, this starts a fresh container with the same configuration as your anihpj Django service but isolated from the main running services, making it perfect for testing, migrations, debugging, and administrative tasks.

For Django development workflows, this command is essential for running database migrations safely, testing code changes in isolation, executing management commands without affecting running services, performing data imports/exports, and conducting debugging sessions in clean environments for your anihpj jobpost application.

Syntax

# Run Django management command
docker compose run web python manage.py migrate

# Run command with port mapping
docker compose run -p 8080:8000 web python manage.py runserver

# Run in detached mode
docker compose run -d web celery worker

# Remove container after run
docker compose run --rm web python manage.py test

# Run with environment variables
docker compose run -e DEBUG=True web python manage.py shell

# Run with volume mount
docker compose run -v ./backup:/backup web python manage.py dumpdata

# Run without dependencies
docker compose run --no-deps web python manage.py check

Parameters Description

  • --rm: Remove container after command completion. Essential for Django one-time tasks to prevent container accumulation.
  • -p, --publish: Publish container ports to host. Enable Django development server access during testing and debugging.
  • -d, --detach: Run container in background. Useful for Django background tasks and long-running processes.
  • -e, --env: Set environment variables. Configure Django settings and debug modes for specific command execution.
  • --no-deps: Don't start linked services. Run Django commands without database dependencies for testing isolated components.

Attributes

  • Fresh Container: New container instance with clean state for Django testing and isolated command execution.
  • Service Configuration: Inherits all Django service settings including environment variables, volumes, and network configuration.
  • Dependency Control: Option to start with or without service dependencies for Django isolated testing.
  • Port Publishing: Custom port mapping for Django development servers and debugging sessions.
  • Volume Access: Full access to Django project volumes for data operations and file manipulation.

Methods

Database Migration Testing

Syntax: docker compose run --rm web python manage.py migrate --dry-run

Parameters: --dry-run (boolean) - Test Django migrations without applying changes

Test Django database migrations in isolated container before applying to production database.

Data Backup Operations

Syntax: docker compose run --rm -v ./backup:/backup web python manage.py dumpdata

Parameters: volume mount for Django data export to host filesystem

Create Django application data backups using fresh containers with external volume access.

Return Value

Returns command exit status from executed Django operations in new container. Shows command output and any errors from isolated execution environment.

  • Success (0): Command executed successfully in new container
  • Error (1-255): Command failure with specific exit code from Django operation
  • Container Cleanup: Automatic removal when --rm flag used

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Complete Django Application Testing Suite (Isolated Testing Environment)

Run comprehensive Django test suite in isolated containers for all models and application components using fresh database state.

# Run complete Django test suite
docker compose run --rm web python manage.py test

# Run specific app tests
docker compose run --rm web python manage.py test jobpost

# Run tests with coverage
docker compose run --rm -e DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.test_settings web python manage.py test --verbosity=2

Django Models Context & Testing Framework:

# Complete Django testing suite for all models
# Testing User, Author, Skills, Location, JobPost models and relationships

test_scenarios = """
# Django Test Suite - Complete Model Testing
from django.test import TestCase, TransactionTestCase
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from jobpost.models import Author, Skills, Location, JobPost
from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError
from django.db import IntegrityError

class UserModelTestCase(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.user = User.objects.create_user(
            username='testuser',
            email='test@anihpj.com',
            password='testpass123'
        )
    
    def test_user_creation(self):
        self.assertEqual(self.user.username, 'testuser')
        self.assertEqual(self.user.email, 'test@anihpj.com')
        self.assertTrue(self.user.check_password('testpass123'))
    
    def test_user_str_representation(self):
        self.assertEqual(str(self.user), 'testuser')

class AuthorModelTestCase(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.author = Author.objects.create(
            username='test_author',
            first_name='Test',
            last_name='Author',
            email='author@anihpj.com',
            expertise_level='senior',
            specialization='fullstack',
            years_experience=5,
            hourly_rate=85.00
        )
    
    def test_author_creation(self):
        self.assertEqual(self.author.username, 'test_author')
        self.assertEqual(self.author.expertise_level, 'senior')
        self.assertEqual(float(self.author.hourly_rate), 85.00)
    
    def test_author_str_representation(self):
        self.assertEqual(str(self.author), 'Test Author (senior)')

class SkillsModelTestCase(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.skill = Skills.objects.create(
            skill_name='Django Testing',
            skill_type='technical',
            proficiency_level='advanced',
            years_experience=3,
            is_verified=True,
            industry_demand='high'
        )
    
    def test_skills_creation(self):
        self.assertEqual(self.skill.skill_name, 'Django Testing')
        self.assertEqual(self.skill.skill_type, 'technical')
        self.assertTrue(self.skill.is_verified)
    
    def test_skills_unique_constraint(self):
        with self.assertRaises(IntegrityError):
            Skills.objects.create(
                skill_name='Django Testing',  # Duplicate
                skill_type='technical'
            )

class LocationModelTestCase(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.location = Location.objects.create(
            location_name='Test City',
            location_type='city',
            country='Test Country',
            latitude=40.7128,
            longitude=-74.0060,
            job_market_score=75
        )
    
    def test_location_creation(self):
        self.assertEqual(self.location.location_name, 'Test City')
        self.assertEqual(self.location.job_market_score, 75)
        self.assertEqual(float(self.location.latitude), 40.7128)

class JobPostModelTestCase(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.location = Location.objects.create(
            location_name='Test Location',
            country='Test Country'
        )
        self.author = Author.objects.create(
            username='job_poster',
            first_name='Job',
            last_name='Poster',
            email='poster@anihpj.com'
        )
        self.skill = Skills.objects.create(
            skill_name='Test Skill',
            skill_type='technical'
        )
        self.job_post = JobPost.objects.create(
            title='Test Developer Position',
            company_name='Test Company',
            location=self.location,
            job_type='full_time',
            experience_level='mid',
            description='Test job description',
            requirements='Test requirements',
            posted_by=self.author
        )
        self.job_post.skills_required.add(self.skill)
    
    def test_job_post_creation(self):
        self.assertEqual(self.job_post.title, 'Test Developer Position')
        self.assertEqual(self.job_post.company_name, 'Test Company')
        self.assertEqual(self.job_post.location, self.location)
        self.assertEqual(self.job_post.posted_by, self.author)
    
    def test_job_post_skills_relationship(self):
        self.assertEqual(self.job_post.skills_required.count(), 1)
        self.assertIn(self.skill, self.job_post.skills_required.all())
    
    def test_job_post_str_representation(self):
        expected = 'Test Developer Position at Test Company'
        self.assertEqual(str(self.job_post), expected)

class IntegrationTestCase(TransactionTestCase):
    def test_complete_job_posting_workflow(self):
        # Create complete job posting with all relationships
        user = User.objects.create_user('workflow_user', 'user@anihpj.com', 'pass')
        
        location = Location.objects.create(
            location_name='Integration City',
            country='Test Country',
            job_market_score=90
        )
        
        author = Author.objects.create(
            username='integration_author',
            first_name='Integration',
            last_name='Tester',
            email='integration@anihpj.com',
            expertise_level='lead',
            specialization='backend'
        )
        
        skills = [
            Skills.objects.create(skill_name='Python', skill_type='technical'),
            Skills.objects.create(skill_name='Django', skill_type='technical'),
            Skills.objects.create(skill_name='PostgreSQL', skill_type='technical')
        ]
        
        job_post = JobPost.objects.create(
            title='Senior Backend Developer',
            company_name='Integration Corp',
            location=location,
            job_type='full_time',
            experience_level='senior',
            salary_range='100k-150k',
            description='Senior backend position',
            requirements='5+ years experience',
            posted_by=author,
            is_active=True
        )
        
        job_post.skills_required.set(skills)
        
        # Verify complete workflow
        self.assertEqual(JobPost.objects.count(), 1)
        self.assertEqual(job_post.skills_required.count(), 3)
        self.assertTrue(job_post.is_active)
        self.assertEqual(job_post.posted_by.expertise_level, 'lead')
"""

# Simulate test execution results
test_results = {
    "UserModelTestCase": {"tests_run": 2, "failures": 0, "errors": 0, "status": "PASSED"},
    "AuthorModelTestCase": {"tests_run": 2, "failures": 0, "errors": 0, "status": "PASSED"},
    "SkillsModelTestCase": {"tests_run": 2, "failures": 0, "errors": 0, "status": "PASSED"},
    "LocationModelTestCase": {"tests_run": 1, "failures": 0, "errors": 0, "status": "PASSED"},
    "JobPostModelTestCase": {"tests_run": 3, "failures": 0, "errors": 0, "status": "PASSED"},
    "IntegrationTestCase": {"tests_run": 1, "failures": 0, "errors": 0, "status": "PASSED"}
}

print("Django Application Testing Suite - Docker Compose Run Results:")
print("Running isolated test environment in fresh container...")
print("=" * 60)

total_tests = sum(case["tests_run"] for case in test_results.values())
total_failures = sum(case["failures"] for case in test_results.values())
total_errors = sum(case["errors"] for case in test_results.values())

for test_case, results in test_results.items():
    status_icon = "✅" if results["status"] == "PASSED" else "❌"
    print(f"{status_icon} {test_case}")
    print(f"   Tests: {results['tests_run']}, Failures: {results['failures']}, Errors: {results['errors']}")

print("=" * 60)
print(f"🎯 Test Summary:")
print(f"   Total tests run: {total_tests}")
print(f"   Failures: {total_failures}")
print(f"   Errors: {total_errors}")
print(f"   Success rate: {((total_tests - total_failures - total_errors) / total_tests * 100):.1f}%")

print(f"\n🧪 Model Coverage:")
models_tested = ["User", "Author", "Skills", "Location", "JobPost"]
print(f"   Models tested: {len(models_tested)} (User, Author, Skills, Location, JobPost)")
print(f"   Relationship testing: Many-to-Many (JobPost-Skills), Foreign Key (JobPost-Location, JobPost-Author)")
print(f"   Constraint testing: Unique constraints, required fields, data validation")

print(f"\n⚡ Test Performance:")
print(f"   Container startup: Fresh isolated environment")
print(f"   Database: Clean test database with transactions")
print(f"   Cleanup: Automatic container removal after completion")
print(f"   Test execution time: ~15 seconds")

print(f"\n✅ All Django models and relationships tested successfully!")
print(f"   Ready for production deployment with verified functionality")
Creating test database for alias 'default'... System check identified no issues (0 silenced). docker compose pull

Official Docs: docker compose pull | Docker CLI: docker compose pull

Pull latest images for services defined in compose file to ensure updated base images.

Full Description

The docker compose pull command downloads the latest versions of images for services defined in your compose configuration. For your anihpj Django project, this ensures you have the latest Python base images, database updates, Redis versions, and any other service dependencies with security patches and performance improvements.

This command is crucial for maintaining Django application security, getting latest Python features, updating database engines, and ensuring your anihpj jobpost application stack runs on current and secure base images before deployment or development work.

Syntax

# Pull all service images
docker compose pull

# Pull specific service
docker compose pull web

# Pull with parallel processing
docker compose pull --parallel

# Pull and ignore missing images
docker compose pull --ignore-pull-failures

# Pull with policy control
docker compose pull --policy always

# Pull quietly (suppress output)
docker compose pull -q

# Pull multiple specific services
docker compose pull web db redis

Parameters Description

  • --parallel: Pull multiple images simultaneously. Speeds up Django development environment updates by downloading base images concurrently.
  • --ignore-pull-failures: Continue pulling other images if some fail. Ensures Django development continues even if some service images are unavailable.
  • --policy: Set pull policy (missing, always). Control when Django service images are updated based on availability and version requirements.
  • -q, --quiet: Suppress verbose output. Reduce noise during automated Django deployment processes and CI/CD pipelines.
  • --include-deps: Also pull images of services declared as dependencies. Ensure Django service dependencies are updated together.

Attributes

  • Image Registry: Pulls from configured registries (Docker Hub, private registries) for Django service base images and dependencies.
  • Version Control: Respects image tags and versions specified in Django compose configuration for consistent deployments.
  • Parallel Downloads: Concurrent image pulling for faster Django development environment setup and updates.
  • Dependency Resolution: Handles Django service image dependencies and linked service requirements automatically.
  • Security Updates: Ensures Django applications run on latest security-patched base images and system packages.

Methods

Production Deployment Preparation

Syntax: docker compose pull --policy always --parallel

Parameters: --policy always (force updates), --parallel (speed optimization)

Prepare Django production deployment with latest security updates and performance improvements from base images.

Development Environment Refresh

Syntax: docker compose pull --ignore-pull-failures

Parameters: --ignore-pull-failures (continue on errors)

Update Django development environment images while allowing development to continue if some images are temporarily unavailable.

Return Value

Returns pull status and progress for each Django service image. Shows download progress, version updates, and any pull failures or errors.

  • Success (0): All specified images pulled successfully
  • Error (1): Image pull failure or registry access issues
  • Partial Success: Some images pulled, others failed (with --ignore-pull-failures)

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Security Update Workflow (Complete Stack Image Updates)

Update all Django stack images for security patches including Python base images, PostgreSQL, Redis, and supporting services for anihpj project.

# Pull all latest images for security updates
docker compose pull --parallel

# Verify image updates
docker image ls | grep anihpj

# Check for security updates in base images
docker compose pull --policy always python postgres redis

Django Models Context & Security Updates:

# Security-focused image update analysis for Django stack
# Ensuring all model operations run on secure, updated infrastructure

import datetime
from typing import Dict, List

# Security update tracking for Django stack components
security_updates = {
    "python_base": {
        "current_version": "3.11.7",
        "latest_version": "3.11.8",
        "security_patches": ["CVE-2024-0001", "CVE-2024-0002"],
        "update_priority": "high",
        "services_affected": ["web", "worker", "scheduler"]
    },
    "postgresql": {
        "current_version": "15.4",
        "latest_version": "15.6", 
        "security_patches": ["CVE-2024-0010"],
        "update_priority": "medium",
        "services_affected": ["db"]
    },
    "redis": {
        "current_version": "7.0.12",
        "latest_version": "7.0.15",
        "security_patches": ["CVE-2024-0020"],
        "update_priority": "medium", 
        "services_affected": ["redis", "cache"]
    },
    "nginx": {
        "current_version": "1.24.0",
        "latest_version": "1.25.3",
        "security_patches": ["CVE-2024-0030"],
        "update_priority": "high",
        "services_affected": ["nginx", "proxy"]
    }
}

# Django application components requiring secure infrastructure
django_components = {
    "user_auth": {
        "model": "User",
        "security_requirements": ["secure_sessions", "password_hashing", "csrf_protection"],
        "affected_services": ["web", "db", "redis"],
        "critical_operations": ["login", "password_reset", "session_management"]
    },
    "job_posting": {
        "model": "JobPost", 
        "security_requirements": ["input_validation", "sql_injection_protection", "xss_prevention"],
        "affected_services": ["web", "db"],
        "critical_operations": ["job_creation", "application_submission", "search_queries"]
    },
    "author_profiles": {
        "model": "Author",
        "security_requirements": ["data_encryption", "access_control", "audit_logging"],
        "affected_services": ["web", "db", "redis"],
        "critical_operations": ["profile_updates", "skill_verification", "rate_management"]
    },
    "skills_database": {
        "model": "Skills",
        "security_requirements": ["data_integrity", "access_validation", "caching_security"],
        "affected_services": ["web", "db", "redis"],
        "critical_operations": ["skill_creation", "verification_process", "demand_analytics"]
    },
    "location_services": {
        "model": "Location", 
        "security_requirements": ["geo_data_protection", "privacy_compliance", "api_security"],
        "affected_services": ["web", "db", "elasticsearch"],
        "critical_operations": ["location_lookup", "geo_search", "market_analysis"]
    }
}

# Print security update analysis
print("Django Stack Security Update Analysis - Docker Compose Pull:")
print("=" * 70)
print(f"Security scan date: {datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}")

total_vulnerabilities = sum(len(component["security_patches"]) for component in security_updates.values())
high_priority_updates = sum(1 for component in security_updates.values() if component["update_priority"] == "high")

print(f"\n🔒 Security Overview:")
print(f"   Total vulnerabilities patched: {total_vulnerabilities}")
print(f"   High priority updates: {high_priority_updates}/4 components")

for component, details in security_updates.items():
    priority_icon = "🔴" if details["update_priority"] == "high" else "🟡"
    version_change = f"{details['current_version']} → {details['latest_version']}"
    
    print(f"\n{priority_icon} {component.replace('_', ' ').title()}:")
    print(f"   Version update: {version_change}")
    print(f"   Security patches: {len(details['security_patches'])} CVEs")
    print(f"   Services affected: {', '.join(details['services_affected'])}")
    
    if details['security_patches']:
        print(f"   CVEs addressed: {', '.join(details['security_patches'])}")

print(f"\n🛡️ Django Application Security Impact:")
for component, details in django_components.items():
    component_name = component.replace('_', ' ').title()
    affected_services = details['affected_services']
    
    # Count security updates affecting this component
    updates_affecting = []
    for service in affected_services:
        for update_component, update_details in security_updates.items():
            if service in update_details['services_affected']:
                updates_affecting.append(update_component)
    
    unique_updates = len(set(updates_affecting))
    
    print(f"\n   📊 {component_name} ({details['model']} model):")
    print(f"      Security updates affecting component: {unique_updates}")
    print(f"      Critical operations secured: {len(details['critical_operations'])}")
    print(f"      Operations: {', '.join(details['critical_operations'])}")
    
    security_reqs = details['security_requirements']
    print(f"      Security requirements: {', '.join(security_reqs)}")

# Pull operation simulation
pull_results = {
    "python:3.11.8-slim": {"status": "pulled", "size": "45.2MB", "new_layers": 3},
    "postgres:15.6": {"status": "pulled", "size": "142.8MB", "new_layers": 5}, 
    "redis:7.0.15": {"status": "pulled", "size": "31.4MB", "new_layers": 2},
    "nginx:1.25.3": {"status": "pulled", "size": "67.1MB", "new_layers": 4}
}

print(f"\n📦 Image Pull Results:")
total_size = sum(float(result["size"].replace("MB", "")) for result in pull_results.values())
total_layers = sum(result["new_layers"] for result in pull_results.values())

for image, result in pull_results.items():
    status_icon = "✅" if result["status"] == "pulled" else "❌"
    print(f"   {status_icon} {image}: {result['status']} ({result['size']}, {result['new_layers']} new layers)")

print(f"\n   Total download: {total_size:.1f}MB, {total_layers} new layers")

print(f"\n🎯 Security Update Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ All critical security patches applied")
print(f"   ✅ Django application components secured")
print(f"   ✅ Infrastructure vulnerabilities addressed") 
print(f"   ✅ Production deployment ready with latest security updates")

print(f"\n⚡ Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Run 'docker compose down' to stop current services")
print(f"   2. Run 'docker compose up --build' to deploy with updated images")
print(f"   3. Verify Django application functionality with security updates")
print(f"   4. Monitor logs for any compatibility issues with updated stack")
python:3.11.8-slim: Pulling from library/python postgres:15.6: Pulling from library/postgres redis:7.0.15: Pulling from library/redis nginx:1.25.3: Pulling from library/nginx python:3.11.8-slim: Pull complete postgres:15.6: Pull complete redis:7.0.15: Pull complete nginx:1.25.3: Pull complete Django Stack Security Update Analysis - Docker Compose Pull: ====================================================================== Security scan date: 2026-01-11 15:30:45 🔒 Security Overview: Total vulnerabilities patched: 5 High priority updates: 2/4 components 🔴 Python Base: Version update: 3.11.7 → 3.11.8 Security patches: 2 CVEs Services affected: web, worker, scheduler CVEs addressed: CVE-2024-0001, CVE-2024-0002 🟡 Postgresql: Version update: 15.4 → 15.6 Security patches: 1 CVEs Services affected: db CVEs addressed: CVE-2024-0010 🟡 Redis: Version update: 7.0.12 → 7.0.15 Security patches: 1 CVEs Services affected: redis, cache CVEs addressed: CVE-2024-0020 🔴 Nginx: Version update: 1.24.0 → 1.25.3 Security patches: 1 CVEs Services affected: nginx, proxy CVEs addressed: CVE-2024-0030 🛡️ Django Application Security Impact: 📊 User Auth (User model): Security updates affecting component: 3 Critical operations secured: 3 Operations: login, password_reset, session_management Security requirements: secure_sessions, password_hashing, csrf_protection 📊 Job Posting (JobPost model): Security updates affecting component: 2 Critical operations secured: 3 Operations: job_creation, application_submission, search_queries Security requirements: input_validation, sql_injection_protection, xss_prevention 📦 Image Pull Results: ✅ python:3.11.8-slim: pulled (45.2MB, 3 new layers) ✅ postgres:15.6: pulled (142.8MB, 5 new layers) ✅ redis:7.0.15: pulled (31.4MB, 2 new layers) ✅ nginx:1.25.3: pulled (67.1MB, 4 new layers) Total download: 286.5MB, 14 new layers 🎯 Security Update Summary: ✅ All critical security patches applied ✅ Django application components secured ✅ Infrastructure vulnerabilities addressed ✅ Production deployment ready with latest security updates

docker compose restart

Official Docs: docker compose restart | Docker CLI: docker compose restart

Restart running services to apply configuration changes and refresh service state.

Full Description

The docker compose restart command stops and starts existing containers for your anihpj Django services. This is useful for applying environment variable changes, refreshing service connections, clearing temporary states, and implementing configuration updates without full container recreation.

For Django applications, restarting services helps apply new settings, refresh database connections, reload application code (in development), clear cache states, and resolve temporary networking or performance issues in your anihpj jobpost application stack.

Syntax

# Restart all services
docker compose restart

# Restart specific service
docker compose restart web

# Restart with timeout
docker compose restart --timeout 30 web

# Restart multiple services
docker compose restart web db redis

# Restart with no timeout (immediate)
docker compose restart --timeout 0 worker

# Sequential restart of services
docker compose restart db && docker compose restart web

# Restart all except specific services
docker compose restart $(docker compose ps --services | grep -v db)

Parameters Description

  • -t, --timeout: Set stop timeout in seconds before force killing. Allows Django applications to complete current requests gracefully.
  • Service Names: Specify which Django services to restart. Target specific components without affecting entire stack.
  • Multiple Services: Restart several related Django services together. Coordinate database, cache, and web service restarts.
  • Zero Timeout: Immediate restart without graceful shutdown. Use for Django services that need immediate state reset.
  • Dependency Order: Restart services in dependency order. Ensure Django database services start before web applications.

Attributes

  • State Preservation: Maintains container configuration while refreshing Django service processes and connections.
  • Network Continuity: Preserves Django service networking and inter-service communication during restart cycles.
  • Volume Persistence: Django data volumes remain intact during restart operations, preserving database and file states.
  • Graceful Shutdown: Allows Django applications to complete in-flight requests before stopping services.
  • Quick Recovery: Faster than full recreation, ideal for Django configuration updates and state refreshes.

Methods

Configuration Refresh

Syntax: docker compose restart --timeout 60

Parameters: --timeout (integer) - Grace period for Django application shutdown

Apply Django configuration changes with graceful restart allowing current operations to complete safely.

Service Health Reset

Syntax: docker compose restart db redis web

Parameters: Service list in dependency order for Django stack restart

Reset Django service health by restarting in proper dependency sequence for optimal recovery.

Return Value

Returns restart status for each Django service showing stop and start completion. Indicates any failures during restart cycles.

  • Success (0): All specified services restarted successfully
  • Error (1): Service restart failure or timeout issues
  • Partial Success: Some services restarted, others failed

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Production Configuration Refresh (Environment Variables Update)

Apply new Django environment variables and configuration changes to production services without full downtime, affecting User authentication and JobPost functionality.

# Restart web services with new configuration
docker compose restart --timeout 60 web

# Restart background workers for job processing
docker compose restart worker scheduler

# Verify restart success and health
docker compose ps

Django Models Context & Configuration Impact:

# Configuration refresh impact on Django models and services
# User authentication, JobPost processing, and system performance

import datetime
from typing import Dict, List

# Configuration changes requiring service restart
configuration_updates = {
    "database_settings": {
        "changes": [
            "CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE: 10 → 20",
            "QUERY_TIMEOUT: 30s → 45s", 
            "MAX_CONNECTIONS: 100 → 150"
        ],
        "affected_models": ["User", "Author", "Skills", "Location", "JobPost"],
        "restart_services": ["web", "worker"],
        "impact": "improved_performance"
    },
    "cache_configuration": {
        "changes": [
            "REDIS_TIMEOUT: 300s → 600s",
            "CACHE_KEY_PREFIX: anihpj_v1 → anihpj_v2",
            "SESSION_CACHE_ALIAS: default → sessions"
        ],
        "affected_models": ["User", "JobPost"],
        "restart_services": ["web", "redis"],
        "impact": "enhanced_caching"
    },
    "security_settings": {
        "changes": [
            "SESSION_COOKIE_AGE: 3600 → 7200",
            "CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE: True",
            "SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT: True"
        ],
        "affected_models": ["User"],
        "restart_services": ["web"],
        "impact": "increased_security"
    },
    "job_processing": {
        "changes": [
            "CELERY_WORKER_CONCURRENCY: 4 → 8",
            "CELERY_TASK_TIMEOUT: 300s → 600s",
            "QUEUE_DEFAULT_PRIORITY: 5 → 7"
        ],
        "affected_models": ["JobPost", "Author"],
        "restart_services": ["worker", "scheduler"],
        "impact": "faster_job_processing"
    }
}

# Django service restart analysis
service_restart_plan = {
    "phase_1": {
        "services": ["redis"],
        "reason": "Cache configuration updates",
        "estimated_downtime": "5 seconds",
        "affected_operations": ["session_storage", "cache_retrieval"]
    },
    "phase_2": {
        "services": ["db"],
        "reason": "Database connection pool updates", 
        "estimated_downtime": "10 seconds",
        "affected_operations": ["all_model_operations", "query_performance"]
    },
    "phase_3": {
        "services": ["worker", "scheduler"],
        "reason": "Job processing configuration",
        "estimated_downtime": "15 seconds", 
        "affected_operations": ["background_jobs", "task_scheduling"]
    },
    "phase_4": {
        "services": ["web"],
        "reason": "Web server and security settings",
        "estimated_downtime": "20 seconds",
        "affected_operations": ["user_requests", "api_calls"]
    }
}

# Model operation impact during restart
model_operations = {
    "User": {
        "authentication": {"critical": True, "fallback": "cached_sessions"},
        "registration": {"critical": False, "fallback": "queue_requests"},
        "profile_updates": {"critical": False, "fallback": "retry_mechanism"}
    },
    "Author": {
        "profile_creation": {"critical": False, "fallback": "draft_save"},
        "skill_updates": {"critical": False, "fallback": "background_sync"},
        "rate_changes": {"critical": True, "fallback": "immediate_cache"}
    },
    "JobPost": {
        "job_creation": {"critical": True, "fallback": "draft_queue"},
        "application_submission": {"critical": True, "fallback": "email_backup"},
        "search_queries": {"critical": False, "fallback": "cached_results"}
    },
    "Skills": {
        "skill_verification": {"critical": False, "fallback": "manual_review"},
        "demand_analytics": {"critical": False, "fallback": "scheduled_update"}
    },
    "Location": {
        "geo_search": {"critical": False, "fallback": "approximate_results"},
        "market_analysis": {"critical": False, "fallback": "cached_data"}
    }
}

# Print configuration restart analysis
print("Django Configuration Refresh - Production Restart Analysis:")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"Restart initiated: {datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}")

print(f"\n🔧 Configuration Changes Applied:")
for config_area, details in configuration_updates.items():
    area_name = config_area.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   📊 {area_name}:")
    print(f"      Impact: {details['impact'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"      Models affected: {', '.join(details['affected_models'])}")
    print(f"      Services to restart: {', '.join(details['restart_services'])}")
    
    for change in details['changes']:
        print(f"      - {change}")

print(f"\n⚡ Restart Sequence Plan:")
total_estimated_downtime = 0
for phase, details in service_restart_plan.items():
    phase_name = phase.replace('_', ' ').title()
    downtime_seconds = int(details['estimated_downtime'].split()[0])
    total_estimated_downtime += downtime_seconds
    
    print(f"\n   🔄 {phase_name}:")
    print(f"      Services: {', '.join(details['services'])}")
    print(f"      Reason: {details['reason']}")
    print(f"      Downtime: {details['estimated_downtime']}")
    print(f"      Operations affected: {', '.join(details['affected_operations'])}")

print(f"\n📊 Model Operation Impact:")
for model_name, operations in model_operations.items():
    critical_ops = sum(1 for op in operations.values() if op['critical'])
    total_ops = len(operations)
    
    print(f"\n   🎯 {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Critical operations: {critical_ops}/{total_ops}")
    
    for operation, details in operations.items():
        critical_indicator = "🔴" if details['critical'] else "🟡"
        fallback_strategy = details['fallback'].replace('_', ' ').title()
        op_name = operation.replace('_', ' ').title()
        
        print(f"      {critical_indicator} {op_name}: Fallback = {fallback_strategy}")

# Restart execution simulation
restart_results = {
    "redis": {"status": "completed", "time": "4.2s", "health": "healthy"},
    "db": {"status": "completed", "time": "8.7s", "health": "healthy"},
    "worker": {"status": "completed", "time": "12.3s", "health": "healthy"},
    "scheduler": {"status": "completed", "time": "11.9s", "health": "healthy"},
    "web": {"status": "completed", "time": "18.4s", "health": "healthy"}
}

print(f"\n🚀 Restart Execution Results:")
for service, result in restart_results.items():
    status_icon = "✅" if result["status"] == "completed" else "❌"
    health_icon = "💚" if result["health"] == "healthy" else "💔"
    
    print(f"   {status_icon} {service}: {result['status']} in {result['time']} {health_icon}")

actual_total_time = sum(float(result["time"].replace("s", "")) for result in restart_results.values())
print(f"\n   Total restart time: {actual_total_time:.1f}s (estimated: {total_estimated_downtime}s)")

print(f"\n🎯 Configuration Refresh Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ All configuration changes applied successfully")
print(f"   ✅ All Django services restarted and healthy")
print(f"   ✅ Enhanced performance and security settings active")
print(f"   ✅ Job processing optimization enabled")
print(f"   ✅ Database connection pool expanded for higher load")

print(f"\n📈 Performance Improvements:")
print(f"   - Database connections: 50% increase (100→150)")
print(f"   - Cache timeout: 100% increase (300s→600s)") 
print(f"   - Worker concurrency: 100% increase (4→8 workers)")
print(f"   - Session security: Enhanced with longer duration")
Restarting anihpj_redis_1 ... done Restarting anihpj_db_1 ... done Restarting anihpj_worker_1 ... done Restarting anihpj_web_1 ... done Django Configuration Refresh - Production Restart Analysis: ================================================================= Restart initiated: 2026-01-11 15:45:30 🔧 Configuration Changes Applied: 📊 Database Settings: Impact: Improved Performance Models affected: User, Author, Skills, Location, JobPost Services to restart: web, worker - CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE: 10 → 20 - QUERY_TIMEOUT: 30s → 45s - MAX_CONNECTIONS: 100 → 150 📊 Cache Configuration: Impact: Enhanced Caching Models affected: User, JobPost Services to restart: web, redis - REDIS_TIMEOUT: 300s → 600s - CACHE_KEY_PREFIX: anihpj_v1 → anihpj_v2 - SESSION_CACHE_ALIAS: default → sessions ⚡ Restart Sequence Plan: 🔄 Phase 1: Services: redis Reason: Cache configuration updates Downtime: 5 seconds Operations affected: session_storage, cache_retrieval 🔄 Phase 4: Services: web Reason: Web server and security settings Downtime: 20 seconds Operations affected: user_requests, api_calls 📊 Model Operation Impact: 🎯 User Model: Critical operations: 1/3 🔴 Authentication: Fallback = Cached Sessions 🟡 Registration: Fallback = Queue Requests 🟡 Profile Updates: Fallback = Retry Mechanism 🎯 JobPost Model: Critical operations: 2/3 🔴 Job Creation: Fallback = Draft Queue 🔴 Application Submission: Fallback = Email Backup 🟡 Search Queries: Fallback = Cached Results 🚀 Restart Execution Results: ✅ redis: completed in 4.2s 💚 ✅ db: completed in 8.7s 💚 ✅ worker: completed in 12.3s 💚 ✅ scheduler: completed in 11.9s 💚 ✅ web: completed in 18.4s 💚 Total restart time: 55.5s (estimated: 50s) 🎯 Configuration Refresh Summary: ✅ All configuration changes applied successfully ✅ All Django services restarted and healthy ✅ Enhanced performance and security settings active ✅ Job processing optimization enabled ✅ Database connection pool expanded for higher load 📈 Performance Improvements: - Database connections: 50% increase (100→150) - Cache timeout: 100% increase (300s→600s) - Worker concurrency: 100% increase (4→8 workers) - Session security: Enhanced with longer duration

docker compose stop

Official Docs: docker compose stop | Docker CLI: docker compose stop

Stop running services gracefully without removing containers or losing data.

Full Description

The docker compose stop command gracefully stops running containers for your anihpj Django services without removing them or their data. This preserves container state, volumes, and configuration while halting service execution, making it perfect for maintenance windows, temporary shutdowns, or resource conservation.

For Django applications, stopping services maintains database data, preserves session information, keeps configuration intact, and allows for quick restart without container recreation. This is essential for planned maintenance, system updates, or temporary resource management in your anihpj jobpost application environment.

Syntax

# Stop all services
docker compose stop

# Stop specific service
docker compose stop web

# Stop with custom timeout
docker compose stop --timeout 60 web

# Stop multiple services
docker compose stop web worker db

# Stop immediately (no graceful shutdown)
docker compose stop --timeout 0 worker

# Stop services in reverse dependency order
docker compose stop web && docker compose stop db

# Stop all except specific services  
docker compose stop $(docker compose ps --services | grep -v redis)

Parameters Description

  • -t, --timeout: Set stop timeout before force kill. Allows Django applications to finish current requests and close connections gracefully.
  • Service Names: Specify which Django services to stop. Target specific components while keeping others running.
  • Zero Timeout: Immediate stop without graceful shutdown. Use for Django services that need immediate halting.
  • Multiple Services: Stop several Django services together. Coordinate shutdown of related application components.
  • Dependency Order: Stop services in proper sequence. Halt Django web services before stopping database dependencies.

Attributes

  • Container Preservation: Stops Django services without removing containers, maintaining configuration and state for restart.
  • Data Persistence: Preserves all Django database data, uploaded files, and application state during shutdown.
  • Graceful Shutdown: Allows Django applications to complete in-flight operations before stopping services.
  • Quick Recovery: Enables fast restart with preserved state, ideal for Django maintenance and updates.
  • Resource Management: Frees CPU and memory while maintaining Django service configuration and data.

Methods

Maintenance Window

Syntax: docker compose stop --timeout 120

Parameters: --timeout (integer) - Extended grace period for Django application shutdown

Initiate Django maintenance window with extended graceful shutdown for complex operations completion.

Selective Service Shutdown

Syntax: docker compose stop web worker

Parameters: Service list for selective Django component shutdown

Stop Django application services while keeping database and cache running for data consistency.

Return Value

Returns stop status for each Django service showing graceful shutdown completion. Indicates timeout or force-stop situations.

  • Success (0): All specified services stopped gracefully
  • Timeout: Services stopped after timeout period elapsed
  • Error (1): Service stop failure or container access issues

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Scheduled Maintenance Window (Graceful System Shutdown)

Implement planned maintenance window for Django system updates, database optimization, and security patches while preserving all data and configurations.

# Initiate maintenance mode with graceful shutdown
docker compose stop --timeout 180

# Check stop status and verify data preservation
docker compose ps -a

# Verify data volumes are intact
docker volume ls | grep anihpj

Django Models Context & Maintenance Planning:

# Maintenance window planning for Django application
# Ensuring data integrity during system shutdown

import datetime
from typing import Dict, List

# Pre-maintenance system state analysis
system_state = {
    "active_users": 1247,
    "jobs_posted_today": 89,
    "applications_pending": 342,
    "authors_online": 156,
    "skill_verifications_queue": 78,
    "location_searches_active": 23
}

# Django services and their shutdown priorities
service_shutdown_plan = {
    "nginx_proxy": {
        "priority": 1,
        "timeout": 30,
        "reason": "Stop new request acceptance",
        "impact": "No new users can access system",
        "data_safety": "No data loss risk"
    },
    "web_django": {
        "priority": 2,
        "timeout": 120,
        "reason": "Complete current user requests",
        "impact": "Active sessions finish gracefully",
        "data_safety": "All transactions complete"
    },
    "celery_worker": {
        "priority": 3,
        "timeout": 180,
        "reason": "Finish background job processing",
        "impact": "JobPost notifications and skill verifications complete",
        "data_safety": "No job processing data lost"
    },
    "celery_scheduler": {
        "priority": 4,
        "timeout": 60,
        "reason": "Stop scheduled task creation",
        "impact": "No new background tasks scheduled",
        "data_safety": "Scheduled tasks preserved for restart"
    },
    "redis_cache": {
        "priority": 5,
        "timeout": 30,
        "reason": "Flush cache and session data",
        "impact": "User sessions preserved to database",
        "data_safety": "Session data backed up"
    },
    "postgresql_db": {
        "priority": 6,
        "timeout": 300,
        "reason": "Complete all database transactions",
        "impact": "All model data safely persisted",
        "data_safety": "Critical - all Django model data preserved"
    }
}

# Model data preservation status
model_preservation = {
    "User": {
        "total_records": 1247,
        "critical_data": ["authentication_tokens", "profile_data", "preferences"],
        "preservation_method": "database_persistence",
        "backup_status": "automated_hourly"
    },
    "Author": {
        "total_records": 823,
        "critical_data": ["portfolio_links", "skill_ratings", "hourly_rates"],
        "preservation_method": "database_persistence", 
        "backup_status": "automated_hourly"
    },
    "Skills": {
        "total_records": 2156,
        "critical_data": ["verification_status", "demand_metrics", "proficiency_levels"],
        "preservation_method": "database_persistence",
        "backup_status": "automated_hourly"
    },
    "Location": {
        "total_records": 487,
        "critical_data": ["geographic_coordinates", "market_scores", "timezone_data"],
        "preservation_method": "database_persistence",
        "backup_status": "automated_hourly"
    },
    "JobPost": {
        "total_records": 3429,
        "critical_data": ["application_submissions", "view_analytics", "posting_status"],
        "preservation_method": "database_persistence",
        "backup_status": "automated_hourly"
    }
}

# Maintenance activities during shutdown
maintenance_activities = {
    "database_optimization": {
        "duration": "45 minutes",
        "activities": ["VACUUM ANALYZE", "index_rebuild", "statistics_update"],
        "models_affected": ["JobPost", "User", "Skills"],
        "performance_improvement": "25% query speed increase expected"
    },
    "security_updates": {
        "duration": "30 minutes",
        "activities": ["system_patches", "dependency_updates", "certificate_renewal"],
        "models_affected": ["User"],
        "security_improvement": "Latest CVE patches applied"
    },
    "storage_optimization": {
        "duration": "20 minutes", 
        "activities": ["log_rotation", "temp_file_cleanup", "volume_optimization"],
        "models_affected": ["All models"],
        "storage_improvement": "15% disk space recovery"
    }
}

# Print maintenance window analysis
print("Django Maintenance Window - Graceful Shutdown Analysis:")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"Maintenance started: {datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}")

print(f"\n📊 Pre-Maintenance System State:")
print(f"   Active users: {system_state['active_users']:,}")
print(f"   Jobs posted today: {system_state['jobs_posted_today']}")
print(f"   Pending applications: {system_state['applications_pending']}")
print(f"   Authors online: {system_state['authors_online']}")
print(f"   Skill verifications in queue: {system_state['skill_verifications_queue']}")

print(f"\n⚡ Service Shutdown Sequence:")
for service, details in service_shutdown_plan.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   {details['priority']}. {service_name} (Timeout: {details['timeout']}s)")
    print(f"      Reason: {details['reason']}")
    print(f"      Impact: {details['impact']}")
    print(f"      Data Safety: {details['data_safety']}")

print(f"\n🛡️ Data Preservation Status:")
total_records = sum(model['total_records'] for model in model_preservation.values())
print(f"   Total records across all models: {total_records:,}")

for model_name, details in model_preservation.items():
    print(f"\n   📋 {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Records: {details['total_records']:,}")
    print(f"      Critical data: {', '.join(details['critical_data'])}")
    print(f"      Preservation: {details['preservation_method'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"      Backup: {details['backup_status'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🔧 Planned Maintenance Activities:")
total_maintenance_time = sum(
    int(activity['duration'].split()[0]) 
    for activity in maintenance_activities.values()
)

for activity_name, details in maintenance_activities.items():
    activity_title = activity_name.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   ⚙️ {activity_title} ({details['duration']}):")
    print(f"      Activities: {', '.join(details['activities'])}")
    print(f"      Models affected: {', '.join(details['models_affected'])}")
    
    if 'performance_improvement' in details:
        print(f"      Improvement: {details['performance_improvement']}")
    if 'security_improvement' in details:
        print(f"      Security: {details['security_improvement']}")
    if 'storage_improvement' in details:
        print(f"      Storage: {details['storage_improvement']}")

print(f"\n   Total estimated maintenance time: {total_maintenance_time} minutes")

# Shutdown execution simulation
shutdown_results = {
    "nginx_proxy": {"stopped": True, "time": "28s", "graceful": True},
    "web_django": {"stopped": True, "time": "115s", "graceful": True},
    "celery_worker": {"stopped": True, "time": "175s", "graceful": True},
    "celery_scheduler": {"stopped": True, "time": "45s", "graceful": True},
    "redis_cache": {"stopped": True, "time": "25s", "graceful": True},
    "postgresql_db": {"stopped": True, "time": "285s", "graceful": True}
}

print(f"\n🛑 Shutdown Execution Results:")
for service, result in shutdown_results.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_', ' ').title()
    status_icon = "✅" if result["stopped"] else "❌"
    graceful_icon = "💚" if result["graceful"] else "💔"
    
    print(f"   {status_icon} {service_name}: Stopped in {result['time']} {graceful_icon}")

print(f"\n🎯 Maintenance Window Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ All services stopped gracefully")
print(f"   ✅ All data preserved and backed up") 
print(f"   ✅ Zero data loss during shutdown")
print(f"   ✅ System ready for maintenance activities")
print(f"   ✅ Expected improvements: 25% performance, enhanced security")

print(f"\n📝 Post-Maintenance Checklist:")
print(f"   □ Database optimization completion")
print(f"   □ Security patches verification") 
print(f"   □ Storage cleanup validation")
print(f"   □ Service restart and health checks")
print(f"   □ Application functionality testing")
Stopping anihpj_nginx_1 ... done Stopping anihpj_web_1 ... done Stopping anihpj_worker_1 ... done Stopping anihpj_scheduler_1 ... done Stopping anihpj_redis_1 ... done Stopping anihpj_db_1 ... done Django Maintenance Window - Graceful Shutdown Analysis: ============================================================ Maintenance started: 2026-01-11 16:00:15 📊 Pre-Maintenance System State: Active users: 1,247 Jobs posted today: 89 Pending applications: 342 Authors online: 156 Skill verifications in queue: 78 ⚡ Service Shutdown Sequence: 1. Nginx Proxy (Timeout: 30s) Reason: Stop new request acceptance Impact: No new users can access system Data Safety: No data loss risk 2. Web Django (Timeout: 120s) Reason: Complete current user requests Impact: Active sessions finish gracefully Data Safety: All transactions complete 6. Postgresql Db (Timeout: 300s) Reason: Complete all database transactions Impact: All model data safely persisted Data Safety: Critical - all Django model data preserved 🛡️ Data Preservation Status: Total records across all models: 8,142 📋 User Model: Records: 1,247 Critical data: authentication_tokens, profile_data, preferences Preservation: Database Persistence Backup: Automated Hourly 📋 JobPost Model: Records: 3,429 Critical data: application_submissions, view_analytics, posting_status Preservation: Database Persistence Backup: Automated Hourly 🔧 Planned Maintenance Activities: ⚙️ Database Optimization (45 minutes): Activities: VACUUM ANALYZE, index_rebuild, statistics_update Models affected: JobPost, User, Skills Improvement: 25% query speed increase expected ⚙️ Security Updates (30 minutes): Activities: system_patches, dependency_updates, certificate_renewal Models affected: User Security: Latest CVE patches applied Total estimated maintenance time: 95 minutes 🛑 Shutdown Execution Results: ✅ Nginx Proxy: Stopped in 28s 💚 ✅ Web Django: Stopped in 115s 💚 ✅ Celery Worker: Stopped in 175s 💚 ✅ Celery Scheduler: Stopped in 45s 💚 ✅ Redis Cache: Stopped in 25s 💚 ✅ Postgresql Db: Stopped in 285s 💚 🎯 Maintenance Window Summary: ✅ All services stopped gracefully ✅ All data preserved and backed up ✅ Zero data loss during shutdown ✅ System ready for maintenance activities ✅ Expected improvements: 25% performance, enhanced security

docker compose start

Official Docs: docker compose start | Docker CLI: docker compose start

Start existing stopped containers to resume Django services with preserved state.

Full Description

The docker compose start command starts previously stopped containers for your anihpj Django services. Unlike 'up', this command only starts existing containers without creating new ones, preserving all previous configurations, data states, and service relationships from before the shutdown.

For Django applications, starting stopped services restores your application stack to its previous state, reconnects to existing databases, resumes job processing, and continues serving user requests exactly where it left off. This is perfect for resuming after maintenance windows, system reboots, or planned downtime in your anihpj jobpost application environment.

Syntax

# Start all stopped services
docker compose start

# Start specific service
docker compose start web

# Start multiple services
docker compose start web db redis

# Start services in dependency order
docker compose start db && docker compose start web

# Start with verification
docker compose start && docker compose ps

# Start and follow logs
docker compose start && docker compose logs -f

# Start all except specific services
docker compose start $(docker compose ps --services --filter status=exited | grep -v worker)

Parameters Description

  • Service Names: Specify which Django services to start. Target specific components for selective restoration.
  • Multiple Services: Start several related Django services together. Coordinate database, cache, and application service startup.
  • Dependency Order: Start services in proper sequence. Ensure Django database services start before web applications.
  • State Preservation: Maintains all Django service configuration and data from before shutdown.
  • Quick Recovery: Fast startup since containers already exist with preserved Django application state.

Attributes

  • Container Reuse: Uses existing Django containers with preserved configuration, environment variables, and mount points.
  • State Continuity: Maintains Django application state including database connections, cache entries, and session data.
  • Fast Startup: Quick service restoration without image pulling or container creation for Django applications.
  • Data Integrity: Preserves all Django model data, uploaded files, and application state from before shutdown.
  • Network Preservation: Maintains Django service networking and inter-service communication configuration.

Methods

Post-Maintenance Startup

Syntax: docker compose start db && sleep 10 && docker compose start web

Parameters: Staged startup with delay for Django database initialization

Resume Django services after maintenance with proper dependency startup sequence for optimal recovery.

Selective Service Recovery

Syntax: docker compose start web redis

Parameters: Specific service list for selective Django component restoration

Start critical Django services while keeping other components stopped for testing or resource management.

Return Value

Returns start status for each Django service showing successful restoration. Indicates any startup failures or dependency issues.

  • Success (0): All specified services started successfully
  • Error (1): Service start failure or container access issues
  • Dependency Errors: Required services not running for Django application startup

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Post-Maintenance Recovery (Complete System Restoration)

Restore Django application stack after scheduled maintenance, ensuring all models and services resume with preserved data and optimal startup sequence.

# Start database first for Django models
docker compose start db

# Wait for database readiness, then start web services  
sleep 15 && docker compose start web worker

# Verify all services are healthy
docker compose ps && docker compose logs --tail 50

Django Models Context & Recovery Process:

# Post-maintenance recovery analysis for Django application
# Restoring User, Author, Skills, Location, JobPost services

import datetime
from typing import Dict, List

# Pre-maintenance snapshot preserved during shutdown
preserved_state = {
    "maintenance_start": "2026-01-11 16:00:15",
    "system_state": {
        "active_users": 1247,
        "jobs_posted_today": 89,
        "applications_pending": 342,
        "authors_online": 156,
        "skill_verifications_queue": 78,
        "location_searches_active": 23
    },
    "database_records": {
        "User": 1247,
        "Author": 823, 
        "Skills": 2156,
        "Location": 487,
        "JobPost": 3429
    }
}

# Service startup sequence for optimal Django recovery
startup_sequence = {
    "phase_1_infrastructure": {
        "services": ["postgresql_db", "redis_cache"],
        "startup_time": "30 seconds",
        "purpose": "Restore core data and cache infrastructure",
        "health_checks": ["database_connectivity", "cache_responsiveness"],
        "critical_for": ["All Django models", "Session management"]
    },
    "phase_2_core_services": {
        "services": ["web_django", "nginx_proxy"], 
        "startup_time": "45 seconds",
        "purpose": "Restore Django web application and request routing",
        "health_checks": ["django_ready", "url_routing", "admin_access"],
        "critical_for": ["User authentication", "JobPost operations"]
    },
    "phase_3_background": {
        "services": ["celery_worker", "celery_scheduler"],
        "startup_time": "20 seconds", 
        "purpose": "Resume background job processing and task scheduling",
        "health_checks": ["worker_connectivity", "task_processing"],
        "critical_for": ["JobPost notifications", "Skills verification"]
    },
    "phase_4_monitoring": {
        "services": ["elasticsearch", "monitoring"],
        "startup_time": "35 seconds",
        "purpose": "Restore search functionality and system monitoring", 
        "health_checks": ["search_indexing", "metrics_collection"],
        "critical_for": ["Location search", "Performance monitoring"]
    }
}

# Model data verification after startup
data_verification = {
    "User": {
        "verification_queries": [
            "Count active users",
            "Verify authentication tokens",
            "Check session validity"
        ],
        "expected_count": 1247,
        "critical_fields": ["username", "email", "last_login"],
        "integrity_checks": ["unique_constraints", "password_hashes"]
    },
    "Author": {
        "verification_queries": [
            "Count author profiles", 
            "Verify skill associations",
            "Check availability status"
        ],
        "expected_count": 823,
        "critical_fields": ["expertise_level", "hourly_rate", "is_available"],
        "integrity_checks": ["profile_completeness", "rate_validation"]
    },
    "JobPost": {
        "verification_queries": [
            "Count active job posts",
            "Verify application counts", 
            "Check posting status"
        ],
        "expected_count": 3429,
        "critical_fields": ["title", "company_name", "application_deadline"],
        "integrity_checks": ["status_consistency", "application_tracking"]
    },
    "Skills": {
        "verification_queries": [
            "Count skill records",
            "Verify demand metrics",
            "Check verification status"
        ], 
        "expected_count": 2156,
        "critical_fields": ["skill_name", "proficiency_level", "industry_demand"],
        "integrity_checks": ["unique_skills", "demand_calculations"]
    },
    "Location": {
        "verification_queries": [
            "Count location records",
            "Verify coordinates",
            "Check market scores"
        ],
        "expected_count": 487,
        "critical_fields": ["location_name", "latitude", "longitude"],
        "integrity_checks": ["coordinate_validation", "market_scoring"]
    }
}

# Recovery process execution
recovery_execution = {
    "database_startup": {
        "duration": "28s",
        "status": "successful", 
        "records_verified": 8142,
        "integrity_check": "passed"
    },
    "django_startup": {
        "duration": "42s",
        "status": "successful",
        "migrations_status": "up_to_date", 
        "admin_accessible": True
    },
    "cache_restoration": {
        "duration": "15s",
        "status": "successful",
        "cache_hit_rate": "0% (cold start)",
        "session_recovery": "successful"
    },
    "background_services": {
        "duration": "18s", 
        "status": "successful",
        "queue_processing": "resumed",
        "scheduled_tasks": "reactivated"
    }
}

# Print post-maintenance recovery analysis
print("Django Post-Maintenance Recovery - Service Startup Analysis:")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"Recovery started: {datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}")
print(f"Downtime duration: {(datetime.datetime.now() - datetime.datetime.strptime(preserved_state['maintenance_start'], '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')).total_seconds() / 3600:.1f} hours")

print(f"\n📊 Preserved State Verification:")
print(f"   Database records maintained: {sum(preserved_state['database_records'].values()):,}")
for model, count in preserved_state['database_records'].items():
    print(f"   - {model}: {count:,} records")

print(f"\n⚡ Service Startup Sequence:")
total_startup_time = 0
for phase, details in startup_sequence.items():
    phase_name = phase.replace('_', ' ').title()
    startup_seconds = int(details['startup_time'].split()[0])
    total_startup_time += startup_seconds
    
    print(f"\n   🔄 {phase_name} ({details['startup_time']}):")
    print(f"      Services: {', '.join(details['services'])}")
    print(f"      Purpose: {details['purpose']}")
    print(f"      Health checks: {', '.join(details['health_checks'])}")
    print(f"      Critical for: {details['critical_for']}")

print(f"\n   Total estimated startup time: {total_startup_time} seconds")

print(f"\n🔍 Model Data Verification:")
for model_name, verification in data_verification.items():
    print(f"\n   📋 {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Expected records: {verification['expected_count']:,}")
    print(f"      Verification queries: {len(verification['verification_queries'])}")
    print(f"      Critical fields: {', '.join(verification['critical_fields'])}")
    print(f"      Integrity checks: {', '.join(verification['integrity_checks'])}")

print(f"\n🚀 Recovery Execution Results:")
for component, result in recovery_execution.items():
    component_name = component.replace('_', ' ').title()
    status_icon = "✅" if result["status"] == "successful" else "❌"
    
    print(f"\n   {status_icon} {component_name}: {result['status']} in {result['duration']}")
    
    if 'records_verified' in result:
        print(f"      Records verified: {result['records_verified']:,}")
    if 'migrations_status' in result:
        print(f"      Migrations: {result['migrations_status'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    if 'cache_hit_rate' in result:
        print(f"      Cache status: {result['cache_hit_rate']}")
    if 'queue_processing' in result:
        print(f"      Queue processing: {result['queue_processing'].title()}")

# System health post-recovery
health_metrics = {
    "response_time": "145ms (normal: 120-180ms)",
    "database_connections": "24/150 (healthy)",
    "cache_utilization": "2% (rebuilding)",
    "active_sessions": "0 (expected after maintenance)",
    "background_jobs": "3 queued, processing resumed"
}

print(f"\n💚 Post-Recovery Health Status:")
for metric, value in health_metrics.items():
    metric_name = metric.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"   ✓ {metric_name}: {value}")

print(f"\n🎯 Recovery Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ All Django services started successfully")
print(f"   ✅ All model data preserved and verified")
print(f"   ✅ Database integrity confirmed")
print(f"   ✅ Application functionality restored")
print(f"   ✅ Background processing resumed")
print(f"   ✅ System ready for user traffic")

print(f"\n📈 Post-Maintenance Improvements:")
print(f"   - Database performance: 25% query speed improvement")
print(f"   - Security patches: All CVEs addressed")
print(f"   - Storage optimization: 15% disk space recovered")
print(f"   - System stability: Enhanced monitoring active")

User: 1,247 records - Author: 823 records - Skills: 2,156 records - Location: 487 records - JobPost: 3,429 records ⚡ Service Startup Sequence: 🔄 Phase 1 Infrastructure (30 seconds): Services: postgresql_db, redis_cache Purpose: Restore core data and cache infrastructure Health checks: database_connectivity, cache_responsiveness Critical for: ['All Django models', 'Session management'] 🔄 Phase 2 Core Services (45 seconds): Services: web_django, nginx_proxy Purpose: Restore Django web application and request routing Health checks: django_ready, url_routing, admin_access Critical for: ['User authentication', 'JobPost operations'] Total estimated startup time: 130 seconds 🔍 Model Data Verification: 📋 User Model: Expected records: 1,247 Verification queries: 3 Critical fields: username, email, last_login Integrity checks: unique_constraints, password_hashes 📋 JobPost Model: Expected records: 3,429 Verification queries: 3 Critical fields: title, company_name, application_deadline Integrity checks: status_consistency, application_tracking 🚀 Recovery Execution Results: ✅ Database Startup: successful in 28s Records verified: 8,142 ✅ Django Startup: successful in 42s Migrations: Up To Date ✅ Cache Restoration: successful in 15s Cache status: 0% (cold start) ✅ Background Services: successful in 18s Queue processing: Resumed 💚 Post-Recovery Health Status: ✓ Response Time: 145ms (normal: 120-180ms) ✓ Database Connections: 24/150 (healthy) ✓ Cache Utilization: 2% (rebuilding) ✓ Active Sessions: 0 (expected after maintenance) ✓ Background Jobs: 3 queued, processing resumed 🎯 Recovery Summary: ✅ All Django services started successfully ✅ All model data preserved and verified ✅ Database integrity confirmed ✅ Application functionality restored ✅ Background processing resumed ✅ System ready for user traffic
Example 2: Production Status Monitoring (Service Health Dashboard)

Monitor production and staging environments for anihpj Django application with comprehensive service health checks, User authentication status, JobPost processing metrics, Skills API performance, Author content management, and Location services availability.

# Monitor production services health
docker compose ls --filter status=running --format table

# Check all environments including stopped ones
docker compose ls --all --format json | jq '.[] | {name, status, services: .services | length}'

# Filter specific project patterns
docker compose ls --filter name=anihpj* --quiet

Django Production Monitoring Context:

# Production environment monitoring for Django anihpj application
# Monitor all models: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location across environments

import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Production environment status monitoring
production_status = {
    "monitoring_timestamp": "2026-01-12 16:45:30",
    "environment_scope": "production_health_check",
    "critical_services_monitored": ["web", "db", "redis", "worker", "nginx"]
}

# Production environment projects
production_environments = {
    "anihpj-production": {
        "name": "anihpj-production",
        "status": "running",
        "uptime": "72h 15m",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.prod.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/srv/anihpj-prod",
        "services_running": 8,
        "services_total": 8,
        "environment_type": "production",
        "load_balancer": "enabled",
        "auto_scaling": "active",
        "service_health": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "instances": 3, "health": "healthy", "cpu": "35%"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "instances": 1, "health": "healthy", "connections": "45/100"},
            "redis": {"status": "running", "instances": 2, "health": "healthy", "memory": "67%"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "instances": 4, "health": "healthy", "queue": "23 jobs"},
