            "nginx": {"status": "running", "instances": 2, "health": "healthy", "requests": "1,247/min"},
            "monitoring": {"status": "running", "instances": 1, "health": "healthy", "alerts": "none"},
            "backup": {"status": "running", "instances": 1, "health": "healthy", "last_backup": "2h ago"},
            "log_aggregator": {"status": "running", "instances": 1, "health": "healthy", "log_rate": "150/sec"}
        },
        "model_performance": {
            "User_model": {
                "active_sessions": 245,
                "authentication_rate": "98.5%",
                "avg_response_time": "45ms",
                "peak_concurrent_users": 1200
            },
            "JobPost_model": {
                "active_listings": 3429,
                "applications_today": 567,
                "search_performance": "78ms avg",
                "matching_accuracy": "94.2%"
            },
            "Skills_model": {
                "total_skills": 2156,
                "api_calls_per_minute": 890,
                "search_latency": "23ms",
                "cache_hit_rate": "89%"
            },
            "Author_model": {
                "active_authors": 823,
                "content_published_today": 45,
                "moderation_queue": 12,
                "profile_completion_rate": "78%"
            },
            "Location_model": {
                "geocoding_requests": 234,
                "accuracy_rate": "96.7%",
                "proximity_searches": 156,
                "mapping_performance": "95ms avg"
            }
        }
    },
    "anihpj-staging": {
        "name": "anihpj-staging", 
        "status": "running",
        "uptime": "24h 8m",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.staging.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/srv/anihpj-staging",
        "services_running": 6,
        "services_total": 6,
        "environment_type": "staging",
        "deployment_testing": "active",
        "service_health": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "instances": 2, "health": "healthy", "cpu": "22%"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "instances": 1, "health": "healthy", "connections": "12/50"},
            "redis": {"status": "running", "instances": 1, "health": "healthy", "memory": "45%"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "instances": 2, "health": "healthy", "queue": "5 jobs"},
            "nginx": {"status": "running", "instances": 1, "health": "healthy", "requests": "89/min"},
            "selenium": {"status": "running", "instances": 1, "health": "healthy", "tests": "45 passed"}
        }
    }
}

print("Production Status Monitoring - Docker Compose LS Analysis:")
print(f"Monitoring initiated: {production_status['monitoring_timestamp']}")

# Overall environment health
total_services = 0
running_services = 0

for env_name, env_data in production_environments.items():
    total_services += env_data["services_total"]
    running_services += env_data["services_running"]
    
    status_icon = "🟢" if env_data["status"] == "running" else "🔴"
    uptime_hours = float(env_data["uptime"].split("h")[0])
    uptime_icon = "🚀" if uptime_hours > 48 else "⚡" if uptime_hours > 12 else "🔄"
    
    print(f"\n{status_icon} Environment: {env_name}")
    print(f"   {uptime_icon} Uptime: {env_data['uptime']} | Type: {env_data['environment_type'].title()}")
    print(f"   🔧 Services: {env_data['services_running']}/{env_data['services_total']} running")
    
    # Service health details
    if "service_health" in env_data:
        print(f"   📊 Service Health Overview:")
        for service, health in env_data["service_health"].items():
            health_icon = "✅" if health["health"] == "healthy" else "⚠️"
            instances = health.get("instances", 1)
            print(f"      {health_icon} {service.capitalize()}: {instances} instance(s) - {health['status']}")
            
            # Additional metrics
            if "cpu" in health:
                print(f"         💾 CPU: {health['cpu']}")
            if "connections" in health:
                print(f"         🔗 Connections: {health['connections']}")
            if "memory" in health:
                print(f"         🧠 Memory: {health['memory']}")
            if "queue" in health:
                print(f"         📋 Queue: {health['queue']}")
            if "requests" in health:
                print(f"         🌐 Requests: {health['requests']}")

# Model performance analysis for production
if "anihpj-production" in production_environments:
    prod_env = production_environments["anihpj-production"]
    if "model_performance" in prod_env:
        print(f"\n📈 Production Model Performance Analysis:")
        
        for model_name, metrics in prod_env["model_performance"].items():
            print(f"   🔸 {model_name}:")
            
            if "active_sessions" in metrics:
                print(f"      👥 Active Sessions: {metrics['active_sessions']:,}")
                print(f"      🎯 Authentication Rate: {metrics['authentication_rate']}")
                
            if "active_listings" in metrics:
                print(f"      📋 Active Listings: {metrics['active_listings']:,}")
                print(f"      📝 Applications Today: {metrics['applications_today']:,}")
                print(f"      🔍 Search Performance: {metrics['search_performance']}")
                
            if "total_skills" in metrics:
                print(f"      🏷️ Total Skills: {metrics['total_skills']:,}")
                print(f"      📊 API Calls/Min: {metrics['api_calls_per_minute']:,}")
                print(f"      💾 Cache Hit Rate: {metrics['cache_hit_rate']}")
                
            if "active_authors" in metrics:
                print(f"      ✍️ Active Authors: {metrics['active_authors']:,}")
                print(f"      📄 Content Published Today: {metrics['content_published_today']}")
                print(f"      👀 Moderation Queue: {metrics['moderation_queue']}")
                
            if "geocoding_requests" in metrics:
                print(f"      🗺️ Geocoding Requests: {metrics['geocoding_requests']}")
                print(f"      🎯 Accuracy Rate: {metrics['accuracy_rate']}")

# System health summary
overall_health = (running_services / total_services) * 100 if total_services > 0 else 0
health_icon = "🟢" if overall_health > 95 else "🟡" if overall_health > 85 else "🔴"

print(f"\n{health_icon} Overall System Health: {overall_health:.1f}% ({running_services}/{total_services} services)")
print(f"🔍 Environments Monitored: {len(production_environments)}")
print(f"💼 Django Models Active: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location")
print(f"⚡ Performance Status: All models operating within normal parameters")
anihpj-production running 8 /srv/anihpj-prod anihpj-staging running 6 /srv/anihpj-staging Production Status Monitoring - Docker Compose LS Analysis: Monitoring initiated: 2026-01-12 16:45:30 🟢 Environment: anihpj-production 🚀 Uptime: 72h 15m | Type: Production 🔧 Services: 8/8 running 📊 Service Health Overview: ✅ Web: 3 instance(s) - running 💾 CPU: 35% ✅ Db: 1 instance(s) - running 🔗 Connections: 45/100 ✅ Redis: 2 instance(s) - running 🧠 Memory: 67% ✅ Worker: 4 instance(s) - running 📋 Queue: 23 jobs ✅ Nginx: 2 instance(s) - running 🌐 Requests: 1,247/min 🟢 Environment: anihpj-staging ⚡ Uptime: 24h 8m | Type: Staging 🔧 Services: 6/6 running 📊 Service Health Overview: ✅ Web: 2 instance(s) - running 💾 CPU: 22% ✅ Selenium: 1 instance(s) - running 📈 Production Model Performance Analysis: 🔸 User_model: 👥 Active Sessions: 245 🎯 Authentication Rate: 98.5% 🔸 JobPost_model: 📋 Active Listings: 3,429 📝 Applications Today: 567 🔍 Search Performance: 78ms 🔸 Skills_model: 🏷️ Total Skills: 2,156 📊 API Calls/Min: 890 💾 Cache Hit Rate: 89% 🔸 Author_model: ✍️ Active Authors: 823 📄 Content Published Today: 45 👀 Moderation Queue: 12 🔸 Location_model: 🗺️ Geocoding Requests: 234 🎯 Accuracy Rate: 96.7% 🟢 Overall System Health: 100.0% (14/14 services) 🔍 Environments Monitored: 2 💼 Django Models Active: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location ⚡ Performance Status: All models operating within normal parameters
Example 3: Development Environment Cleanup (Project Lifecycle Management)

Manage development environment lifecycle with project cleanup, resource optimization, and development workspace organization for anihpj Django application including User testing environments, JobPost development instances, Skills prototyping, Author content staging, and Location service testing.

# List all projects including stopped ones for cleanup
docker compose ls --all

# Filter projects by status for cleanup identification
docker compose ls --filter status=exited --format json

# Identify development projects for optimization
docker compose ls --filter name=*-dev --format table

Django Development Cleanup Context:

# Development environment cleanup for Django anihpj application
# Manage User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model development environments

import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Development cleanup analysis
cleanup_analysis = {
    "analysis_timestamp": "2026-01-12 10:15:45", 
    "cleanup_scope": "development_environment_optimization",
    "target_models": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
    "cleanup_objectives": ["resource_optimization", "disk_cleanup", "container_management"]
}

# Development environments discovered
development_environments = {
    "anihpj-dev-main": {
        "name": "anihpj-dev-main",
        "status": "running",
        "created": "2026-01-10T09:00:00Z",
        "last_activity": "2026-01-12T09:45:00Z",
        "services_total": 5,
        "services_running": 5,
        "disk_usage": "2.4GB",
        "environment_type": "active_development",
        "developer": "team_lead",
        "models_tested": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "cleanup_priority": "keep",
        "cleanup_reason": "active_primary_development"
    },
    "anihpj-dev-feature-auth": {
        "name": "anihpj-dev-feature-auth", 
        "status": "exited",
        "created": "2026-01-08T14:30:00Z",
        "last_activity": "2026-01-09T18:20:00Z",
        "services_total": 4,
        "services_running": 0,
        "disk_usage": "1.8GB",
        "environment_type": "feature_branch",
        "developer": "sarah_dev",
        "models_tested": ["User", "Author"],
        "feature_branch": "feature/enhanced-user-auth",
        "cleanup_priority": "archive",
        "cleanup_reason": "feature_merged_3_days_ago"
    },
    "anihpj-dev-jobpost-api": {
        "name": "anihpj-dev-jobpost-api",
        "status": "exited", 
        "created": "2026-01-07T11:15:00Z",
        "last_activity": "2026-01-08T16:45:00Z",
        "services_total": 6,
        "services_running": 0,
        "disk_usage": "3.1GB",
        "environment_type": "api_development",
        "developer": "alex_backend",
        "models_tested": ["JobPost", "Skills", "Location"],
        "api_version": "v2.1",
        "cleanup_priority": "remove",
        "cleanup_reason": "api_development_completed_deployed"
    },
    "anihpj-dev-skills-prototype": {
        "name": "anihpj-dev-skills-prototype",
        "status": "running",
        "created": "2026-01-11T15:00:00Z", 
        "last_activity": "2026-01-12T09:30:00Z",
        "services_total": 3,
        "services_running": 2,
        "disk_usage": "1.2GB",
        "environment_type": "prototype",
        "developer": "emma_frontend",
        "models_tested": ["Skills", "User"],
        "prototype_status": "active_testing",
        "cleanup_priority": "optimize",
        "cleanup_reason": "reduce_resource_usage_1_service_down"
    },
    "anihpj-dev-location-testing": {
        "name": "anihpj-dev-location-testing",
        "status": "paused",
        "created": "2026-01-09T13:20:00Z",
        "last_activity": "2026-01-11T14:15:00Z", 
        "services_total": 4,
        "services_running": 0,
        "disk_usage": "2.0GB",
        "environment_type": "geospatial_testing",
        "developer": "tom_gis",
        "models_tested": ["Location", "JobPost"],
        "testing_phase": "geospatial_algorithms",
        "cleanup_priority": "resume_or_archive",
        "cleanup_reason": "paused_for_24h_needs_decision"
    }
}

print("Development Environment Cleanup - Docker Compose LS Analysis:")
print(f"Cleanup analysis started: {cleanup_analysis['analysis_timestamp']}")

# Environment categorization
environment_categories = {
    "keep": [],
    "optimize": [],
    "archive": [],
    "remove": [],
    "resume_or_archive": []
}

total_disk_usage = 0
active_environments = 0
inactive_environments = 0

for env_name, env_data in development_environments.items():
    cleanup_priority = env_data["cleanup_priority"]
    environment_categories[cleanup_priority].append(env_name)
    
    # Parse disk usage
    disk_size = float(env_data["disk_usage"].replace("GB", ""))
    total_disk_usage += disk_size
    
    # Count active vs inactive
    if env_data["status"] == "running":
        active_environments += 1
    else:
        inactive_environments += 1
    
    # Display environment details
    status_icon = "🟢" if env_data["status"] == "running" else "⏸️" if env_data["status"] == "paused" else "🔴"
    priority_icon = "🔒" if cleanup_priority == "keep" else "🔧" if cleanup_priority == "optimize" else "📦" if cleanup_priority == "archive" else "🗑️" if cleanup_priority == "remove" else "❓"
    
    print(f"\n{status_icon} {env_name}")
    print(f"   {priority_icon} Action: {cleanup_priority.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"   📊 Status: {env_data['status']} | Type: {env_data['environment_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"   💾 Disk Usage: {env_data['disk_usage']} | Services: {env_data['services_running']}/{env_data['services_total']}")
    print(f"   👤 Developer: {env_data['developer']}")
    
    # Show tested models
    models = ", ".join(env_data["models_tested"])
    print(f"   🧬 Models Tested: {models}")
    
    # Activity status
    last_activity = datetime.fromisoformat(env_data["last_activity"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    activity_age = datetime.now() - last_activity.replace(tzinfo=None)
    activity_days = activity_age.days
    
    activity_icon = "🔥" if activity_days == 0 else "⚡" if activity_days <= 1 else "🕒" if activity_days <= 3 else "🧊"
    print(f"   {activity_icon} Last Activity: {activity_days} day(s) ago")
    print(f"   📝 Reason: {env_data['cleanup_reason'].replace('_', ' ')}")

# Cleanup recommendations
print(f"\n📊 Cleanup Summary:")
print(f"   Total Environments: {len(development_environments)}")
print(f"   Active: {active_environments} | Inactive: {inactive_environments}")
print(f"   Total Disk Usage: {total_disk_usage:.1f}GB")

print(f"\n🔧 Recommended Cleanup Actions:")
for action, environments in environment_categories.items():
    if environments:
        action_icon = "🔒" if action == "keep" else "🔧" if action == "optimize" else "📦" if action == "archive" else "🗑️" if action == "remove" else "❓"
        print(f"   {action_icon} {action.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {len(environments)} environment(s)")
        
        if action == "remove":
            remove_size = sum(float(development_environments[env]["disk_usage"].replace("GB", "")) for env in environments)
            print(f"      💾 Disk Space Recoverable: {remove_size:.1f}GB")
        
        if action == "archive":
            archive_size = sum(float(development_environments[env]["disk_usage"].replace("GB", "")) for env in environments)
            print(f"      📦 Data to Archive: {archive_size:.1f}GB")

# Model-specific cleanup insights
model_usage = {}
for env_data in development_environments.values():
    for model in env_data["models_tested"]:
        if model not in model_usage:
            model_usage[model] = {"environments": 0, "active": 0, "total_disk": 0}
        model_usage[model]["environments"] += 1
        if env_data["status"] == "running":
            model_usage[model]["active"] += 1
        model_usage[model]["total_disk"] += float(env_data["disk_usage"].replace("GB", ""))

print(f"\n🧬 Model Development Usage Analysis:")
for model, usage in model_usage.items():
    active_ratio = (usage["active"] / usage["environments"]) * 100 if usage["environments"] > 0 else 0
    usage_icon = "🔥" if active_ratio > 60 else "⚡" if active_ratio > 30 else "💤"
    
    print(f"   {usage_icon} {model} Model:")
    print(f"      📊 Used in {usage['environments']} environments ({usage['active']} active)")
    print(f"      💾 Total Disk: {usage['total_disk']:.1f}GB | Activity: {active_ratio:.1f}%")

# Final recommendations
potential_savings = sum(float(development_environments[env]["disk_usage"].replace("GB", "")) 
                       for env in environment_categories["remove"])
print(f"\n💡 Optimization Potential:")
print(f"   💾 Immediate Disk Savings: {potential_savings:.1f}GB from cleanup")
print(f"   🔧 Service Optimization: {len(environment_categories['optimize'])} environments need attention")
print(f"   📦 Archive Candidates: {len(environment_categories['archive'])} environments for long-term storage")
anihpj-dev-main running 5 /workspace/anihpj-dev anihpj-dev-feature-auth exited 0 /workspace/feature-auth anihpj-dev-jobpost-api exited 0 /workspace/jobpost-api anihpj-dev-skills-prototype running 2 /workspace/skills-proto anihpj-dev-location-testing paused 0 /workspace/location-test Development Environment Cleanup - Docker Compose LS Analysis: Cleanup analysis started: 2026-01-12 10:15:45 🟢 anihpj-dev-main 🔒 Action: Keep 📊 Status: running | Type: Active Development 💾 Disk Usage: 2.4GB | Services: 5/5 👤 Developer: team_lead 🧬 Models Tested: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 🔥 Last Activity: 0 day(s) ago 📝 Reason: active primary development 🔴 anihpj-dev-feature-auth 📦 Action: Archive 📊 Status: exited | Type: Feature Branch 💾 Disk Usage: 1.8GB | Services: 0/4 👤 Developer: sarah_dev 🧬 Models Tested: User, Author 🧊 Last Activity: 3 day(s) ago 📝 Reason: feature merged 3 days ago 🔴 anihpj-dev-jobpost-api 🗑️ Action: Remove 📊 Status: exited | Type: Api Development 💾 Disk Usage: 3.1GB | Services: 0/6 👤 Developer: alex_backend 🧬 Models Tested: JobPost, Skills, Location 🧊 Last Activity: 4 day(s) ago 📝 Reason: api development completed deployed 📊 Cleanup Summary: Total Environments: 5 Active: 2 | Inactive: 3 Total Disk Usage: 10.5GB 🔧 Recommended Cleanup Actions: 🔒 Keep: 1 environment(s) 🔧 Optimize: 1 environment(s) 📦 Archive: 1 environment(s) 📦 Data to Archive: 1.8GB 🗑️ Remove: 1 environment(s) 💾 Disk Space Recoverable: 3.1GB ❓ Resume Or Archive: 1 environment(s) 🧬 Model Development Usage Analysis: 🔥 User Model: 📊 Used in 3 environments (1 active) 💾 Total Disk: 5.4GB | Activity: 33.3% 🔥 JobPost Model: 📊 Used in 3 environments (1 active) 💾 Total Disk: 7.5GB | Activity: 33.3% 💤 Skills Model: 📊 Used in 3 environments (2 active) 💾 Total Disk: 6.7GB | Activity: 66.7% 💡 Optimization Potential: 💾 Immediate Disk Savings: 3.1GB from cleanup 🔧 Service Optimization: 1 environments need attention 📦 Archive Candidates: 1 environments for long-term storage
Example 4: CI/CD Pipeline Integration (Automated Deployment Monitoring)

Monitor CI/CD pipeline environments for anihpj Django application with automated deployment tracking, User authentication testing, JobPost integration verification, Skills API validation, Author content workflows, and Location service deployment across build stages.

# Monitor CI/CD pipeline environments
docker compose ls --filter name=*-pipeline* --format json

# Check deployment environments status
docker compose ls --filter status=running | grep -E "(build|test|deploy)"

# List all pipeline stages for monitoring
docker compose ls --all | grep -E "(ci|cd|pipeline)"

Django CI/CD Pipeline Context:

# CI/CD pipeline monitoring for Django anihpj application
# Track User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model deployments across pipeline stages

import json
from datetime import datetime

# CI/CD pipeline monitoring
pipeline_monitoring = {
    "monitoring_timestamp": "2026-01-12 14:20:15",
    "pipeline_scope": "automated_deployment_tracking",
    "deployment_models": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
    "pipeline_stages": ["build", "test", "security_scan", "staging_deploy", "production_deploy"]
}

# CI/CD environment pipeline
cicd_environments = {
    "anihpj-pipeline-build": {
        "name": "anihpj-pipeline-build",
        "status": "running", 
        "pipeline_stage": "build",
        "build_number": "#1247",
        "commit_hash": "a7f9d2c",
        "branch": "main",
        "triggered_by": "push_event",
        "services_total": 3,
        "services_running": 3,
        "environment_type": "ci_build",
        "stage_duration": "4m 23s",
        "models_tested": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "build_status": {
            "docker_build": "completed",
            "dependency_install": "completed",
            "code_compilation": "in_progress",
            "unit_tests": "pending",
            "integration_tests": "pending"
        }
    },
    "anihpj-pipeline-test": {
        "name": "anihpj-pipeline-test",
        "status": "running",
        "pipeline_stage": "test",
        "build_number": "#1246", 
        "commit_hash": "b8e3a1f",
        "branch": "main",
        "triggered_by": "build_completion",
        "services_total": 7,
        "services_running": 7,
        "environment_type": "automated_testing",
        "stage_duration": "12m 45s",
        "models_tested": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "test_results": {
            "unit_tests": {"total": 1247, "passed": 1245, "failed": 2, "success_rate": "99.8%"},
            "integration_tests": {"total": 89, "passed": 87, "failed": 2, "success_rate": "97.8%"},
            "api_tests": {"total": 156, "passed": 156, "failed": 0, "success_rate": "100.0%"},
            "model_tests": {
                "User": {"tests": 234, "passed": 234, "coverage": "98.5%"},
                "JobPost": {"tests": 189, "passed": 189, "coverage": "96.2%"},
                "Skills": {"tests": 145, "passed": 143, "coverage": "94.8%"},
                "Author": {"tests": 123, "passed": 123, "coverage": "97.1%"},
                "Location": {"tests": 98, "passed": 96, "coverage": "95.4%"}
            }
        }
    },
    "anihpj-pipeline-security": {
        "name": "anihpj-pipeline-security",
        "status": "running",
        "pipeline_stage": "security_scan",
        "build_number": "#1245",
        "commit_hash": "c9f4b2e",
        "branch": "main",
        "triggered_by": "test_completion", 
        "services_total": 4,
        "services_running": 4,
        "environment_type": "security_analysis",
        "stage_duration": "8m 12s",
        "models_analyzed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "security_scans": {
            "vulnerability_scan": {"status": "completed", "critical": 0, "high": 1, "medium": 3, "low": 7},
            "dependency_check": {"status": "completed", "outdated": 2, "vulnerable": 1, "secure": 145},
            "code_analysis": {"status": "completed", "issues": 4, "security_score": "A-"},
            "secrets_scan": {"status": "completed", "secrets_found": 0, "false_positives": 2}
        }
    }
}

print("CI/CD Pipeline Monitoring - Docker Compose LS Analysis:")
print(f"Pipeline monitoring started: {pipeline_monitoring['monitoring_timestamp']}")

# Pipeline status overview
total_stages = len(cicd_environments)
active_stages = len([env for env in cicd_environments.values() if env["status"] == "running"])

print(f"\n🔄 Pipeline Overview:")
print(f"   Total Stages: {total_stages}")
print(f"   Active Stages: {active_stages}/{total_stages}")
print(f"   Models Deployed: {', '.join(pipeline_monitoring['deployment_models'])}")

# Stage-by-stage analysis
for env_name, env_data in cicd_environments.items():
    status_icon = "🟢" if env_data["status"] == "running" else "🔴" if env_data["status"] == "exited" else "🟡"
    stage_icon = "🔨" if env_data["pipeline_stage"] == "build" else "🧪" if env_data["pipeline_stage"] == "test" else "🛡️" if env_data["pipeline_stage"] == "security_scan" else "🚀"
    
    print(f"\n{status_icon} {env_name}")
    print(f"   {stage_icon} Stage: {env_data['pipeline_stage'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"   🏗️ Build: {env_data['build_number']} | Commit: {env_data['commit_hash']}")
    print(f"   ⏱️ Duration: {env_data['stage_duration']} | Services: {env_data['services_running']}/{env_data['services_total']}")
    
    # Stage-specific details
    if "build_status" in env_data:
        print(f"   🔨 Build Progress:")
        for task, status in env_data["build_status"].items():
            task_icon = "✅" if status == "completed" else "🔄" if status == "in_progress" else "⏳"
            print(f"      {task_icon} {task.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {status}")
    
    if "test_results" in env_data:
        print(f"   🧪 Test Results:")
        for test_type, results in env_data["test_results"].items():
            if isinstance(results, dict) and "success_rate" in results:
                rate = float(results["success_rate"].replace("%", ""))
                rate_icon = "✅" if rate >= 98 else "🟡" if rate >= 95 else "🔴"
                print(f"      {rate_icon} {test_type.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {results['success_rate']} ({results['passed']}/{results['total']})")
    
    if "security_scans" in env_data:
        print(f"   🛡️ Security Analysis:")
        for scan_type, results in env_data["security_scans"].items():
            scan_icon = "✅" if results["status"] == "completed" else "🔄"
            print(f"      {scan_icon} {scan_type.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {results['status']}")
            
            if "critical" in results:
                critical = results.get("critical", 0)
                high = results.get("high", 0)
                vuln_icon = "🔴" if critical > 0 else "🟡" if high > 0 else "🟢"
                print(f"         {vuln_icon} Vulnerabilities: {critical} critical, {high} high")

# Model test coverage analysis
if "anihpj-pipeline-test" in cicd_environments:
    test_env = cicd_environments["anihpj-pipeline-test"]
    if "test_results" in test_env and "model_tests" in test_env["test_results"]:
        print(f"\n🧬 Model Test Coverage Analysis:")
        
        for model, test_data in test_env["test_results"]["model_tests"].items():
            coverage = float(test_data["coverage"].replace("%", ""))
            coverage_icon = "🟢" if coverage >= 95 else "🟡" if coverage >= 90 else "🔴"
            
            print(f"   {coverage_icon} {model} Model:")
            print(f"      📊 Tests: {test_data['tests']} total, {test_data['passed']} passed")
            print(f"      📈 Coverage: {test_data['coverage']}")

# Pipeline efficiency metrics
total_duration_minutes = 0
for env_data in cicd_environments.values():
    duration_str = env_data["stage_duration"]
    minutes = int(duration_str.split("m")[0]) + (int(duration_str.split("m")[1].split("s")[0]) / 60)
    total_duration_minutes += minutes

print(f"\n📊 Pipeline Performance Metrics:")
print(f"   ⏱️ Total Pipeline Duration: {total_duration_minutes:.1f} minutes")
print(f"   🔧 Services Efficiency: {sum(env['services_running'] for env in cicd_environments.values())}/{sum(env['services_total'] for env in cicd_environments.values())} running")
print(f"   🎯 Overall Success Rate: High (based on test results)")
print(f"   🛡️ Security Status: 1 high vulnerability detected, action required")
anihpj-pipeline-build running 3 /ci/builds/1247 anihpj-pipeline-test running 7 /ci/tests/1246 anihpj-pipeline-security running 4 /ci/security/1245 CI/CD Pipeline Monitoring - Docker Compose LS Analysis: Pipeline monitoring started: 2026-01-12 14:20:15 🔄 Pipeline Overview: Total Stages: 3 Active Stages: 3/3 Models Deployed: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 🟢 anihpj-pipeline-build 🔨 Stage: Build 🏗️ Build: #1247 | Commit: a7f9d2c ⏱️ Duration: 4m 23s | Services: 3/3 🔨 Build Progress: ✅ Docker Build: completed ✅ Dependency Install: completed 🔄 Code Compilation: in_progress ⏳ Unit Tests: pending 🟢 anihpj-pipeline-test 🧪 Stage: Test 🏗️ Build: #1246 | Commit: b8e3a1f ⏱️ Duration: 12m 45s | Services: 7/7 🧪 Test Results: ✅ Unit Tests: 99.8% (1245/1247) 🟡 Integration Tests: 97.8% (87/89) ✅ Api Tests: 100.0% (156/156) 🟢 anihpj-pipeline-security 🛡️ Stage: Security Scan 🏗️ Build: #1245 | Commit: c9f4b2e ⏱️ Duration: 8m 12s | Services: 4/4 🛡️ Security Analysis: ✅ Vulnerability Scan: completed 🟡 Vulnerabilities: 0 critical, 1 high ✅ Dependency Check: completed ✅ Code Analysis: completed 🧬 Model Test Coverage Analysis: 🟢 User Model: 📊 Tests: 234 total, 234 passed 📈 Coverage: 98.5% 🟢 JobPost Model: 📊 Tests: 189 total, 189 passed 📈 Coverage: 96.2% 🟡 Skills Model: 📊 Tests: 145 total, 143 passed 📈 Coverage: 94.8% 📊 Pipeline Performance Metrics: ⏱️ Total Pipeline Duration: 25.3 minutes 🔧 Services Efficiency: 14/14 running 🎯 Overall Success Rate: High (based on test results) 🛡️ Security Status: 1 high vulnerability detected, action required
Example 5: Security Audit & Compliance Monitoring (Enterprise Security Management)

Conduct comprehensive security audits and compliance monitoring for anihpj Django application environments including User data protection, JobPost privacy compliance, Skills data validation, Author content security, and Location service geolocation privacy across all deployment environments.

# Audit all environments for security compliance
docker compose ls --all --format json | jq '.[] | {name, status, created}'

# Monitor production environments for compliance
docker compose ls --filter name=*-prod* --format table

# Check environments with security configurations
docker compose ls | grep -E "(secure|audit|compliance)"

Django Security Audit Context:

# Security audit and compliance monitoring for Django anihpj application
# Audit User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location models for security compliance

import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Security audit framework
security_audit = {
    "audit_timestamp": "2026-01-12 18:30:00",
    "audit_scope": "enterprise_security_compliance",
    "compliance_frameworks": ["GDPR", "SOC2", "HIPAA", "ISO27001"],
    "audited_models": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
    "security_domains": ["data_protection", "access_control", "encryption", "audit_logging", "privacy"]
}

# Audited environments for compliance
audited_environments = {
    "anihpj-production-secure": {
        "name": "anihpj-production-secure",
        "status": "running",
        "security_tier": "enterprise",
        "compliance_level": "SOC2_Type2",
        "last_audit": "2026-01-10T09:00:00Z",
        "next_audit": "2026-01-17T09:00:00Z",
        "services_total": 12,
        "services_running": 12,
        "environment_type": "production_secure",
        "data_classification": "confidential",
        "encryption_status": "full_encryption",
        "security_controls": {
            "User_model_security": {
                "data_encryption": "AES-256",
                "pii_protection": "enabled",
                "access_logging": "comprehensive",
                "retention_policy": "7_years",
                "anonymization": "gdpr_compliant",
                "compliance_score": 98
            },
            "JobPost_model_security": {
                "content_filtering": "enabled",
                "data_masking": "sensitive_fields",
                "audit_trail": "complete",
                "privacy_controls": "location_anonymization",
                "compliance_score": 95
            },
            "Skills_model_security": {
                "api_rate_limiting": "strict",
                "data_validation": "enhanced",
                "access_controls": "rbac_enabled",
                "compliance_score": 92
            },
            "Author_model_security": {
                "content_moderation": "ai_assisted",
                "identity_verification": "multi_factor",
                "intellectual_property": "protected",
                "compliance_score": 96
            },
            "Location_model_security": {
                "geolocation_privacy": "anonymized",
                "data_minimization": "enabled",
                "cross_border_compliance": "gdpr_article_46",
                "compliance_score": 94
            }
        }
    },
    "anihpj-staging-audit": {
        "name": "anihpj-staging-audit",
        "status": "running",
        "security_tier": "standard",
        "compliance_level": "internal_audit",
        "last_audit": "2026-01-11T14:00:00Z",
        "next_audit": "2026-01-18T14:00:00Z",
        "services_total": 8,
        "services_running": 8,
        "environment_type": "security_testing",
        "data_classification": "internal",
        "audit_findings": {
            "critical_issues": 0,
            "high_issues": 2,
            "medium_issues": 5,
            "low_issues": 12,
            "total_issues": 19
        },
        "remediation_status": {
            "resolved": 15,
            "in_progress": 3,
            "planned": 1
        }
    }
}

print("Security Audit & Compliance - Docker Compose LS Analysis:")
print(f"Security audit initiated: {security_audit['audit_timestamp']}")

# Compliance overview
total_environments = len(audited_environments)
compliant_environments = len([env for env in audited_environments.values() 
                             if env.get("compliance_level") in ["SOC2_Type2", "internal_audit"]])

print(f"\n🛡️ Security Audit Overview:")
print(f"   Environments Audited: {total_environments}")
print(f"   Compliance Frameworks: {', '.join(security_audit['compliance_frameworks'])}")
print(f"   Models Audited: {', '.join(security_audit['audited_models'])}")
print(f"   Compliant Environments: {compliant_environments}/{total_environments}")

# Environment-specific security analysis
for env_name, env_data in audited_environments.items():
    status_icon = "🟢" if env_data["status"] == "running" else "🔴"
    tier_icon = "🏆" if env_data["security_tier"] == "enterprise" else "🥈" if env_data["security_tier"] == "standard" else "🧪"
    
    print(f"\n{status_icon} {env_name}")
    print(f"   {tier_icon} Security Tier: {env_data['security_tier'].title()}")
    print(f"   📋 Compliance Level: {env_data['compliance_level'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"   🔧 Services: {env_data['services_running']}/{env_data['services_total']}")
    
    # Last audit information
    last_audit = datetime.fromisoformat(env_data["last_audit"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    audit_age = (datetime.now() - last_audit.replace(tzinfo=None)).days
    audit_icon = "🟢" if audit_age <= 3 else "🟡" if audit_age <= 7 else "🔴"
    
    print(f"   {audit_icon} Last Audit: {audit_age} day(s) ago")
    
    # Security controls analysis
    if "security_controls" in env_data:
        print(f"   🔐 Model Security Compliance:")
        
        total_score = 0
        model_count = 0
        
        for model, controls in env_data["security_controls"].items():
            score = controls.get("compliance_score", 0)
            total_score += score
            model_count += 1
            
            score_icon = "🟢" if score >= 95 else "🟡" if score >= 90 else "🔴"
            model_display = model.replace("_model_security", "").replace("_", " ").title()
            
            print(f"      {score_icon} {model_display}: {score}/100")
        
        # Calculate average compliance score
        if model_count > 0:
            avg_score = total_score / model_count
            avg_icon = "🟢" if avg_score >= 95 else "🟡" if avg_score >= 90 else "🔴"
            print(f"      {avg_icon} Average Compliance Score: {avg_score:.1f}/100")
    
    # Audit findings
    if "audit_findings" in env_data:
        findings = env_data["audit_findings"]
        critical = findings.get("critical_issues", 0)
        high = findings.get("high_issues", 0)
        
        findings_icon = "🔴" if critical > 0 else "🟡" if high > 0 else "🟢"
        print(f"   {findings_icon} Audit Findings: {findings['total_issues']} total")
        print(f"      🚨 Critical: {critical}, High: {high}, Medium: {findings.get('medium_issues', 0)}")
        
        if "remediation_status" in env_data:
            remediation = env_data["remediation_status"]
            resolved_rate = (remediation["resolved"] / findings["total_issues"]) * 100 if findings["total_issues"] > 0 else 100
            remediation_icon = "🟢" if resolved_rate >= 80 else "🟡" if resolved_rate >= 60 else "🔴"
            
            print(f"      {remediation_icon} Remediation: {resolved_rate:.1f}% complete ({remediation['resolved']}/{findings['total_issues']})")

# Overall security metrics
total_compliance_scores = []
for env_data in audited_environments.values():
    if "security_controls" in env_data:
        env_scores = [controls.get("compliance_score", 0) 
                     for controls in env_data["security_controls"].values()]
        if env_scores:
            total_compliance_scores.extend(env_scores)

if total_compliance_scores:
    avg_compliance = sum(total_compliance_scores) / len(total_compliance_scores)
    compliance_icon = "🟢" if avg_compliance >= 95 else "🟡" if avg_compliance >= 90 else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n📊 Overall Security Metrics:")
    print(f"   {compliance_icon} Average Compliance Score: {avg_compliance:.1f}/100")
    print(f"   🔐 Security Frameworks: {len(security_audit['compliance_frameworks'])} implemented")
    print(f"   🧬 Model Coverage: {len(security_audit['audited_models'])} models audited")
    
    print(f"\n🏆 Security Excellence Summary:")
    print(f"   ✅ GDPR Compliance: Fully implemented across all models")
    print(f"   ✅ Data Encryption: AES-256 encryption for sensitive data")
    print(f"   ✅ Access Controls: RBAC implemented with audit logging")
    print(f"   ✅ Privacy Protection: Comprehensive anonymization and data minimization")
anihpj-production-secure running 12 /srv/secure-prod anihpj-staging-audit running 8 /srv/audit-staging Security Audit & Compliance - Docker Compose LS Analysis: Security audit initiated: 2026-01-12 18:30:00 🛡️ Security Audit Overview: Environments Audited: 2 Compliance Frameworks: GDPR, SOC2, HIPAA, ISO27001 Models Audited: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location Compliant Environments: 2/2 🟢 anihpj-production-secure 🏆 Security Tier: Enterprise 📋 Compliance Level: Soc2 Type2 🔧 Services: 12/12 🟢 Last Audit: 2 day(s) ago 🔐 Model Security Compliance: 🟢 User: 98/100 🟢 Jobpost: 95/100 🟡 Skills: 92/100 🟢 Author: 96/100 🟡 Location: 94/100 🟢 Average Compliance Score: 95.0/100 🟢 anihpj-staging-audit 🥈 Security Tier: Standard 📋 Compliance Level: Internal Audit 🔧 Services: 8/8 🟡 Last Audit: 1 day(s) ago 🟡 Audit Findings: 19 total 🚨 Critical: 0, High: 2, Medium: 5 🟢 Remediation: 78.9% complete (15/19) 📊 Overall Security Metrics: 🟢 Average Compliance Score: 95.0/100 🔐 Security Frameworks: 4 implemented 🧬 Model Coverage: 5 models audited 🏆 Security Excellence Summary: ✅ GDPR Compliance: Fully implemented across all models ✅ Data Encryption: AES-256 encryption for sensitive data ✅ Access Controls: RBAC implemented with audit logging ✅ Privacy Protection: Comprehensive anonymization and data minimization

docker compose pause

Official Docs: docker compose pause | Docker CLI: docker compose pause

Suspend Django service processes while preserving memory state and container configuration.

Full Description

The docker compose pause command suspends all processes in your anihpj Django service containers without stopping them completely. This freezes the current state in memory including active connections, loaded data, and running processes, making it perfect for temporary resource conservation, testing scenarios, or system inspection without service disruption.

For Django applications, pausing services maintains application state, preserves database connections, keeps sessions active, and allows for instant resumption without restart overhead. This is useful for debugging, resource management, testing deployments, or temporary system isolation in your anihpj jobpost application environment.

Syntax

# Pause all services
docker compose pause

# Pause specific service
docker compose pause web

# Pause multiple services  
docker compose pause web worker

# Pause all except critical services
docker compose pause $(docker compose ps --services | grep -v db)

# Pause with verification
docker compose pause && docker compose ps

# Pause for debugging session
docker compose pause web && docker exec -it anihpj_db_1 psql

# Pause worker services only
docker compose pause worker scheduler

Parameters Description

  • Service Names: Specify which Django services to pause. Target specific components while keeping others active.
  • Multiple Services: Pause several related Django services together. Coordinate suspension of application components.
  • Selective Pausing: Keep critical Django services running while suspending others for testing or resource management.
  • State Preservation: Maintains all Django application state in memory during pause period.
  • Instant Resumption: Enables immediate service restoration without startup delays for Django applications.

Attributes

  • Memory Preservation: Maintains Django application memory state including loaded models, cached data, and active sessions.
  • Process Suspension: Freezes Django service processes without termination, preserving execution context and state.
  • Resource Conservation: Reduces CPU usage while maintaining Django service availability for instant resumption.
  • Connection Maintenance: Preserves Django database connections and inter-service communication channels during pause.
  • Zero Restart Overhead: Instant service resumption without Django application startup or initialization delays.

Methods

Debug Session Preparation

Syntax: docker compose pause web worker

Parameters: Service list for Django component suspension during debugging

Suspend Django application services while keeping database active for safe debugging and inspection.

Resource Conservation

Syntax: docker compose pause $(docker compose ps --services | grep -v critical)

Parameters: Filtered service list excluding critical Django infrastructure

Pause non-essential Django services to conserve system resources while maintaining core functionality.

Return Value

Returns pause status for each Django service showing successful suspension. Indicates any failures during pause operations.

  • Success (0): All specified services paused successfully
  • Error (1): Service pause failure or container access issues
  • Partial Success: Some services paused, others failed or already paused

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Performance Testing Isolation (Load Testing Preparation)

Pause non-essential Django services during load testing to isolate performance metrics for core User authentication and JobPost operations.

# Pause non-essential services for load testing
docker compose pause worker scheduler monitoring

# Verify core services remain active
docker compose ps | grep -E "(web|db|redis)"

# Run load test on isolated Django core services
ab -n 1000 -c 10 http://localhost:8000/api/jobs/

Django Models Context & Performance Isolation:

# Performance testing isolation for Django core operations
# Focusing on User authentication and JobPost API performance

import datetime
import time
from typing import Dict, List

# Services configuration for performance testing
service_configuration = {
    "core_services": {
        "active_services": ["web_django", "postgresql_db", "redis_cache"],
        "purpose": "Core Django functionality for load testing",
        "target_operations": [
            "user_authentication", 
            "job_search_api",
            "job_post_creation",
            "application_submission"
        ],
        "expected_performance": {
            "response_time": "< 200ms",
            "throughput": "> 100 req/s", 
            "error_rate": "< 1%"
        }
    },
    "paused_services": {
        "suspended_services": ["celery_worker", "celery_scheduler", "elasticsearch", "monitoring"],
        "purpose": "Remove background processing overhead",
        "resource_savings": {
            "cpu_usage": "40% reduction",
            "memory_usage": "25% reduction",
            "network_io": "60% reduction"
        },
        "maintained_state": [
            "job_queue_positions",
            "scheduled_task_timing", 
            "search_index_status",
            "monitoring_metrics"
        ]
    }
}

# Load testing scenarios for Django models
load_test_scenarios = {
    "user_authentication": {
        "model": "User",
        "endpoints": ["/api/auth/login", "/api/auth/refresh", "/api/auth/logout"],
        "test_data": {
            "concurrent_users": 50,
            "requests_per_user": 20,
            "test_duration": "5 minutes"
        },
        "performance_targets": {
            "avg_response_time": "150ms",
            "95th_percentile": "300ms",
            "success_rate": "99.5%"
        }
    },
    "job_search_api": {
        "model": "JobPost",
        "endpoints": ["/api/jobs/", "/api/jobs/search", "/api/jobs/{id}"],
        "test_data": {
            "concurrent_users": 100,
            "requests_per_user": 15,
            "search_queries": ["python", "django", "remote", "senior"]
        },
        "performance_targets": {
            "avg_response_time": "200ms",
            "95th_percentile": "400ms", 
            "success_rate": "99.0%"
        }
    },
    "job_posting": {
        "model": "JobPost",
        "endpoints": ["/api/jobs/create", "/api/jobs/update", "/api/jobs/delete"],
        "test_data": {
            "concurrent_users": 25,
            "requests_per_user": 10,
            "job_categories": ["technical", "design", "management"]
        },
        "performance_targets": {
            "avg_response_time": "300ms",
            "95th_percentile": "500ms",
            "success_rate": "99.8%"
        }
    },
    "author_profiles": {
        "model": "Author", 
        "endpoints": ["/api/authors/", "/api/authors/{id}", "/api/authors/{id}/skills"],
        "test_data": {
            "concurrent_users": 30,
            "requests_per_user": 12,
            "profile_operations": ["view", "update", "skill_add"]
        },
        "performance_targets": {
            "avg_response_time": "180ms",
            "95th_percentile": "350ms",
            "success_rate": "99.7%"
        }
    }
}

# System resource monitoring during pause
resource_monitoring = {
    "before_pause": {
        "cpu_usage": "65%",
        "memory_usage": "4.2GB",
        "network_io": "45MB/s",
        "disk_io": "12MB/s",
        "active_connections": 156
    },
    "after_pause": {
        "cpu_usage": "39%", 
        "memory_usage": "3.1GB",
        "network_io": "18MB/s",
        "disk_io": "8MB/s",
        "active_connections": 89
    },
    "resource_savings": {
        "cpu_reduction": "40%",
        "memory_reduction": "26%", 
        "network_reduction": "60%",
        "connection_reduction": "43%"
    }
}

# Print performance testing isolation analysis
print("Django Performance Testing - Service Pause Analysis:")
print("=" * 58)
print(f"Load testing session: {datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}")

print(f"\n🎯 Core Services (Active for Testing):")
core = service_configuration["core_services"]
print(f"   Services: {', '.join(core['active_services'])}")
print(f"   Purpose: {core['purpose']}")
print(f"   Target operations:")
for operation in core['target_operations']:
    print(f"     - {operation.replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n   Performance expectations:")
for metric, target in core['expected_performance'].items():
    print(f"     {metric.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {target}")

print(f"\n⏸️ Paused Services (Suspended for Isolation):")
paused = service_configuration["paused_services"]
print(f"   Services: {', '.join(paused['suspended_services'])}")
print(f"   Purpose: {paused['purpose']}")

print(f"\n   Resource savings:")
for resource, saving in paused['resource_savings'].items():
    print(f"     {resource.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {saving}")

print(f"\n   State preserved:")
for state in paused['maintained_state']:
    print(f"     - {state.replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📊 Load Testing Scenarios:")
for scenario_name, details in load_test_scenarios.items():
    scenario_title = scenario_name.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   🔥 {scenario_title} ({details['model']} model):")
    print(f"      Endpoints: {', '.join(details['endpoints'])}")
    
    if 'concurrent_users' in details['test_data']:
        print(f"      Concurrent users: {details['test_data']['concurrent_users']}")
    if 'requests_per_user' in details['test_data']:
        print(f"      Requests per user: {details['test_data']['requests_per_user']}")
    
    print(f"      Performance targets:")
    for metric, target in details['performance_targets'].items():
        print(f"        {metric.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {target}")

print(f"\n📈 Resource Impact Analysis:")
before = resource_monitoring["before_pause"]
after = resource_monitoring["after_pause"] 
savings = resource_monitoring["resource_savings"]

print(f"   Before pause: CPU {before['cpu_usage']}, Memory {before['memory_usage']}")
print(f"   After pause:  CPU {after['cpu_usage']}, Memory {after['memory_usage']}")
print(f"   Network I/O:  {before['network_io']} → {after['network_io']}")

print(f"\n   Resource optimization:")
for resource, reduction in savings.items():
    resource_name = resource.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"     {resource_name}: {reduction}")

# Simulate load test execution
load_test_results = {
    "user_authentication": {"avg_time": "142ms", "95th": "278ms", "success": "99.7%"},
    "job_search_api": {"avg_time": "185ms", "95th": "362ms", "success": "99.1%"},
    "job_posting": {"avg_time": "267ms", "95th": "445ms", "success": "99.9%"},
    "author_profiles": {"avg_time": "156ms", "95th": "301ms", "success": "99.8%"}
}

print(f"\n🚀 Load Test Results (With Services Paused):")
for test_name, results in load_test_results.items():
    test_title = test_name.replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"   ✅ {test_title}:")
    print(f"      Avg: {results['avg_time']}, 95th: {results['95th']}, Success: {results['success']}")

print(f"\n🎯 Performance Testing Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ All core Django services isolated successfully")
print(f"   ✅ Background services paused, state preserved")
print(f"   ✅ Performance targets met across all scenarios")
print(f"   ✅ Resource usage optimized for accurate testing")
print(f"   ✅ 40% CPU reduction improved test reliability")

print(f"\n📋 Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Resume paused services: docker compose unpause")
print(f"   2. Verify background processing restoration")
print(f"   3. Monitor system performance under full load")
print(f"   4. Compare isolated vs full-system performance metrics")
Pausing anihpj_worker_1 ... done Pausing anihpj_scheduler_1 ... done Pausing anihpj_monitoring_1 ... done NAME SERVICE STATUS PORTS anihpj_web_1 web running 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp anihpj_db_1 db running 0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp anihpj_redis_1 redis running 0.0.0.0:6379->6379/tcp anihpj_worker_1 worker paused anihpj_scheduler_1 scheduler paused anihpj_monitoring_1 monitoring paused Django Performance Testing - Service Pause Analysis: ========================================================== Load testing session: 2026-01-11 18:15:22 🎯 Core Services (Active for Testing): Services: web_django, postgresql_db, redis_cache Purpose: Core Django functionality for load testing Target operations: - User Authentication - Job Search Api - Job Post Creation - Application Submission Performance expectations: Response Time: < 200ms Throughput: > 100 req/s Error Rate: < 1% ⏸️ Paused Services (Suspended for Isolation): Services: celery_worker, celery_scheduler, elasticsearch, monitoring Purpose: Remove background processing overhead Resource savings: Cpu Usage: 40% reduction Memory Usage: 25% reduction Network Io: 60% reduction State preserved: - Job Queue Positions - Scheduled Task Timing - Search Index Status - Monitoring Metrics 📊 Load Testing Scenarios: 🔥 User Authentication (User model): Endpoints: /api/auth/login, /api/auth/refresh, /api/auth/logout Concurrent users: 50 Requests per user: 20 Performance targets: Avg Response Time: 150ms 95th Percentile: 300ms Success Rate: 99.5% 🔥 Job Search Api (JobPost model): Endpoints: /api/jobs/, /api/jobs/search, /api/jobs/{id} Concurrent users: 100 Requests per user: 15 Performance targets: Avg Response Time: 200ms 95th Percentile: 400ms Success Rate: 99.0% 📈 Resource Impact Analysis: Before pause: CPU 65%, Memory 4.2GB After pause: CPU 39%, Memory 3.1GB Network I/O: 45MB/s → 18MB/s Resource optimization: Cpu Reduction: 40% Memory Reduction: 26% Network Reduction: 60% Connection Reduction: 43% 🚀 Load Test Results (With Services Paused): ✅ User Authentication: Avg: 142ms, 95th: 278ms, Success: 99.7% ✅ Job Search Api: Avg: 185ms, 95th: 362ms, Success: 99.1% ✅ Job Posting: Avg: 267ms, 95th: 445ms, Success: 99.9% ✅ Author Profiles: Avg: 156ms, 95th: 301ms, Success: 99.8% 🎯 Performance Testing Summary: ✅ All core Django services isolated successfully ✅ Background services paused, state preserved ✅ Performance targets met across all scenarios ✅ Resource usage optimized for accurate testing ✅ 40% CPU reduction improved test reliability
Example 2: Debugging Session Management (Developer Workflow Optimization)

Pause Django application services strategically for debugging User authentication issues, JobPost data corruption investigation, Skills algorithm testing, Author content review, and Location service geospatial debugging without losing application state.

# Pause web service for debugging session
docker compose pause web

# Access database for debugging while maintaining state
docker compose exec db psql -U postgres -d anihpj

# Pause worker processes to prevent job interference
docker compose pause worker scheduler

# Debug with application state preserved
docker compose exec web bash

Django Debugging Context & State Preservation:

# Debugging session management for Django anihpj application
# Preserve User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model states during debugging

import json
from datetime import datetime

# Debug session configuration
debug_session = {
    "session_start": "2026-01-12 09:30:15",
    "debug_scope": "user_authentication_investigation",
    "affected_models": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
    "debugging_strategy": "state_preservation_analysis"
}

# Services state before debugging
pre_debug_state = {
    "web_django": {
        "status": "running",
        "connections": 47,
        "active_sessions": 234,
        "memory_usage": "1.2GB",
        "cpu_usage": "35%",
        "debug_action": "pause_for_inspection",
        "state_preserved": {
            "User_sessions": {
                "active_users": 234,
                "authenticated_sessions": 198,
                "session_data": "preserved_in_memory",
                "login_tokens": "jwt_cache_maintained"
            },
            "JobPost_cache": {
                "cached_listings": 1247,
                "search_results": "preserved",
                "recommendation_engine": "state_frozen",
                "application_queue": "processing_halted"
            },
            "Skills_api": {
                "loaded_skills": 2156,
                "matching_algorithms": "paused",
                "cache_warm": "preserved",
                "api_rate_limits": "counters_frozen"
            },
            "Author_profiles": {
                "loaded_profiles": 823,
                "content_cache": "preserved", 
                "moderation_queue": "halted_safely",
                "verification_states": "maintained"
            },
            "Location_services": {
                "geocode_cache": 487,
                "proximity_calculations": "paused",
                "mapping_data": "preserved",
                "coordinate_lookups": "state_frozen"
            }
        }
    },
    "worker_celery": {
        "status": "running",
        "active_jobs": 15,
        "pending_jobs": 67,
        "memory_usage": "512MB",
        "cpu_usage": "18%", 
        "debug_action": "pause_to_prevent_interference",
        "job_preservation": {
            "current_executions": "safely_paused",
            "job_queue": "positions_maintained",
            "scheduler_state": "timing_preserved",
            "worker_memory": "task_data_frozen"
        }
    }
}

# Debugging investigation details
debugging_investigation = {
    "primary_issue": "user_authentication_timeouts",
    "investigation_areas": [
        "session_management",
        "database_connections",
        "cache_performance", 
        "jwt_token_validation"
    ],
    "debug_findings": {
        "User_model_analysis": {
            "database_connections": {
                "active_connections": 12,
                "connection_pool": "healthy",
                "slow_queries": 2,
                "lock_contention": "minimal"
            },
            "session_storage": {
                "redis_sessions": 198,
                "session_size_avg": "2.4KB",
                "expired_sessions": 5,
                "cleanup_pending": "paused_safely"
            },
            "authentication_flow": {
                "login_attempts_sec": 0,
                "jwt_validations": "suspended",
                "password_hashing": "not_active",
                "oauth_callbacks": "queued_safely"
            }
        },
        "memory_inspection": {
            "heap_analysis": "django_objects_preserved",
            "cache_contents": "skills_and_jobpost_data_intact",
            "orm_state": "queryset_cache_maintained",
            "background_tasks": "safely_suspended"
        }
    }
}

print("Debug Session Management - Docker Compose Pause Analysis:")
print(f"Debug session started: {debug_session['session_start']}")
print(f"Investigation scope: {debug_session['debug_scope']}")

# Service state analysis during debugging
print(f"\n⏸️ Service State During Debugging:")

for service, state in pre_debug_state.items():
    service_icon = "🔍" if "debug" in service else "⏸️"
    action = state.get("debug_action", "monitoring").replace("_", " ").title()
    
    print(f"   {service_icon} {service.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    print(f"      🎯 Action: {action}")
    print(f"      📊 Connections: {state.get('connections', state.get('active_jobs', 'N/A'))}")
    print(f"      💾 Memory: {state['memory_usage']} | CPU: {state['cpu_usage']}")

# State preservation details
print(f"\n💾 Critical State Preservation:")

# User model state
user_state = pre_debug_state["web_django"]["state_preserved"]["User_sessions"]
print(f"   👥 User Sessions:")
print(f"      Active Users: {user_state['active_users']}")
print(f"      Authenticated: {user_state['authenticated_sessions']}")
print(f"      Session Data: {user_state['session_data'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

# JobPost model state  
jobpost_state = pre_debug_state["web_django"]["state_preserved"]["JobPost_cache"]
print(f"   📋 JobPost Cache:")
print(f"      Cached Listings: {jobpost_state['cached_listings']:,}")
print(f"      Search Results: {jobpost_state['search_results'].title()}")
print(f"      Queue Status: {jobpost_state['application_queue'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

# Skills model state
skills_state = pre_debug_state["web_django"]["state_preserved"]["Skills_api"]
print(f"   🏷️ Skills API:")
print(f"      Loaded Skills: {skills_state['loaded_skills']:,}")
print(f"      Algorithms: {skills_state['matching_algorithms'].title()}")
print(f"      Cache Status: {skills_state['cache_warm'].title()}")

# Debugging findings analysis
print(f"\n🔍 Debugging Investigation Results:")

user_analysis = debugging_investigation["debug_findings"]["User_model_analysis"]
print(f"   👥 User Model Analysis:")
print(f"      🔗 Active DB Connections: {user_analysis['database_connections']['active_connections']}")
print(f"      📊 Redis Sessions: {user_analysis['session_storage']['redis_sessions']}")
print(f"      🔐 Auth Flow: {user_analysis['authentication_flow']['login_attempts_sec']} login attempts/sec (paused)")

# Memory inspection results
memory_info = debugging_investigation["debug_findings"]["memory_inspection"]
print(f"   🧠 Memory State Inspection:")
print(f"      Django Objects: {memory_info['heap_analysis'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"      Cache Contents: {memory_info['cache_contents'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"      Background Tasks: {memory_info['background_tasks'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

# Debug session benefits
print(f"\n🎯 Debugging Session Benefits:")
print(f"   ✅ Zero data loss during investigation")
print(f"   ✅ User sessions maintained without disconnection")
print(f"   ✅ Application state preserved for analysis")
print(f"   ✅ Background jobs safely paused without data corruption")
print(f"   ✅ Database connections kept alive for inspection")
print(f"   ✅ Cache contents available for debugging queries")

print(f"\n🔧 Debug Resolution Status:")
print(f"   🔍 Issue identified: Connection pool optimization needed")
print(f"   ⚡ Solution: Increase connection timeout from 30s to 60s")
print(f"   📈 Expected improvement: 40% reduction in authentication timeouts")
print(f"   🚀 Deployment plan: Apply fix and unpause services with zero downtime")
anihpj-web-1 | Container paused anihpj-worker-1 | Container paused anihpj-scheduler-1 | Container paused Debug Session Management - Docker Compose Pause Analysis: Debug session started: 2026-01-12 09:30:15 Investigation scope: user_authentication_investigation ⏸️ Service State During Debugging: 🔍 Web Django 🎯 Action: Pause For Inspection 📊 Connections: 47 💾 Memory: 1.2GB | CPU: 35% ⏸️ Worker Celery 🎯 Action: Pause To Prevent Interference 📊 Connections: 15 💾 Memory: 512MB | CPU: 18% 💾 Critical State Preservation: 👥 User Sessions: Active Users: 234 Authenticated: 198 Session Data: Preserved In Memory 📋 JobPost Cache: Cached Listings: 1,247 Search Results: Preserved Queue Status: Processing Halted 🏷️ Skills API: Loaded Skills: 2,156 Algorithms: Paused Cache Status: Preserved 🔍 Debugging Investigation Results: 👥 User Model Analysis: 🔗 Active DB Connections: 12 📊 Redis Sessions: 198 🔐 Auth Flow: 0 login attempts/sec (paused) 🧠 Memory State Inspection: Django Objects: Django Objects Preserved Cache Contents: Skills And Jobpost Data Intact Background Tasks: Safely Suspended 🎯 Debugging Session Benefits: ✅ Zero data loss during investigation ✅ User sessions maintained without disconnection ✅ Application state preserved for analysis ✅ Background jobs safely paused without data corruption ✅ Database connections kept alive for inspection ✅ Cache contents available for debugging queries 🔧 Debug Resolution Status: 🔍 Issue identified: Connection pool optimization needed ⚡ Solution: Increase connection timeout from 30s to 60s 📈 Expected improvement: 40% reduction in authentication timeouts 🚀 Deployment plan: Apply fix and unpause services with zero downtime
Example 3: Resource Management & Cost Optimization (Infrastructure Efficiency)

Optimize infrastructure costs by pausing non-critical Django services during low-traffic periods while maintaining User session continuity, JobPost search availability, Skills API responsiveness, Author content access, and Location services for essential operations.

# Pause resource-intensive services during off-peak hours
docker compose pause worker elasticsearch monitoring analytics

# Verify core services remain available
docker compose ps | grep -E "(running|Up)"

# Monitor resource usage reduction
docker stats --no-stream | grep anihpj

Django Resource Optimization Context:

# Resource management and cost optimization for Django anihpj application
# Optimize User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location services during off-peak hours

import json
from datetime import datetime, time

# Resource optimization strategy
resource_optimization = {
    "optimization_timestamp": "2026-01-12 02:00:00",
    "optimization_period": "off_peak_hours_2am_6am", 
    "cost_reduction_target": "35%",
    "service_availability_requirement": "core_functionality_maintained"
}

# Service tier categorization for resource management
service_tiers = {
    "essential_services": {
        "services": ["web_django", "postgresql_db", "redis_cache", "nginx_proxy"],
        "purpose": "Core user-facing functionality",
        "availability_requirement": "100%_uptime",
        "resource_impact": "minimal_optimization",
        "model_support": {
            "User": {
                "authentication": "always_available",
                "session_management": "full_support",
                "profile_access": "immediate_response",
                "resource_allocation": "2_cores_1GB_RAM"
            },
            "JobPost": {
                "job_listings": "always_available",
                "search_functionality": "cached_results",
                "application_submission": "immediate_processing", 
                "resource_allocation": "1.5_cores_800MB_RAM"
            },
            "Skills": {
                "skills_api": "always_available",
                "matching_service": "cached_algorithms",
                "skill_validation": "immediate_response",
                "resource_allocation": "1_core_512MB_RAM"
            }
        }
    },
    "pauseable_services": {
        "services": ["celery_worker", "elasticsearch", "monitoring", "analytics", "backup_service"],
        "purpose": "Background processing and non-critical operations",
        "pause_strategy": "graceful_suspension",
        "resource_savings": "significant_cost_reduction",
        "model_impact": {
            "JobPost": {
                "search_indexing": "paused_until_morning",
                "recommendation_updates": "delayed_processing",
                "analytics_collection": "suspended_safely",
                "resource_savings": "3_cores_2GB_RAM"
            },
            "Author": {
                "content_moderation": "queue_accumulation",
                "profile_verification": "batch_processing_later",
                "content_analytics": "paused_temporarily",
                "resource_savings": "2_cores_1.5GB_RAM"
            },
            "Location": {
                "geocoding_batch_jobs": "paused_processing",
                "map_tile_generation": "suspended_overnight",
                "proximity_calculations": "cached_results_only",
                "resource_savings": "1.5_cores_1GB_RAM"
            }
        }
    }
}

# Cost analysis during optimization period
cost_analysis = {
    "pre_optimization": {
        "total_cpu_cores": 16,
        "total_memory_gb": 12,
        "hourly_cost_usd": 2.40,
        "monthly_projection_usd": 1728,
        "resource_utilization": "78%",
        "peak_hour_performance": "excellent"
    },
    "post_optimization": {
        "active_cpu_cores": 10,
        "active_memory_gb": 7.5,
        "hourly_cost_usd": 1.56,
        "monthly_savings_usd": 604.8,
        "resource_utilization": "65%",
        "off_peak_performance": "maintained"
    }
}

print("Resource Management & Cost Optimization - Docker Compose Pause Analysis:")
print(f"Optimization period: {resource_optimization['optimization_period'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"Target cost reduction: {resource_optimization['cost_reduction_target']}")

# Service tier analysis
print(f"\n🔄 Service Tier Management:")

for tier_name, tier_data in service_tiers.items():
    tier_icon = "🔋" if tier_name == "essential_services" else "⏸️"
    tier_display = tier_name.replace("_", " ").title()
    
    print(f"   {tier_icon} {tier_display}:")
    print(f"      Services: {', '.join(tier_data['services'])}")
    print(f"      Purpose: {tier_data['purpose']}")
    
    if "availability_requirement" in tier_data:
        availability = tier_data["availability_requirement"].replace("_", " ")
        print(f"      Availability: {availability}")
    
    if "pause_strategy" in tier_data:
        strategy = tier_data["pause_strategy"].replace("_", " ").title()
        print(f"      Strategy: {strategy}")

# Model-specific resource impact
print(f"\n📊 Model Resource Impact Analysis:")

# Essential services model support
essential_models = service_tiers["essential_services"]["model_support"]
for model, support in essential_models.items():
    print(f"   ✅ {model} Model (Essential):")
    for feature, status in support.items():
        if feature != "resource_allocation":
            status_display = status.replace("_", " ").title()
            print(f"      • {feature.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {status_display}")
        else:
            print(f"      💾 Resources: {status.replace('_', ' ')}")

# Pauseable services impact
print(f"\n   ⏸️ Paused Services Impact:")
pauseable_models = service_tiers["pauseable_services"]["model_impact"]
for model, impact in pauseable_models.items():
    print(f"   🟡 {model} Model (Deferred):")
    for feature, status in impact.items():
        if feature != "resource_savings":
            status_display = status.replace("_", " ").title()
            print(f"      • {feature.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {status_display}")
        else:
            print(f"      💰 Savings: {status.replace('_', ' ')}")

# Cost optimization analysis
print(f"\n💰 Cost Optimization Analysis:")

pre_opt = cost_analysis["pre_optimization"]
post_opt = cost_analysis["post_optimization"]

print(f"   Before Optimization:")
print(f"      💾 Resources: {pre_opt['total_cpu_cores']} cores, {pre_opt['total_memory_gb']}GB RAM")
print(f"      💵 Hourly Cost: ${pre_opt['hourly_cost_usd']:.2f}")
print(f"      📈 Monthly Projection: ${pre_opt['monthly_projection_usd']:,}")

print(f"   After Optimization:")
print(f"      💾 Active Resources: {post_opt['active_cpu_cores']} cores, {post_opt['active_memory_gb']}GB RAM")
print(f"      💵 Hourly Cost: ${post_opt['hourly_cost_usd']:.2f}")
print(f"      💰 Monthly Savings: ${post_opt['monthly_savings_usd']:.2f}")

# Calculate optimization metrics
cpu_reduction = ((pre_opt['total_cpu_cores'] - post_opt['active_cpu_cores']) / pre_opt['total_cpu_cores']) * 100
memory_reduction = ((pre_opt['total_memory_gb'] - post_opt['active_memory_gb']) / pre_opt['total_memory_gb']) * 100
cost_reduction = (post_opt['monthly_savings_usd'] / pre_opt['monthly_projection_usd']) * 100

print(f"\n🎯 Optimization Efficiency Metrics:")
print(f"   CPU Reduction: {cpu_reduction:.1f}% ({pre_opt['total_cpu_cores'] - post_opt['active_cpu_cores']} cores saved)")
print(f"   Memory Reduction: {memory_reduction:.1f}% ({pre_opt['total_memory_gb'] - post_opt['active_memory_gb']}GB saved)")
print(f"   Cost Reduction: {cost_reduction:.1f}% (${post_opt['monthly_savings_usd']:.2f}/month)")

print(f"\n✨ Optimization Benefits:")
print(f"   ✅ 35% cost reduction during off-peak hours (4 hours/day)")
print(f"   ✅ Core user functionality maintained 100%")
print(f"   ✅ Background jobs queue safely without data loss")
print(f"   ✅ Instant service resumption during peak hours")
print(f"   ✅ Zero impact on user experience")
print(f"   ✅ Automated scheduling reduces manual intervention")
anihpj-worker-1 | Container paused anihpj-elasticsearch-1 | Container paused anihpj-monitoring-1 | Container paused anihpj-analytics-1 | Container paused Resource Management & Cost Optimization - Docker Compose Pause Analysis: Optimization period: Off Peak Hours 2am 6am Target cost reduction: 35% 🔄 Service Tier Management: 🔋 Essential Services: Services: web_django, postgresql_db, redis_cache, nginx_proxy Purpose: Core user-facing functionality Availability: 100% uptime ⏸️ Pauseable Services: Services: celery_worker, elasticsearch, monitoring, analytics, backup_service Purpose: Background processing and non-critical operations Strategy: Graceful Suspension 📊 Model Resource Impact Analysis: ✅ User Model (Essential): • Authentication: Always Available • Session Management: Full Support • Profile Access: Immediate Response 💾 Resources: 2 cores 1GB RAM ✅ JobPost Model (Essential): • Job Listings: Always Available • Search Functionality: Cached Results • Application Submission: Immediate Processing 💾 Resources: 1.5 cores 800MB RAM ⏸️ Paused Services Impact: 🟡 JobPost Model (Deferred): • Search Indexing: Paused Until Morning • Recommendation Updates: Delayed Processing 💰 Savings: 3 cores 2GB RAM 🟡 Author Model (Deferred): • Content Moderation: Queue Accumulation • Profile Verification: Batch Processing Later 💰 Savings: 2 cores 1.5GB RAM 💰 Cost Optimization Analysis: Before Optimization: 💾 Resources: 16 cores, 12.0GB RAM 💵 Hourly Cost: $2.40 📈 Monthly Projection: $1,728 After Optimization: 💾 Active Resources: 10 cores, 7.5GB RAM 💵 Hourly Cost: $1.56 💰 Monthly Savings: $604.80 🎯 Optimization Efficiency Metrics: CPU Reduction: 37.5% (6 cores saved) Memory Reduction: 37.5% (4.5GB saved) Cost Reduction: 35.0% ($604.80/month) ✨ Optimization Benefits: ✅ 35% cost reduction during off-peak hours (4 hours/day) ✅ Core user functionality maintained 100% ✅ Background jobs queue safely without data loss ✅ Instant service resumption during peak hours ✅ Zero impact on user experience ✅ Automated scheduling reduces manual intervention
Example 4: Emergency Response & System Isolation (Crisis Management)

Implement emergency response procedures using pause capabilities to isolate issues, preserve User data integrity, maintain JobPost system stability, protect Skills algorithms, secure Author content, and ensure Location service reliability during system emergencies.

# Emergency response: pause affected services immediately
docker compose pause web worker

# Isolate database for emergency backup
docker compose pause $(docker compose ps --services | grep -v db)

# Emergency data preservation
docker compose exec db pg_dump -U postgres anihpj > emergency_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).sql

# Controlled service restart after issue resolution
docker compose unpause db && sleep 30 && docker compose unpause web worker

Django Emergency Response Context:

# Emergency response for Django anihpj application
# Protect User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location data during system emergencies

import json
from datetime import datetime

# Emergency response scenario
emergency_response = {
    "incident_timestamp": "2026-01-12 14:23:47",
    "incident_type": "database_corruption_detected",
    "severity_level": "critical",
    "affected_systems": ["postgresql_primary", "application_layer"],
    "response_time_requirement": "immediate_action_5_minutes"
}

# Critical data protection during emergency
data_protection = {
    "User_model_protection": {
        "user_sessions": "preserved_in_redis",
        "authentication_tokens": "cached_validation",
        "profile_data": "read_only_mode",
        "new_registrations": "queued_safely",
        "data_integrity_status": "protected"
    },
    "JobPost_model_protection": {
        "existing_listings": "served_from_cache",
        "job_search": "elasticsearch_active", 
        "new_applications": "queued_for_safety",
        "posting_submissions": "temporarily_disabled",
        "data_integrity_status": "corruption_risk_isolated"
    },
    "Skills_model_protection": {
        "skills_database": "read_only_access",
        "matching_algorithms": "cached_execution",
        "api_responses": "served_from_memory",
        "skill_updates": "suspended_until_recovery",
        "data_integrity_status": "algorithms_preserved"
    },
    "Author_model_protection": {
        "content_access": "cached_delivery",
        "content_moderation": "queued_processing",
        "profile_updates": "suspended_temporarily",
        "verification_processes": "paused_safely",
        "data_integrity_status": "content_preserved"
    },
    "Location_model_protection": {
        "geocoding_services": "cached_responses",
        "proximity_calculations": "memory_based",
        "mapping_data": "static_delivery",
        "coordinate_updates": "queued_for_recovery",
        "data_integrity_status": "geospatial_data_safe"
    }
}

print("Emergency Response & System Isolation - Docker Compose Pause Analysis:")
print(f"Incident detected: {emergency_response['incident_timestamp']}")
print(f"Incident type: {emergency_response['incident_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"Severity level: {emergency_response['severity_level'].upper()}")

print(f"\n🚨 Immediate Data Protection Status:")

for model, protection in data_protection.items():
    model_display = model.replace("_model_protection", "").replace("_", " ").title()
    integrity_status = protection["data_integrity_status"].replace("_", " ").title()
    
    print(f"   🛡️ {model_display}:")
    print(f"      Status: {integrity_status}")
    
    # Show key protective measures
    protection_count = 0
    for measure, status in protection.items():
        if measure != "data_integrity_status" and protection_count < 3:
            measure_display = measure.replace("_", " ").title()
            status_display = status.replace("_", " ").title()
            print(f"      • {measure_display}: {status_display}")
            protection_count += 1

# Emergency backup analysis
backup_analysis = {
    "backup_size": "2.4GB",
    "backup_duration": "8_minutes",
    "backup_integrity": "verified",
    "records_analyzed": 29158,
    "corrupted_records": 186,
    "corruption_percentage": "0.74%",
    "recovery_feasibility": "excellent"
}

print(f"\n💾 Emergency Backup Analysis:")
print(f"   Backup Size: {backup_analysis['backup_size']}")
print(f"   Backup Duration: {backup_analysis['backup_duration'].replace('_', ' ')}")
print(f"   Integrity Check: {backup_analysis['backup_integrity'].title()}")
print(f"   Data Corruption: {backup_analysis['corrupted_records']:,}/{backup_analysis['records_analyzed']:,} records ({backup_analysis['corruption_percentage']})")
print(f"   Recovery Feasibility: {backup_analysis['recovery_feasibility'].title()}")

print(f"\n⚡ Emergency Response Benefits:")
print(f"   ✅ Immediate system isolation prevented corruption spread")
print(f"   ✅ User sessions preserved throughout emergency")
print(f"   ✅ Critical data protected using pause mechanisms")
print(f"   ✅ Emergency backup completed successfully")
print(f"   ✅ All Django models maintained data integrity")
print(f"   ✅ System ready for controlled recovery operations")
