        "security_rating": "medium"
    }
}

# Print security audit analysis
print("Django Production Images Security Audit - anihpj Deployment:")
print("=" * 63)
print(f"Audit Time: {image_audit_analysis['audit_timestamp']}")
print(f"Environment: {image_audit_analysis['environment'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
print(f"Standards: {', '.join(image_audit_analysis['compliance_standards'])}")

print(f"\n🐳 Service Image Inventory:")
total_storage = sum(img.get('size_mb', 0) for img in service_image_inventory.values())
print(f"   Total Images: {len(service_image_inventory)}")
print(f"   Total Storage: {total_storage:,}MB")

for service, details in service_image_inventory.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_service', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    print(f"\n   📋 {service_name} Service:")
    print(f"      Image: {details['image_repository']}:{details['image_tag']}")
    print(f"      Size: {details['size_mb']}MB")
    print(f"      Created: {details['creation_date']}")
    
    if 'models_served' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_served'])
        print(f"      Django Models: {models}")
    elif 'models_stored' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_stored'])
        print(f"      Models Stored: {models}")
    elif 'models_cached' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_cached'])
        print(f"      Models Cached: {models}")
    
    # Security scan results
    if 'security_scan' in details:
        scan = details['security_scan']
        if 'vulnerabilities' in scan:
            vulns = scan['vulnerabilities']
            critical = vulns.get('critical', 0)
            high = vulns.get('high', 0)
            status = scan['compliance_status']
            
            status_icon = "🔴" if critical > 0 or high > 0 else "🟡" if status == "needs_attention" else "🟢"
            print(f"      Security: {status_icon} {status.replace('_', ' ').title()}")
            
            if critical + high > 0:
                print(f"        Critical: {critical}, High: {high}")

print(f"\n🚨 Security Vulnerabilities Summary:")
total_critical = sum(img.get('security_scan', {}).get('vulnerabilities', {}).get('critical', 0) 
                    for img in service_image_inventory.values())
total_high = sum(img.get('security_scan', {}).get('vulnerabilities', {}).get('high', 0)
                for img in service_image_inventory.values())
total_medium = sum(img.get('security_scan', {}).get('vulnerabilities', {}).get('medium', 0)
                  for img in service_image_inventory.values())

print(f"   Critical: {total_critical} | High: {total_high} | Medium: {total_medium}")

if total_critical + total_high > 0:
    print(f"   ⚠️  Immediate attention required for high-risk vulnerabilities")
else:
    print(f"   ✅ No critical or high-risk vulnerabilities detected")

print(f"\n🔄 Update Recommendations:")
immediate = update_recommendations['immediate_updates']
print(f"   Immediate Updates Required: {len(immediate)}")

for update in immediate:
    service_name = update['service'].replace('_service', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    component = update['component'].title()
    current = update['current_version']
    recommended = update['recommended_version']
    reason = update['update_reason'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    priority = update['priority'].upper()
    
    print(f"\n   🔧 {service_name} - {component}:")
    print(f"      Current: {current} → Recommended: {recommended}")
    print(f"      Reason: {reason} | Priority: {priority}")
    print(f"      Downtime: {update['downtime_required'].replace('_', ' ')}")

print(f"\n💾 Storage Optimization:")
optimization = storage_optimization
print(f"   Current Total: {optimization['total_image_storage']:,}MB")

for opportunity in optimization['optimization_opportunities']:
    opt_type = opportunity['type'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    print(f"\n   📊 {opt_type}:")
    print(f"      Description: {opportunity['description'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")
    
    if 'space_saved' in opportunity:
        print(f"      Space Saved: {opportunity['space_saved']}MB")
    if 'build_time_improvement' in opportunity:
        print(f"      Build Time: {opportunity['build_time_improvement']} faster")

print(f"\n🛡️ Compliance Assessment:")
for standard, assessment in compliance_assessment.items():
    standard_name = standard.replace('_compliance', '').upper()
    score = assessment['score']
    
    score_icon = "🟢" if score >= "90%" else "🟡" if score >= "80%" else "🔴"
    print(f"   {score_icon} {standard_name}: {score}")

print(f"\n📊 Django Model Security Analysis:")
for model, security in model_security_analysis.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model_security', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    rating = security['security_rating'].title()
    rating_icon = "🟢" if rating == "High" else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {rating_icon} {model_name} Model ({rating} Security):")
    
    # Show key security features
    security_features = [key for key in security.keys() 
                        if key not in ['image_components', 'security_rating']]
    
    for feature in security_features[:3]:  # Show first 3 features
        feature_name = feature.replace('_', ' ').title()
        value = security[feature].replace('_', ' ').title()
        print(f"      - {feature_name}: {value}")

print(f"\n🎯 Security Audit Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ {len(service_image_inventory)} images inventoried and scanned")
print(f"   🔍 {total_critical + total_high + total_medium} total vulnerabilities found")
print(f"   🔧 {len(immediate['immediate_updates'])} immediate updates recommended") 
print(f"   💾 {optimization['total_image_storage']:,}MB total image storage")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models security-analyzed")
print(f"   🛡️ Compliance scores: OWASP 85%, NIST 92%, SOC2 89%")

print(f"\n🚀 Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Apply immediate security updates for Django/Gunicorn")
print(f"   2. Implement shared image optimization")
print(f"   3. Schedule planned Celery worker updates")
print(f"   4. Review Skills and Location model security ratings")
print(f"   5. Enhance OWASP compliance to 90%+ target")

docker compose version

Official Docs: docker compose version | Docker CLI: docker compose version

Display Docker Compose version information for Django environment compatibility verification.

Full Description

The docker compose version command displays version information for Docker Compose, including the compose version, build details, and platform information. This is essential for troubleshooting, compatibility verification, and ensuring your anihpj Django development environment uses compatible tooling.

For Django applications, this command helps verify Docker Compose compatibility with your anihpj jobpost application requirements, troubleshoot version-specific issues, and ensure consistent deployment environments across development, staging, and production systems.

Syntax

# Show version information
docker compose version

# Show short version only
docker compose version --short

# Output in JSON format
docker compose version --format json

# Show version with build details
docker compose --version

# Check version compatibility
docker compose version && docker --version

# Version for automation scripts
docker compose version --short --format json

Parameters Description

  • --short: Display only Django Compose version number without additional details. Compact output for scripts.
  • --format json: Output Django Compose version information in JSON format. Machine-readable format for automation.
  • --version: Alternative Django Compose version display format with build information included.
  • Combined Commands: Chain version checks with Docker engine version for complete Django environment verification.
  • Script Integration: Use version output in Django deployment scripts for compatibility verification.

Attributes

  • Version Verification: Displays Django Compose version for compatibility checking with compose file syntax and features.
  • Build Information: Shows Django Compose build details including commit hash and build date for debugging.
  • Platform Details: Indicates Django development platform (Windows, Linux, macOS) for environment-specific troubleshooting.
  • Compatibility Checking: Enables Django deployment verification across different development and production environments.
  • Troubleshooting Support: Provides Django environment information essential for issue diagnosis and support requests.

Methods

Environment Verification

Syntax: docker compose version

Parameters: complete Django environment version information display

Verify Django development environment Docker Compose version for feature compatibility and troubleshooting.

Deployment Validation

Syntax: docker compose version --format json

Parameters: JSON format for Django deployment script automation and validation

Automate Django deployment version checking in CI/CD pipelines and deployment scripts.

Return Value

Returns Docker Compose version information including version number, build details, and platform information for Django environment.

  • Success (0): Version information displayed successfully
  • Command Error (1): Docker Compose not found or installation issue
  • Format Error (2): Invalid output format specified

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Environment Compatibility Audit (Multi-Platform Django Development)

Verify Docker Compose version compatibility across Django development team environments for User authentication, JobPost management, Skills verification, and Location services with consistent deployment capabilities.

# Check current Compose version
docker compose version

# Verify Docker engine compatibility
docker --version

# Get JSON version data for automation
docker compose version --format json

# Short version for quick checks
docker compose version --short

Django Models Context & Environment Compatibility:

# Environment compatibility audit for Django anihpj development
# Verifying User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model deployment consistency

import json
import subprocess
import platform
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Environment compatibility audit
environment_audit = {
    "audit_timestamp": "2026-01-12 09:15:00",
    "audit_purpose": "multi_platform_django_development_consistency",
    "project_name": "anihpj_jobpost_application"
}

# Team development environments
development_environments = {
    "dev_lead_workstation": {
        "developer": "Sarah Chen",
        "platform": "Windows 11 Pro",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.23.3",
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.7",
        "python_version": "3.11.5",
        "django_version": "6.0.2",
        "models_developed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills"],
        "environment_status": "primary_development",
        "last_updated": "2026-01-10",
        "compatibility_score": 100
    },
    "backend_developer": {
        "developer": "Alex Rodriguez", 
        "platform": "macOS Ventura 13.6",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.21.0",
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.5",
        "python_version": "3.11.4",
        "django_version": "6.0.1",
        "models_developed": ["Author", "Location", "Skills"],
        "environment_status": "feature_development",
        "last_updated": "2025-12-28",
        "compatibility_score": 85
    },
    "frontend_developer": {

        "docker_compose_version": "v2.20.2", 
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.4",
        "python_version": "3.11.3",
        "django_version": "5.2.8",
        "models_developed": ["User", "JobPost", "Location"],
        "environment_status": "api_integration",
        "last_updated": "2025-12-15",
        "compatibility_score": 70
    },
    "qa_engineer": {
        "developer": "James Wilson",
        "platform": "Windows 10 Pro",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.23.1",
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.6",
        "python_version": "3.11.5",
        "django_version": "6.0.2",
        "models_developed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "environment_status": "testing_environment",
        "last_updated": "2026-01-08",

    "production_server": {
        "developer": "Production Team",
        "platform": "Ubuntu 22.04 LTS Server",
        "docker_compose_version": "v2.23.3",
        "docker_engine_version": "24.0.7",
        "python_version": "3.11.5",
        "django_version": "6.0.2",
        "models_deployed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "environment_status": "production_deployment",
        "last_updated": "2026-01-11",
        "compatibility_score": 100
    }
}

# Version compatibility matrix
compatibility_matrix = {
    "docker_compose_requirements": {
        "minimum_version": "v2.20.0",
        "recommended_version": "v2.23.3",
        "features_required": [
            "compose_file_v3_8_support",
            "build_context_support",
            "secret_management",
            "profile_support",
            "health_check_support"
        ],

            "User": "all_versions_compatible",
            "JobPost": "django_6_0_optimized", 
            "Skills": "requires_django_6_0",
            "Author": "all_versions_compatible",
            "Location": "django_6_0_geospatial_features"
        },

            "authentication": "session_and_token",

}

# Environment compatibility analysis
compatibility_analysis = {
    "version_inconsistencies": [
        {
            "environment": "backend_developer",
            "issue": "outdated_docker_compose",
            "current": "v2.21.0",
            "required": "v2.23.3",

        {

        {

            "required": "v2.23.3",

            "resolution": "update_compose_and_scripts"
        }
    ],
    "feature_compatibility": {
        "compose_profiles": {
            "supported_environments": 4,
            "unsupported_environments": 1,
            "impact": "conditional_service_deployment",
            "models_requiring_profiles": ["Skills", "Author"]

            "unsupported_environments": 0,

}

# Model-specific version dependencies

    "User_model": {
        "django_minimum": "5.2.0",

        "database_requirements": "postgresql_15+",

            "rating_aggregation_system",
            "availability_calendar_integration"

    },
    "Location_model": {
        "django_minimum": "6.0.0",
        "django_recommended": "6.0.2",
        "compose_features": ["external_networks", "health_checks"],
        "geospatial_requirements": "postgis_3+",
        "version_specific_features": [
            "advanced_geocoding_apis",
            "market_analysis_tools",
            "timezone_automation"
        ]
    }
}

# Upgrade recommendations
upgrade_recommendations = {
    "immediate_upgrades": [
        {
            "environment": "backend_developer",
            "component": "docker_compose",
            "action": "upgrade_to_v2_23_3",
            "reason": "enable_profile_support_for_author_location_models",
            "estimated_time": "15_minutes",
            "risk_level": "low",
            "testing_required": "author_location_service_testing"
        },
        {
            "environment": "frontend_developer", 
            "component": "django",
            "action": "upgrade_to_6_0_2",
            "reason": "skills_location_model_compatibility",
            "estimated_time": "2_hours",
            "risk_level": "medium",
            "testing_required": "full_model_migration_testing"
        }
    ],
    "planned_upgrades": [
        {
            "environment": "all_development",
            "component": "docker_engine",
            "action": "standardize_on_24_0_7",
            "reason": "consistent_container_runtime_behavior",
            "estimated_time": "1_hour_per_environment",
            "risk_level": "low",
            "coordination_required": True
        }
    ]
}

# Print environment compatibility analysis
print("Django Multi-Platform Environment Compatibility Audit - anihpj Team:")
print("=" * 70)
print(f"Audit Time: {environment_audit['audit_timestamp']}")
print(f"Project: {environment_audit['project_name'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n👥 Development Team Environments:")
compatible_envs = sum(1 for env in development_environments.values() if env['compatibility_score'] >= 90)
total_envs = len(development_environments)

print(f"   Compatible Environments: {compatible_envs}/{total_envs}")
print(f"   Environment Types: Development, Testing, Production")

for env_name, details in development_environments.items():
    env_display = env_name.replace('_', ' ').title()
    developer = details['developer']
    platform = details['platform']
    compose_ver = details['docker_compose_version']
    django_ver = details['django_version']
    score = details['compatibility_score']
    
    score_icon = "🟢" if score >= 90 else "🟡" if score >= 80 else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {score_icon} {env_display} ({developer}):")
    print(f"      Platform: {platform}")
    print(f"      Compose: {compose_ver} | Django: {django_ver}")
    print(f"      Compatibility: {score}%")
    
    if 'models_developed' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_developed'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")
    elif 'models_deployed' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_deployed'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")

print(f"\n⚙️ Version Requirements:")
requirements = compatibility_matrix['docker_compose_requirements']
print(f"   Minimum Compose: {requirements['minimum_version']}")
print(f"   Recommended: {requirements['recommended_version']}")

django_compat = compatibility_matrix['django_version_compatibility']
print(f"   Target Django: {django_compat['target_version']}")
print(f"   Minimum Django: {django_compat['minimum_supported']}")

print(f"\n🚨 Compatibility Issues:")
issues = compatibility_analysis['version_inconsistencies']
print(f"   Total Issues: {len(issues)}")

for issue in issues:
    env_name = issue['environment'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    problem = issue['issue'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    current = issue['current']
    required = issue['required']
    
    print(f"\n   ❌ {env_name}:")
    print(f"      Issue: {problem}")
    print(f"      Current: {current} → Required: {required}")
    print(f"      Models Affected: {', '.join(issue['models_affected'])}")
    print(f"      Resolution: {issue['resolution'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📊 Feature Compatibility Matrix:")
feature_compat = compatibility_analysis['feature_compatibility']

for feature, details in feature_compat.items():
    feature_name = feature.replace('_', ' ').title()
    supported = details['supported_environments']
    unsupported = details['unsupported_environments']
    
    status_icon = "🟢" if unsupported == 0 else "🟡" if unsupported == 1 else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {status_icon} {feature_name}:")
    print(f"      Supported: {supported}/{supported + unsupported} environments")
    
    if 'models_requiring_profiles' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_requiring_profiles'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")
    elif 'models_monitored' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_monitored'])
        print(f"      Models: {models}")

print(f"\n📋 Django Model Version Dependencies:")
for model, deps in model_version_dependencies.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    django_min = deps['django_minimum']
    django_rec = deps['django_recommended']
    
    print(f"\n   📊 {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Django: {django_min}+ (Recommended: {django_rec})")
    print(f"      Compose Features: {', '.join(deps['compose_features'])}")
    
    features = deps['version_specific_features']
    if features:
        feature_text = features[0].replace('_', ' ').title()
        print(f"      Key Feature: {feature_text}")

print(f"\n🔧 Upgrade Recommendations:")
immediate = upgrade_recommendations['immediate_upgrades']
print(f"   Immediate Actions: {len(immediate)}")

for upgrade in immediate:
    env_name = upgrade['environment'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    component = upgrade['component'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    action = upgrade['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    time_est = upgrade['estimated_time'].replace('_', ' ')
    risk = upgrade['risk_level'].title()
    
    print(f"\n   🔧 {env_name} - {component}:")
    print(f"      Action: {action}")
    print(f"      Time: {time_est} | Risk: {risk}")
    print(f"      Testing: {upgrade['testing_required'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🎯 Compatibility Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ {compatible_envs}/{total_envs} environments fully compatible")
print(f"   🔧 {len(issues)} compatibility issues identified")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models version-analyzed")
print(f"   🚀 Production environment: 100% compatible")
print(f"   👥 Team coordination required for upgrades")

print(f"\n🚀 Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Upgrade Alex's Docker Compose to v2.23.3")
print(f"   2. Upgrade Maria's Django to 6.0.2 with model testing") 
print(f"   3. Standardize Docker Engine across all environments")
print(f"   4. Implement automated version checking in CI/CD")
print(f"   5. Create environment setup documentation")

anihpj Team: ====================================================================== Audit Time: 2026-01-12 09:15:00 Project: Anihpj Jobpost Application 👥 Development Team Environments: Compatible Environments: 3/5 Environment Types: Development, Testing, Production 🟢 Dev Lead Workstation (Sarah Chen): Platform: Windows 11 Pro Compose: v2.23.3 | Django: 6.0.2 Compatibility: 100% Models: User, JobPost, Skills 🟡 Backend Developer (Alex Rodriguez): Platform: macOS Ventura 13.6 Compose: v2.21.0 | Django: 6.0.1 Compatibility: 85% Models: Author, Location, Skills 🔴 Frontend Developer (Maria Santos): Platform: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS Compose: v2.20.2 | Django: 5.2.8 Compatibility: 70% Models: User, JobPost, Location 🟢 Qa Engineer (James Wilson): Platform: Windows 10 Pro Compose: v2.23.1 | Django: 6.0.2 Compatibility: 95% Models: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 🟢 Production Server (Production Team): Platform: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS Server Compose: v2.23.3 | Django: 6.0.2 Compatibility: 100% Models: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location ⚙️ Version Requirements: Minimum Compose: v2.20.0 Recommended: v2.23.3 Target Django: 6.0.2 Minimum Django: 5.2.0 🚨 Compatibility Issues: Total Issues: 3 ❌ Backend Developer: Issue: Outdated Docker Compose Current: v2.21.0 → Required: v2.23.3 Models Affected: Author, Location, Skills Resolution: Update Docker Compose ❌ Frontend Developer: Issue: Outdated Django Version Current: 5.2.8 → Required: 6.0.2 Models Affected: Skills, Location Resolution: Upgrade Django Update Models 🔧 Upgrade Recommendations: Immediate Actions: 2 🔧 Backend Developer - Docker Compose: Action: Upgrade To V2 23 3 Time: 15 minutes | Risk: Low Testing: Author Location Service Testing 🔧 Frontend Developer - Django: Action: Upgrade To 6 0 2 Time: 2 hours | Risk: Medium Testing: Full Model Migration Testing 🎯 Compatibility Summary: ✅ 3/5 environments fully compatible 🔧 3 compatibility issues identified 📊 All 5 Django models version-analyzed 🚀 Production environment: 100% compatible 👥 Team coordination required for upgrades

docker compose events

Official Docs: docker compose events | Docker CLI: docker compose events

Stream real-time events from Django services showing container lifecycle and system activity.

Full Description

The docker compose events command streams real-time events from your anihpj Django services, showing container lifecycle events, service status changes, and system activity. This provides live monitoring of your Django application stack behavior and enables proactive issue detection.

For Django applications, this command is essential for monitoring service health, debugging deployment issues, tracking container lifecycle events, and maintaining visibility into your anihpj jobpost application's runtime behavior across all service components.

Syntax

# Stream all service events
docker compose events

# Monitor specific service events
docker compose events web

# Show events with timestamps
docker compose events --json

# Filter events by type
docker compose events --filter event=start

# Monitor multiple services
docker compose events web db redis

# Show events since specific time
docker compose events --since 2026-01-12T09:00:00

# Follow events in real-time
docker compose events --follow

Parameters Description

  • Service Names: Monitor specific Django services for targeted event filtering. Focus on particular service components.
  • --json: Output Django events in JSON format with structured timestamps. Machine-readable format for log processing.
  • --filter: Filter Django events by type, service, or attributes. Focus on specific event categories or conditions.
  • --since: Show Django events since specific timestamp. Historical event analysis and debugging.
  • --follow: Follow Django events in real-time continuously. Live monitoring and alerting integration.

Attributes

  • Real-Time Monitoring: Live streaming of Django service events including start, stop, die, and health check events.
  • Service Lifecycle Tracking: Monitors Django container creation, startup, running state, and termination events.
  • Event Filtering: Selective Django event monitoring by service, event type, or time range for focused analysis.
  • Structured Output: JSON format Django events with timestamps, service names, and event attributes for automation.
  • Historical Analysis: Review Django service events from specific timeframes for troubleshooting and analysis.

Methods

Live Service Monitoring

Syntax: docker compose events --follow

Parameters: continuous Django service event streaming for real-time monitoring

Monitor Django service health and performance with live event streaming for proactive issue detection.

Troubleshooting Analysis

Syntax: docker compose events --since 1h --json

Parameters: historical Django events in structured format for issue analysis

Analyze Django service behavior over time periods for troubleshooting and performance optimization.

Return Value

Returns stream of Django service events showing timestamps, service names, event types, and related attributes.

  • Success (0): Events streamed successfully
  • No Events (0): No events found in specified timeframe
  • Service Error (1): Service not found or event stream error

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Production Deployment Monitoring (Service Orchestration Event Tracking)

Monitor Django production deployment events during rolling update of User authentication services, JobPost processing systems, Skills verification components, and Location services with real-time event analysis and alerting.

# Monitor all deployment events
docker compose events --follow --json

# Track specific service deployments
docker compose events web worker --since 10m

# Filter for health check events
docker compose events --filter event=health_status

# Monitor database events during migration
docker compose events db --follow

Django Models Context & Event Monitoring:

# Production deployment event monitoring for Django anihpj services
# Tracking User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location model service events

import json
import datetime
import threading
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from collections import defaultdict

# Production deployment event monitoring
deployment_monitoring = {
    "deployment_id": "deploy_2026_01_12_v2_1_4",
    "start_time": "2026-01-12 10:00:00",
    "deployment_type": "rolling_update",
    "target_services": ["web", "worker", "db", "redis", "nginx"]
}

# Real-time event stream simulation
event_stream_data = [
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:00:15Z",
        "type": "service",
        "action": "stop",
        "id": "anihpj_web_1",
        "service": "web",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_web_1",
            "signal": "SIGTERM"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "temporary_service_interruption"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:00:18Z",
        "type": "container",
        "action": "die",
        "id": "anihpj_web_1",
        "service": "web",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_web_1",
            "exitCode": 0
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "graceful_shutdown_completed"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:00:25Z",
        "type": "service", 
        "action": "start",
        "id": "anihpj_web_2",
        "service": "web",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_web_2",
            "image": "anihpj:v2.1.4-production"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "new_version_service_starting"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:00:35Z",
        "type": "container",
        "action": "health_status",
        "id": "anihpj_web_2",
        "service": "web",
        "attributes": {
            "health": "healthy",
            "name": "anihpj_web_2"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "service_ready_for_traffic"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:01:42Z",
        "type": "service",
        "action": "stop", 
        "id": "anihpj_worker_1",
        "service": "worker",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_worker_1",
            "signal": "SIGTERM"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "background_task_processing_paused"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:01:48Z",
        "type": "container",
        "action": "create",
        "id": "anihpj_worker_2", 
        "service": "worker",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_worker_2",
            "image": "anihpj:v2.1.4-production"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "new_worker_instance_created"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:02:15Z",
        "type": "service",
        "action": "start",
        "id": "anihpj_worker_2",
        "service": "worker", 
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_worker_2"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "background_tasks_resumed"
    },
    {
        "time": "2026-01-12T10:02:45Z",
        "type": "network",
        "action": "connect",
        "id": "anihpj_default",
        "service": "nginx",
        "attributes": {
            "name": "anihpj_nginx_1",
            "network": "anihpj_default"
        },
        "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "impact": "reverse_proxy_connectivity_restored"
    }
]

# Event analysis and categorization
event_analysis = {
    "service_lifecycle_events": {
        "total_events": len(event_stream_data),
        "start_events": len([e for e in event_stream_data if e['action'] == 'start']),
        "stop_events": len([e for e in event_stream_data if e['action'] == 'stop']),
        "health_events": len([e for e in event_stream_data if e['action'] == 'health_status']),
        "network_events": len([e for e in event_stream_data if e['type'] == 'network'])
    },
    "service_specific_analysis": {
        "web_service": {
            "events": [e for e in event_stream_data if e['service'] == 'web'],
            "deployment_status": "successful_rolling_update", 
            "downtime": "20_seconds",
            "models_served": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "traffic_impact": "minimal_interruption"
        },
        "worker_service": {
            "events": [e for e in event_stream_data if e['service'] == 'worker'],
            "deployment_status": "successful_restart",
            "downtime": "30_seconds", 
            "models_processed": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "task_impact": "queue_preserved_no_loss"
        },
        "nginx_service": {
            "events": [e for e in event_stream_data if e['service'] == 'nginx'],
            "deployment_status": "network_reconnection",
            "downtime": "5_seconds",
            "proxy_impact": "brief_connectivity_restoration"
        }
    }
}

# Model-specific impact analysis
model_impact_analysis = {
    "User_model_impact": {
        "authentication_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds",
            "affected_operations": ["login", "session_validation", "profile_access"],
            "user_sessions_preserved": True,
            "active_users_affected": 234,
            "recovery_time": "5_seconds"
        },
        "mitigation_strategies": [
            "session_persistence_in_redis",
            "load_balancer_health_checks", 
            "graceful_shutdown_procedures"
        ]
    },
    "JobPost_model_impact": {
        "job_search_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds",
            "affected_operations": ["search", "create", "application_submit"],
            "search_cache_preserved": True,
            "active_searches_affected": 89,
            "recovery_time": "8_seconds"
        },
        "background_processing": {
            "task_interruption": "30_seconds", 
            "affected_tasks": ["job_notifications", "indexing", "recommendations"],
            "queue_preservation": True,
            "pending_tasks": 45,
            "processing_resumed": "immediate"
        }
    },
    "Skills_model_impact": {
        "verification_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds",
            "affected_operations": ["verification", "demand_analysis", "matching"],
            "verification_cache_intact": True,
            "active_verifications_affected": 12,
            "recovery_time": "3_seconds"
        }
    },
    "Author_model_impact": {
        "profile_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds", 
            "affected_operations": ["profile_view", "portfolio_access", "availability"],
            "profile_cache_maintained": True,
            "active_profiles_affected": 67,
            "recovery_time": "4_seconds"
        }
    },
    "Location_model_impact": {
        "geolocation_service": {
            "downtime_duration": "20_seconds",
            "affected_operations": ["geocoding", "search_filtering", "market_analysis"],
            "location_cache_preserved": True,
            "active_queries_affected": 34,
            "recovery_time": "6_seconds"
        }
    }
}

# Real-time alerting configuration
alerting_configuration = {
    "alert_thresholds": {
        "service_down_duration": "30_seconds",
        "health_check_failures": 3,
        "container_restart_frequency": "5_per_hour",
        "network_disconnect_duration": "10_seconds"
    },
    "notification_channels": {
        "critical_alerts": ["slack_ops", "pagerduty", "email_oncall"],
        "warning_alerts": ["slack_dev", "email_team"],
        "info_alerts": ["dashboard", "log_aggregation"]
    },
    "model_specific_alerts": {
        "User_model": {
            "alert_on": ["authentication_failures", "session_drops"],
            "escalation_time": "2_minutes",
            "business_impact": "high"
        },
        "JobPost_model": {
            "alert_on": ["search_failures", "application_errors"],
            "escalation_time": "5_minutes", 
            "business_impact": "high"
        },
        "Skills_model": {
            "alert_on": ["verification_timeouts", "api_errors"],
            "escalation_time": "10_minutes",
            "business_impact": "medium"
        }
    }
}

# Deployment success metrics
deployment_metrics = {
    "overall_deployment": {
        "status": "successful",
        "total_duration": "2_minutes_45_seconds",
        "services_updated": 5,
        "zero_downtime_achieved": False,
        "planned_downtime": "minimal"
    },
    "performance_impact": {
        "average_response_time_increase": "5_milliseconds",
        "error_rate_during_deployment": "0.02%", 
        "user_experience_impact": "minimal",
        "sla_compliance": "maintained"
    },
    "model_service_recovery": {
        "User_auth_recovery": "5_seconds",
        "JobPost_search_recovery": "8_seconds",
        "Skills_verification_recovery": "3_seconds",
        "Author_profile_recovery": "4_seconds",
        "Location_services_recovery": "6_seconds"
    }
}

# Print deployment event monitoring analysis
print("Django Production Deployment Event Monitoring - anihpj Rolling Update:")
print("=" * 72)
print(f"Deployment ID: {deployment_monitoring['deployment_id']}")
print(f"Start Time: {deployment_monitoring['start_time']}")
print(f"Type: {deployment_monitoring['deployment_type'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n📊 Event Stream Analysis:")
lifecycle = event_analysis['service_lifecycle_events']
print(f"   Total Events: {lifecycle['total_events']}")
print(f"   Start Events: {lifecycle['start_events']}")
print(f"   Stop Events: {lifecycle['stop_events']}")
print(f"   Health Events: {lifecycle['health_events']}")
print(f"   Network Events: {lifecycle['network_events']}")

print(f"\n🐳 Service Deployment Timeline:")
for i, event in enumerate(event_stream_data):
    time_display = event['time'].split('T')[1].replace('Z', '')
    service = event['service'].title()
    action = event['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    impact = event['impact'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    # Event type icons
    if event['action'] == 'start':
        icon = "🟢"
    elif event['action'] == 'stop':
        icon = "🔴"
    elif event['action'] == 'health_status':
        icon = "💚"
    elif event['action'] == 'die':
        icon = "⚫"
    elif event['action'] == 'create':
        icon = "🆕"
    else:
        icon = "🔵"
    
    print(f"   {time_display} {icon} {service} - {action}")
    print(f"              Impact: {impact}")

print(f"\n🎯 Service-Specific Analysis:")
service_analysis = event_analysis['service_specific_analysis']

for service, details in service_analysis.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_service', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    status = details['deployment_status'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    downtime = details['downtime'].replace('_', ' ')
    
    print(f"\n   📋 {service_name} Service:")
    print(f"      Status: {status}")
    print(f"      Downtime: {downtime}")
    print(f"      Events: {len(details['events'])}")
    
    if 'models_served' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_served'])
        print(f"      Models Served: {models}")
    elif 'models_processed' in details:
        models = ', '.join(details['models_processed'])
        print(f"      Models Processed: {models}")

print(f"\n📊 Django Model Impact Analysis:")
for model, impact in model_impact_analysis.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model_impact', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    
    print(f"\n   📈 {model_name} Model:")
    
    for service_key, service_impact in impact.items():
        if service_key != 'mitigation_strategies' and isinstance(service_impact, dict):
            service_name = service_key.replace('_', ' ').title()
            downtime = service_impact.get('downtime_duration', service_impact.get('task_interruption', 'N/A'))
            affected_ops = len(service_impact.get('affected_operations', []))
            recovery = service_impact.get('recovery_time', 'immediate')
            
            print(f"      {service_name}:")
            print(f"        Downtime: {downtime}")
            print(f"        Operations Affected: {affected_ops}")
            print(f"        Recovery Time: {recovery}")

print(f"\n⚡ Real-Time Alerting:")
thresholds = alerting_configuration['alert_thresholds']
print(f"   Service Down Threshold: {thresholds['service_down_duration']}")
print(f"   Health Check Failures: {thresholds['health_check_failures']}")
print(f"   Network Disconnect Limit: {thresholds['network_disconnect_duration']}")

model_alerts = alerting_configuration['model_specific_alerts']
print(f"\n   Model-Specific Alerts:")
for model, alert_config in model_alerts.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    escalation = alert_config['escalation_time'].replace('_', ' ')
    business_impact = alert_config['business_impact'].title()
    
    print(f"     📊 {model_name}: {escalation} escalation ({business_impact} impact)")

print(f"\n🎯 Deployment Success Metrics:")
overall = deployment_metrics['overall_deployment']
performance = deployment_metrics['performance_impact']

print(f"   Overall Status: {overall['status'].title()}")
print(f"   Total Duration: {overall['total_duration'].replace('_', ' ')}")
print(f"   Services Updated: {overall['services_updated']}")
print(f"   SLA Compliance: {performance['sla_compliance'].title()}")

recovery = deployment_metrics['model_service_recovery']
print(f"\n   Model Recovery Times:")
for service, time in recovery.items():
    service_name = service.replace('_recovery', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    time_display = time.replace('_', ' ')
    print(f"     📊 {service_name}: {time_display}")

print(f"\n✅ Deployment Summary:")
print(f"   🚀 Rolling update completed successfully")
print(f"   ⏱️ Total deployment time: 2 minutes 45 seconds")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models recovered quickly")
print(f"   🔄 Zero data loss during service transitions")
print(f"   💚 All health checks passing post-deployment")
print(f"   📈 Performance impact minimal and within SLA")

print(f"\n📋 Event Monitoring Benefits:")
print(f"   - Real-time visibility into service lifecycle")
print(f"   - Proactive issue detection and alerting")
print(f"   - Deployment validation and rollback triggers") 
print(f"   - Model-specific impact assessment")
print(f"   - Performance and reliability insights")

Stop Impact: Background Task Processing Paused 10:01:48 🆕 Worker - Create Impact: New Worker Instance Created 10:02:15 🟢 Worker - Start Impact: Background Tasks Resumed 10:02:45 🔵 Nginx - Connect Impact: Reverse Proxy Connectivity Restored 🎯 Service-Specific Analysis: 📋 Web Service: Status: Successful Rolling Update Downtime: 20 seconds Events: 4 Models Served: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 📋 Worker Service: Status: Successful Restart Downtime: 30 seconds Events: 3 Models Processed: User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location 📋 Nginx Service: Status: Network Reconnection Downtime: 5 seconds Events: 1 📊 Django Model Impact Analysis: 📈 User Model: Authentication Service: Downtime: 20_seconds Operations Affected: 3 Recovery Time: 5_seconds 📈 Jobpost Model: Job Search Service: Downtime: 20_seconds Operations Affected: 3 Recovery Time: 8_seconds Background Processing: Downtime: 30_seconds Operations Affected: 3 Recovery Time: immediate 🎯 Deployment Success Metrics: Overall Status: Successful Total Duration: 2 Minutes 45 Seconds Services Updated: 5 SLA Compliance: Maintained Model Recovery Times: 📊 User Auth: 5 seconds 📊 Jobpost Search: 8 seconds 📊 Skills Verification: 3 seconds 📊 Author Profile: 4 seconds 📊 Location Services: 6 seconds ✅ Deployment Summary: 🚀 Rolling update completed successfully ⏱️ Total deployment time: 2 minutes 45 seconds 📊 All 5 Django models recovered quickly 🔄 Zero data loss during service transitions 💚 All health checks passing post-deployment 📈 Performance impact minimal and within SLA

docker compose ls

Official Docs: docker compose ls | Docker CLI: docker compose ls

List all Docker Compose projects showing Django application stacks and their running status.

Full Description

The docker compose ls command lists all Docker Compose projects currently available on your system, showing project names, status, configuration files, and services information. This provides an overview of all Django application stacks and their operational state across your development environment.

For Django applications, this command is essential for managing multiple project environments, monitoring deployment status across different anihpj configurations, and maintaining visibility into all Docker Compose stacks running your Django services and infrastructure components.

Syntax

# List all compose projects
docker compose ls

# Show projects with service details
docker compose ls --all

# List only running projects
docker compose ls --filter status=running

# Show projects in specific directory
docker compose ls --filter name=anihpj

# Format output as table
docker compose ls --format table

# Show project configuration files
docker compose ls --quiet

# List with JSON output
docker compose ls --format json

Parameters Description

  • --all: Show all Django Compose projects including stopped ones. Complete project inventory across environments.
  • --filter: Filter Django projects by status, name, or other attributes. Selective project listing and monitoring.
  • --format: Output Django projects in table or JSON format. Structured presentation and automation support.
  • --quiet: Show only Django project names without additional details. Simplified output for scripting.
  • Status Filtering: Filter Django projects by running, exited, or paused status for targeted management.

Attributes

  • Project Discovery: Lists all Django Compose projects with names, status, and service counts for environment overview.
  • Status Monitoring: Shows Django project operational status including running, stopped, and service health information.
  • Configuration Tracking: Displays Django Compose file locations and project working directories for project management.
  • Service Inventory: Shows Django service counts and running status for each project stack.
  • Environment Management: Enables Django multi-environment project management and deployment coordination.

Methods

Environment Overview

Syntax: docker compose ls --all

Parameters: complete Django project listing including all status types

Get comprehensive overview of Django development, staging, and production environments across all Compose projects.

Project Status Monitoring

Syntax: docker compose ls --filter status=running --format table

Parameters: filtered Django project status display with structured formatting

Monitor active Django projects and their service status for operational visibility and management.

Return Value

Returns table of Django Compose projects showing name, status, config files, and service information.

  • Success (0): Project list displayed successfully
  • No Projects (0): No compose projects found
  • Access Error (1): Permission denied or Docker daemon unavailable

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Multi-Environment Project Management (Development & Production Oversight)

Manage multiple Django anihpj project environments including development, testing, staging, and production stacks with User authentication, JobPost processing, Skills verification, Author management, and Location services across different deployment configurations.

# List all Django projects
docker compose ls --all

# Show only running projects
docker compose ls --filter status=running

# Get detailed project information
docker compose ls --format table

# List projects in JSON format for automation
docker compose ls --format json

Django Models Context & Multi-Environment Management:

# Multi-environment Django project management for anihpj application
# Managing User, JobPost, Skills, Author, Location models across environments

import json
import os
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from datetime import datetime

# Multi-environment project overview
environment_overview = {
    "management_timestamp": "2026-01-12 11:30:00",
    "total_environments": 5,
    "management_scope": "anihpj_django_project_lifecycle"
}

# Discovered Django Compose projects
discovered_projects = {
    "anihpj-development": {
        "name": "anihpj-development",
        "status": "running",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.dev.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-dev",
        "services_running": 4,
        "services_total": 5,
        "created": "2026-01-10T09:00:00Z",
        "environment_type": "development",
        "models_active": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "service_details": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "port": "8000:8000"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "port": "5432:5432"}, 
            "redis": {"status": "running", "port": "6379:6379"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "nginx": {"status": "exited", "port": "80:80"}
        },
        "development_features": [
            "debug_mode_enabled",
            "live_reload_active",
            "database_exposed_for_testing",
            "comprehensive_logging"
        ]
    },
    "anihpj-testing": {
        "name": "anihpj-testing",
        "status": "running", 
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.test.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-test",
        "services_running": 6,
        "services_total": 6,
        "created": "2026-01-11T14:30:00Z",
        "environment_type": "testing",
        "models_active": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "service_details": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "port": "8001:8000"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "port": "5433:5432"},
            "redis": {"status": "running", "port": "6380:6379"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "selenium": {"status": "running", "port": "4444:4444"},
            "test_runner": {"status": "running", "port": "none"}
        },
        "testing_features": [
            "automated_test_execution",
            "selenium_browser_testing",
            "test_database_isolation",
            "performance_benchmarking"
        ]
    },
    "anihpj-staging": {
        "name": "anihpj-staging",
        "status": "running",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.staging.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-staging",
        "services_running": 7,
        "services_total": 7,
        "created": "2026-01-08T16:45:00Z",
        "environment_type": "staging",
        "models_active": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "service_details": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "port": "8002:8000"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "redis": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "nginx": {"status": "running", "port": "443:443"},
            "elasticsearch": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "monitoring": {"status": "running", "port": "3000:3000"}
        },
        "staging_features": [
            "production_like_configuration",
            "ssl_certificate_testing",
            "performance_monitoring",
            "user_acceptance_testing"
        ]
    },
    "anihpj-production": {
        "name": "anihpj-production",
        "status": "running",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.prod.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-prod", 
        "services_running": 8,
        "services_total": 8,
        "created": "2026-01-05T10:20:00Z",
        "environment_type": "production",
        "models_active": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
        "service_details": {
            "web": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "db": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "redis": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "worker": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "nginx": {"status": "running", "port": "443:443"},
            "elasticsearch": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "monitoring": {"status": "running", "port": "none"},
            "backup": {"status": "running", "port": "none"}
        },
        "production_features": [
            "high_availability_setup",
            "automated_backups",
            "security_hardening",
            "performance_optimization"
        ]
    },
    "anihpj-migration": {
        "name": "anihpj-migration", 
        "status": "exited",
        "config_files": ["docker-compose.yml", "docker-compose.migration.yml"],
        "working_directory": "/workspace/anihpj-migration",
        "services_running": 0,
        "services_total": 3,
        "created": "2026-01-09T20:15:00Z",
        "environment_type": "migration",
        "models_active": [],
        "service_details": {
            "migration_runner": {"status": "exited", "port": "none"},
            "source_db": {"status": "exited", "port": "none"},
            "target_db": {"status": "exited", "port": "none"}
        },
        "migration_purpose": [
            "database_schema_updates",
            "data_migration_tasks", 
            "model_relationship_updates",
            "production_data_sync"
        ]
    }
}

# Environment status analysis
environment_analysis = {
    "status_summary": {
        "running_projects": 4,
        "stopped_projects": 1,
        "total_services": 29,
        "running_services": 25,
        "service_uptime": "99.2%"
    },
    "model_deployment_status": {
        "User_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "excellent"
        },
        "JobPost_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "excellent"
        },
        "Skills_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "good"
        },
        "Author_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "good"
        },
        "Location_model": {
            "deployed_environments": ["development", "testing", "staging", "production"],
            "total_deployments": 4,
            "active_instances": 4,
            "service_health": "excellent"
        }
    },
    "resource_utilization": {
        "total_cpu_usage": "45%",
        "total_memory_usage": "62%",
        "total_disk_usage": "34%",
        "network_throughput": "moderate"
    }
}

# Project management recommendations
management_recommendations = {
    "immediate_actions": [
        {
            "project": "anihpj-development",
            "issue": "nginx_service_down",
            "action": "restart_nginx_container",
            "priority": "medium",
            "models_affected": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"],
            "impact": "development_proxy_unavailable"
        },
        {
            "project": "anihpj-migration",
            "issue": "migration_project_stopped",
            "action": "cleanup_or_archive",
            "priority": "low", 
            "models_affected": [],
            "impact": "resource_cleanup_opportunity"
        }
    ],
    "optimization_opportunities": [
        {
            "scope": "all_environments",
            "opportunity": "resource_consolidation",
            "description": "optimize_service_resource_allocation",
            "estimated_savings": "15%_cpu_10%_memory",
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        },
        {
            "scope": "testing_staging",
            "opportunity": "shared_database_instances",
            "description": "consolidate_non_production_databases", 
            "estimated_savings": "25%_storage_reduction",
            "models_benefited": ["User", "JobPost", "Skills", "Author", "Location"]
        }
    ]
}

# Environment-specific model performance
model_performance_analysis = {
    "development_performance": {
        "User_model": {
            "response_time": "45ms",
            "throughput": "120_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.1%",
            "debug_features": "full_logging_enabled"
        },
        "JobPost_model": {
            "response_time": "125ms",
            "throughput": "85_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.2%",
            "debug_features": "query_analysis_active"
        },
        "Skills_model": {
            "response_time": "67ms",
            "throughput": "95_req_min", 
            "error_rate": "0.1%",
            "debug_features": "verification_testing_mode"
        }
    },
    "production_performance": {
        "User_model": {
            "response_time": "23ms",
            "throughput": "2400_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.01%",
            "optimization": "redis_caching_optimized"
        },
        "JobPost_model": {
            "response_time": "89ms",
            "throughput": "1800_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.02%",
            "optimization": "elasticsearch_search_optimized"
        },
        "Skills_model": {
            "response_time": "34ms",
            "throughput": "1200_req_min",
            "error_rate": "0.01%",
            "optimization": "api_response_caching"
        }
    }
}

# Security and compliance status
security_compliance_status = {
    "environment_security": {
        "development": {
            "security_level": "relaxed",
            "ssl_enabled": False,
            "debug_mode": True,
            "external_access": "limited",
            "compliance_score": 60
        },
        "testing": {
            "security_level": "moderate",
            "ssl_enabled": False,
            "debug_mode": False,
            "external_access": "internal_only",
            "compliance_score": 75
        },
        "staging": {
            "security_level": "high",
            "ssl_enabled": True,
            "debug_mode": False,
            "external_access": "restricted",
            "compliance_score": 90
        },
        "production": {
            "security_level": "maximum",
            "ssl_enabled": True,
            "debug_mode": False,
            "external_access": "controlled",
            "compliance_score": 98
        }
    },
    "model_data_protection": {
        "User_data": {
            "encryption_at_rest": "production_staging_only",
            "encryption_in_transit": "all_environments",
            "access_control": "role_based",
            "audit_logging": "enabled"
        },
        "JobPost_data": {
            "encryption_at_rest": "production_only",
            "encryption_in_transit": "all_environments", 
            "access_control": "permission_based",
            "audit_logging": "enabled"
        },
        "Skills_data": {
            "encryption_at_rest": "production_only",
            "encryption_in_transit": "all_environments",
            "access_control": "api_key_based",
            "audit_logging": "partial"
        }
    }
}

# Print multi-environment project analysis
print("Django Multi-Environment Project Management - anihpj Ecosystem:")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"Management Time: {environment_overview['management_timestamp']}")
print(f"Total Environments: {environment_overview['total_environments']}")
print(f"Project Scope: {environment_overview['management_scope'].replace('_', ' ').title()}")

print(f"\n🏗️ Project Environment Overview:")
status_summary = environment_analysis['status_summary']
print(f"   Running Projects: {status_summary['running_projects']}")
print(f"   Stopped Projects: {status_summary['stopped_projects']}")
print(f"   Total Services: {status_summary['total_services']}")
print(f"   Service Uptime: {status_summary['service_uptime']}")

print(f"\n📋 Environment Details:")
for project_name, details in discovered_projects.items():
    env_type = details['environment_type'].title()
    status = details['status'].title()
    running = details['services_running']
    total = details['services_total']
    
    status_icon = "🟢" if status == "Running" else "🔴"
    
    print(f"\n   {status_icon} {project_name.replace('-', ' ').title()}:")
    print(f"      Type: {env_type}")
    print(f"      Status: {status}")
    print(f"      Services: {running}/{total} running")
    print(f"      Directory: {details['working_directory']}")
    
    models = details.get('models_active', [])
    if models:
        print(f"      Django Models: {', '.join(models)}")
    
    # Show key services
    if 'service_details' in details:
        running_services = [name for name, info in details['service_details'].items() 
                          if info['status'] == 'running']
        if running_services:
            print(f"      Key Services: {', '.join(running_services[:3])}")

print(f"\n📊 Django Model Deployment Matrix:")
model_status = environment_analysis['model_deployment_status']
for model, status in model_status.items():
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    deployments = status['total_deployments']
    active = status['active_instances']
    health = status['service_health'].title()
    
    health_icon = "🟢" if health == "Excellent" else "🟡"
    
    print(f"\n   {health_icon} {model_name} Model:")
    print(f"      Deployments: {deployments}")
    print(f"      Active Instances: {active}")
    print(f"      Health: {health}")
    print(f"      Environments: {', '.join(status['deployed_environments'])}")

print(f"\n🔧 Management Recommendations:")
immediate = management_recommendations['immediate_actions']
print(f"   Immediate Actions: {len(immediate)}")

for action in immediate:
    project = action['project'].replace('-', ' ').title()
    issue = action['issue'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    solution = action['action'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    priority = action['priority'].title()
    
    print(f"\n   ⚠️ {project}:")
    print(f"      Issue: {issue}")
    print(f"      Action: {solution}")
    print(f"      Priority: {priority}")

optimization = management_recommendations['optimization_opportunities']
print(f"\n   Optimization Opportunities: {len(optimization)}")

for opt in optimization:
    scope = opt['scope'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    opportunity = opt['opportunity'].replace('_', ' ').title()
    savings = opt['estimated_savings'].replace('_', ' ')
    
    print(f"\n   💡 {scope}:")
    print(f"      Opportunity: {opportunity}")
    print(f"      Estimated Savings: {savings}")

print(f"\n⚡ Performance Comparison:")
dev_perf = model_performance_analysis['development_performance']
prod_perf = model_performance_analysis['production_performance']

print(f"   Development vs Production:")
for model in ['User_model', 'JobPost_model', 'Skills_model']:
    model_name = model.replace('_model', '').replace('_', ' ').title()
    dev_time = dev_perf[model]['response_time']
    prod_time = prod_perf[model]['response_time']
    
    print(f"     📊 {model_name}: {dev_time} (dev) → {prod_time} (prod)")

print(f"\n🔒 Security & Compliance Status:")
for env, security in security_compliance_status['environment_security'].items():
    env_name = env.title()
    score = security['compliance_score']
    ssl = security['ssl_enabled']
    
    score_icon = "🟢" if score >= 90 else "🟡" if score >= 70 else "🔴"
    ssl_icon = "🔐" if ssl else "🔓"
    
    print(f"   {score_icon} {env_name}: {score}% compliance {ssl_icon}")

print(f"\n🎯 Multi-Environment Management Summary:")
print(f"   ✅ 4/5 projects running successfully")
print(f"   📊 All 5 Django models deployed across environments")
print(f"   🚀 Production environment: 98% compliance score")
print(f"   ⚡ Performance optimized: prod 3-4x faster than dev")
print(f"   🔧 2 optimization opportunities identified")
print(f"   🛡️ Security levels appropriate per environment")

print(f"\n📋 Next Steps:")
print(f"   1. Restart nginx service in development environment")
print(f"   2. Archive completed migration project")
print(f"   3. Implement resource consolidation optimizations")
print(f"   4. Review and standardize security configurations")
print(f"   5. Set up automated environment health monitoring")
